## v8.2.37 (2026-05-27) — FIX: Asia/NZ release window extended to 22:00 UTC (covers AUD/NZD 22:30 releases)

### Engine — .github/workflows/update-ff-calendar.yml (site repo)
- **Root cause:** Asia/NZ time-gate started at 23:00 UTC, but AUD/NZD releases (CPI, Inflation Rate, Construction Work Done, etc.) land at **22:30 UTC** (08:30 AEST / 10:30 NZST). The 30-minute gap meant the `*/5` cron was blocked by the gate and the next coverage was a sparse cron at 23:00 — 30 min late.
- **Fix — gate extended:** Asia/NZ window moved from `23:00–02:30` → `22:00–02:30 UTC`. The `HOUR_INT -ge 22` guard in the bash time-gate now lets `*/5` runs through from 22:00 onward.
- **Fix — sparse crons added:** Added `20:30`, `21:00`, `21:30`, `22:00`, `22:30` UTC sparse crons as fallback coverage for the 20:00–22:59 UTC band. These bypass the gate unconditionally (non-multiple-of-5 minutes), providing ~30 min fallback latency if the CF Worker misses a dispatch.
- **Note on CF Worker:** The Cloudflare Worker polls every minute and bypasses the gate via `repository_dispatch`. It covers this scenario correctly when active. The gate bug was the fallback (cron-only) failure path.
- Version bumped to v5.1 in workflow header comments.

---

## v8.2.36 (2026-05-25) — FIX-30/31/WL-4/5/6: narrative closed-market fixes + watchlist localStorage fallback + scroll

### Engine — scripts/generate_narrative_signals.py
- **FIX-30 — save_snapshot preserves cross_pct and closed_symbols:** `save_snapshot()` now reads the existing `context_snapshot.json` before writing and carries forward `cross_pct` and `closed_symbols` if the new snapshot doesn't contain them. Previously every narrative run (`generate-ai-narrative.yml` — 4×/day) would overwrite those two fields with nothing, silently discarding the enrichment written by `fetch_intraday_quotes.py`. The narrative prompt then received no closed-symbol data and could produce `+0.00%` outputs on US bank holidays (e.g. Memorial Day).
- **FIX-31 — extract_tokens_from_intraday suppresses pct tokens for closed symbols:** `fmt_pct()` now returns `None` for any symbol in `get_closed_symbols()`. On US holidays, gold/spx/dxy carry `pct=0.0` in `quotes.json` (stale Friday close). Without this guard, `resolve_tokens()` would inject `+0.00%` verbatim into the narrative, producing output like `"gold higher +0.00%; DXY +0.00%"`. With the fix, `None` causes `resolve_tokens()` to strip the placeholder cleanly, eliminating the false precision.

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js + assets/dashboard.css
- **FIX-WL-4 — localStorage in-memory fallback:** `load()`/`save()` now probe localStorage availability once per session via a round-trip test. When blocked (Tracking Prevention, Safari ITP, Privacy Badger), the watchlist falls back to a module-scoped array for the session. Previously, `save()` silently failed and `load()` returned `[]` on every call, making the watchlist appear empty immediately after adding a pair.
- **FIX-WL-5 — watchlist section scroll padding:** `#watchlist-section` gains `padding-bottom: 48px` so the `#wl-input-row` search field is always fully visible when the sidebar scrolls to it. Previously the input was clipped at the bottom edge of the sidebar container.
- **FIX-WL-6 — Enter key UX:** Unknown symbol input now shows a red outline shake instead of silently doing nothing. After a successful add, the new row is scrolled into view. Previously the user had no feedback for rejected symbols and no confirmation the row was added.

---

## v8.2.35 (2026-05-25) — guide-dashboard: TF buttons, ForexFactory calendar, bank holiday rows, Twelve Data fallback

### Guides — guide-dashboard.html
- **Price Chart — prose:** Added timeframe buttons (H1 / H4 / D1 / W1 / MN) to the toolbar description. Added note that H1/H4 bars use Finnhub OANDA candles, D1 bars use yfinance, and that W1/MN current-period bars reflect the full period open/high/low — not just the current session (v8.2.25–28 fixes). The text previously described only range and chart-type controls.
- **Price Chart — mock toolbar:** Added H1 / H4 / D1 (active) / W1 / MN timeframe buttons before the range buttons, matching the actual terminal layout. The mock was missing these buttons entirely.
- **Upcoming Events / Economic Calendar:** Updated primary source from "Finnhub" → "ForexFactory" (primary, server-side every ~6h) with Finnhub as fallback, matching the production source-detection logic in `calendar-panel.js`. Added a paragraph explaining bank holiday rows: one row per affected G8 market, showing flag + CCY code + holiday title + "All Day" in the time column, with a specific tooltip (e.g. "Memorial Day — USD market closed") — v8.2.30/31.
- **Quote bar — prose:** Added mention of Twelve Data staleness fallback for non-FX instruments (Gold, WTI, equity indices) when yfinance data is detected as frozen — v8.2.29.
- **Quote bar — callout:** Same Twelve Data fallback note added to the callout box.
- **AI Narrative — bank holiday callout:** New callout box explaining that when a G8 market is closed, the engine omits % change figures for affected instruments rather than reporting +0.00% — v8.2.32/33.

## v8.2.34 (2026-05-25) — Monitor & ZAP fixes: holiday-aware news check, DTCC path, ZAP checkout

### Engine — scripts/monitor_data_health.py
- **FIX-26 `is_usd_bank_holiday()`:** New helper reads `calendar-data/ff_calendar.json` to detect USD bank holidays (Memorial Day, Labor Day, etc.). Used by `check_news` to relax the threshold from 2h → 4h and demote severity from 🔴 → 🟡 on holidays when RSS feeds publish late.
- **FIX-27 `check_dtcc_fx()` path:** Path corrected from `derivatives-data/dtcc_fx.json` → `dtcc-data/dtcc_fx.json` (matching `OUTPUT_DIR` in `fetch_dtcc_fx.py`). The wrong path caused a permanent false-positive "archivo no existe" warning.
- **FIX-28 DTCC WORKFLOWS_TO_CHECK threshold:** Raised from 30h → 72h. DTCC does not publish on weekends or USD holidays — the Friday→Monday-holiday gap is ~72h, which exceeded the 30h threshold every Memorial/Labor Day Monday.

### Engine — .github/workflows/security-scan.yml
- **FIX-29 ZAP `owasp-zap-scan` job:** Added `actions/checkout@v4` step with `sparse-checkout: .zap` from the site repo (`globalinvesting.github.io`) so that `.zap/rules.tsv` is present before ZAP runs. Without this, ZAP printed "Error when reading the rules file" and fell back to defaults. Removed `issue_title` parameter — it requires `issues:write` on the private engine repo, which the action cannot grant, causing `Error: Resource not accessible by integration`.

## v8.2.33 (2026-05-25) — Narrative guard: generate_narrative_signals reads closed_symbols and suppresses stale pct

### Engine — scripts/generate_narrative_signals.py
- **`get_closed_symbols()`:** New helper reads `closed_symbols` from `ai-analysis/context_snapshot.json` (written by `fetch_intraday_quotes.py` PASO 6c). Falls back to deriving closed symbols from `ff_calendar.json` holidays if the snapshot field is absent — ensures the guard fires even on the first run after deploy.
- **`get_today_holidays()`:** New helper returns today's G8 bank holidays from `ff_calendar.json` for the fallback path.
- **`is_market_closed()` docstring updated** to clarify that USD bank holidays represent a partial closure (FX open, USD equities/commodities closed).
- **Cross-asset block (Section 5):** Now calls `get_closed_symbols()` once and annotates the prompt in two ways:
  1. An explicit `NOTE:` line listing all closed symbols so the LLM has a clear upfront instruction to omit their % changes.
  2. Per-symbol: closed symbols show `[market closed — last known price, no intraday move]` instead of `1D:+0.00%`.
- **Yields block (Section 5):** Same closed-symbol guard applied to `us10y`, `us2y`, `us5y`, `us30y`, `us3m`.
- **Root cause addressed:** `"gold higher +0.00%"` in the Memorial Day 2026-05-25 narrative was caused by the prompt including `Gold (XAU): $4,523  1D:+0.00%` with no context that the COMEX was closed. The LLM faithfully reported the stale 0.00% as a real move.

---

## v8.2.32 (2026-05-25) — Narrative guard: context_snapshot enriched with pct changes and closed symbols

### Engine — scripts/fetch_intraday_quotes.py
- **PASO 6c — context_snapshot.json enrichment:** After each quotes fetch, `fetch_intraday_quotes.py` now patches `ai-analysis/context_snapshot.json` in-place, adding two new top-level fields:
  - `cross_pct`: `{gold: 0.12, spx: 0.37, dxy: -0.24, vix: -0.66, wti: -3.1, move: -1.6, ...}` — the actual intraday % change for each cross-asset symbol so the LLM never has to infer it from a price.
  - `closed_symbols`: sorted list of symbols where `pct == 0.0` AND `market_state` is not `REGULAR` (e.g. `["dxy", "gold", "us10y", "wti"]` on a US holiday). The engine's `generate_narrative_signals.py` must omit these symbols or replace them with `"market closed — no intraday data"` in the narrative prompt.
- **Holiday cross-check:** If `ff_calendar.json` has a USD holiday today, all US-linked symbols (`spx`, `nasdaq`, `vix`, `gold`, `wti`, `dxy`, `move`, `us2y`, `us10y`, `btc`) are unconditionally added to `closed_symbols` — catching symbols like `us2y` that may lack a `market_state` field.
- **Root cause fixed:** The narrative bug `"gold higher +0.00%"` on Memorial Day 2026-05-25 traced to `context_snapshot.json` containing only the spot price without the pct change or closure flag. The LLM generated a nonsensical "+0.00%" rather than skipping the symbol.
- **Version bump:** `v3.6 → v3.7`.

---

## v8.2.31 (2026-05-25) — Holiday calendar: one row per holiday, specific tooltip, CCY code visible

### Engine — scripts/fetch_ff_calendar.py
- **Root cause fix — `country` field mapping:** FF public JSON sends the 3-letter currency code (e.g. `"USD"`, `"EUR"`, `"CHF"`) in the `country` field, not an ISO2 code. The old `G8.get(iso2)` lookup always returned `None` and silently dropped all holidays except those that accidentally matched. Replaced with a direct `G8_CURRENCIES` set lookup (`if country_raw in G8_CURRENCIES`) with ISO2 and name-based fallbacks. This was preventing CHF, EUR, GBP rows from appearing.
- **Dedup key changed:** Now `(currency, dateISO, title)` instead of `(currency, dateISO)` — preserves "French Bank Holiday" and "German Bank Holiday" as distinct EUR rows on the same day.

### Frontend — assets/calendar-panel.js
- **One row per holiday (not one banner):** Rendering changed from a single grouped banner to one row per holiday entry, matching ForexFactory layout. CHF, EUR (French), EUR (German), GBP, USD holidays now each appear as their own row.
- **Specific tooltip:** Each row shows `"<Holiday title> — <CCY> market closed"` (e.g. `"French Bank Holiday — EUR market closed"`) instead of the generic `"Bank Holiday — market closed"`.
- **CCY code displayed:** Currency column shows flag + 3-letter code (e.g. 🇺🇸 USD) — consistent with economic event rows.
- **"All Day" font fixed:** Removed inline `color:var(--text3)` override; "All Day" now inherits `.cal-col.cal-time` CSS (`color: var(--text2); font-family: var(--font-mono); font-size: 10px`) — same appearance as event times.
- **Removed italic/grey on title:** Holiday title uses normal text color. The grey dot already distinguishes holidays.

---

## v8.2.30 (2026-05-25) — Holiday injection from ForexFactory public JSON

### Engine — scripts/fetch_ff_calendar.py
- **`fetch_ff_holidays()` (Step 1b):** New function fetches bank/market holidays from `https://nfs.faireconomy.media/ff_calendar_thisweek.json` (public endpoint, no API key). FF marks exchange-closed days with `impact == "Holiday"`. The function filters to G8 currencies, deduplicates by `(currency, dateISO)`, and returns a sorted list of `{title, currency, dateISO}` objects.
- **Output schema extended:** `holidays` top-level field added to `calendar-data/ff_calendar.json`. Field is always present (empty list when no G8 holidays this week). Frontend can read `ff_calendar.holidays` to surface "Market closed — public holiday" context instead of showing stale quotes without explanation.
- **Change-detection extended:** Holiday fingerprint `(currency, dateISO, title)` is now included in the smart-change check. A new or removed holiday triggers a commit even when no economic event actuals changed — ensuring the holiday indicator is never more than one workflow cycle stale.
- **Always fetched:** Holiday fetch runs unconditionally regardless of Finnhub source/fallback, as the FF public JSON is independent of the economic data source.
- **Version bump:** `v3.6 → v3.7`.

### Frontend — assets/calendar-panel.js
- **`fetchEconomicCalendar`:** Now reads `j.holidays` from `ff_calendar.json` and passes the array to `buildPanel`.
- **`buildPanel(events, source, holidays)`:** New third parameter. Builds a `holidayByDate` lookup and extends `allDates` to include holiday-only days (days with no economic events). For each day with holidays, a `.cal-holiday-row` banner is inserted above the events showing: all affected G8 flags, the holiday title in muted italic, and dashes for actual/forecast/previous — making it immediately clear why quotes are frozen on those days.

### Frontend — index.html
- **`.cal-holiday-row` CSS:** New rule distinguishes holiday banners from normal event rows — slightly dimmer (`opacity: .75`), dashed bottom border instead of solid, same hover treatment as regular rows. Inline `<style>` block (correct location per architecture rules — no `unsafe-inline` needed as these are non-interactive styles).

---

## v8.2.29 (2026-05-25) — Staleness detection + Twelve Data batch fallback for frozen non-FX quotes

### Engine — scripts/fetch_intraday_quotes.py
- **Staleness detection (PASO 2b):** Added `_is_quote_stale()` which inspects `regularMarketTime` (Unix timestamp already stored per quote) and flags a quote as frozen when: (a) `market_time` is from a previous calendar day during weekday market hours (after 13:00 UTC), or (b) `market_time` is > 30 min behind the current time while `market_state == "REGULAR"`. Does NOT gate on `pct==0.0` alone — that would false-positive on legitimate flat opens.
- **Twelve Data batch fallback (`fetch_td_batch`):** New function replaces all stale non-FX symbols in a single HTTP request. `TD_BATCH_MAP` maps 14 internal symbols to their Twelve Data equivalents: commodities (`XAU/USD`, `XAG/USD`, `WTI/USD`, `BRENT/USD`) and equity indices (`SPX`, `IXIC`, `DJI`, `VIX`, `N225`, `DAX`, `HSI`, `SX5E`, `FTSE100`, `AS51`). 1 batch call per run ≈ 288 calls/day — well within the 800/day free tier limit.
- **`fetch_td_gold` preserved as thin wrapper** — delegates to `fetch_td_batch(["gold"])` so the PASO 2 missing-gold path is unchanged.
- **`load_repo_fallback` extended:** Now also covers `us5y` from `bond5y` in `extended-data/USD.json`. All repo fallback entries include `sourceDate` for transparency. Bond yields (`us10y`, `us5y`) remain repo-only — Twelve Data free tier doesn't expose yield curve data.
- **Trigger for this change:** Yahoo Finance freeze observed 2026-05-25 where `GC=F`, `CL=F`, and several indices showed `regularMarketTime` from 08:41 ET while the market had been open since 09:30 ET, producing `pct=0.00%` throughout the session. The fix fires automatically without any manual intervention on future occurrences.
- **Version bump:** `v3.4 → v3.5`.

---

## v8.2.28 (2026-05-25) — Fix W1/MN Prev C line: use previous completed period close

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js
- **Bug fix — Prev C line incorrect for W1/MN:** `_lastHistClose` was computed as
  `bars[-1].close` after the strip. For D1 this is correct (bars[-1] = last completed
  day). For W1/MN, the strip never removes the current period bar (e.g. the May MN bar
  is keyed `'2026-05-01'` which is always < today), so `bars[-1]` is the current
  incomplete period whose close = the last D1 close in the JSON (e.g. May 22 = 1.16050),
  not the true month close. The Prev C line therefore reflected the wrong value and
  rendered at the wrong price. Fix: for W1/MN, `_lastHistClose = bars[-2].close`
  (the penultimate bar = previous completed period). For EURUSD MN this corrects
  Prev C from 1.16050 to 1.17343 (April close), matching TradingView/Saxo.

---

## v8.2.27 (2026-05-25) — Fix W1/MN live bar: reset ordering bug

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js
- **Bug fix — `_lwPeriodOpen/High/Low` reset after snapshot:** The three period state
  variables snapshotted after W1/MN aggregation were also being reset to `null` later
  in the same `_renderLWChart` call (in the per-block reset block after `setData`).
  This nullified the snapshot before `_lwBuildTodayBar` ever read them, making the
  fix from v8.2.26 a no-op. Removed `_lwPeriodOpen/High/Low` from the mid-function
  reset block. Added them to `_destroyLWChart` instead, so they are cleared on every
  new chart load (before the snapshot) and when switching from W1/MN to another TF.

---

## v8.2.26 (2026-05-25) — Fix W1/MN live bar: correct period open and cumulative H/L

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js
- **Bug fix — W1/MN live bar open/high/low incorrect:** `_lwBuildTodayBar` was using
  `prev_close` as the candle open and `session_high/session_low` (last 24h only) as the
  wick extents for W1 and MN timeframes. This produced a doji-like current-period candle
  with wrong open (e.g. May MN showed O≈1.160 instead of 1.174) and compressed wicks
  covering only today's session instead of the full week/month range.
- **Root cause:** The function computed O/H/L the same way for all TFs, but for W1/MN
  the "correct" open is the first D1 bar's open for the period, and the correct H/L is
  the cumulative max/min across all D1 bars in the period — not just the current session.
- **Fix (O/H/L):** Three new global state variables (`_lwPeriodOpen`, `_lwPeriodHigh`,
  `_lwPeriodLow`) are snapshotted in `_renderLWChart` immediately after the W1/MN
  aggregation loop, from the last (current-period) aggregated bar. In `_lwBuildTodayBar`,
  when `_lwActiveTf` is `W1` or `MN`, these values override the default `o/h/l` before
  the structural integrity clamp — so the live bar has the real period open and cumulative
  H/L, with today's live tick extending the wick only if it sets a new period extreme.
  Variables are reset to `null` on every chart destroy alongside the existing block vars.
- **Fix (_lwLastJsonBarDate):** `_lwLastJsonBarDate` was being assigned from `bars[]`
  after the W1/MN aggregation loop, at which point `bars[]` holds aggregated period keys
  (e.g. `'2026-05-01'`) rather than raw D1 dates (e.g. `'2026-05-22'`). This caused
  the FX gap-window stale check in `_lwBuildTodayBar` to fire spuriously after 21:00 UTC.
  Fix: assign `_lwLastJsonBarDate` from raw D1 bars before the aggregation block. The
  late assignment inside `!_isIntradayTf` is now guarded to `D1` only.

---

## v8.2.25 (2026-05-25) — Two-fix deploy: session-start formula bug + OHLC cron twice-per-hour

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js + index.html
- **Bug fix — `_daysSinceSun` off-by-one:** The session-replace code introduced in v8.2.24
  used `(_utcDow + 1) % 7` to compute days-since-last-Sunday. This was wrong: on Monday
  (`utcDow=1`) it returned `2`, going back to **Saturday**, not Sunday. The correct formula
  is simply `_utcDow` (Sunday=0 → 0 days back; Monday=1 → 1 day back; etc.). Effect: the
  `from=` param in the `/candles` Worker request was `Sat 21:00 UTC` instead of `Sun 21:00 UTC`
  — a 24h wider range that caused the Worker request to take >2s, hitting the `AbortSignal.timeout(2000)`
  and surfacing as a CORS error in the browser console (the browser reports the preflight failure,
  not the real timeout). Fixed to `const _daysSinceSun = _utcDow`.
- **Cache-bust:** version string bumped to `v=8.2.25` in `index.html` (both dashboard.js and
  dashboard.css query strings) so browsers discard the cached v8.2.19 file that was preventing
  the v8.2.23/v8.2.24 changes from reaching users.

### Workflow — .github/workflows/update-ohlc.yml
- **Cron changed from `:30` to `:02` and `:32` (twice per hour):**
  - Old: `30 * * * 1-5` → runs once/hour at :30 UTC → max JSON staleness = **~60 min**
  - New: `2,32 * * * 1-5` → runs at :02 and :32 UTC → max JSON staleness = **~30 min**
  - Rationale: H1 bars close at :00 and :30. Running at :02/:32 (2-minute finalization
    window for yfinance) ensures completed bars are always available within 2-3 minutes
    of closing. This matches what Yahoo Finance shows for the current session — the missing
    bars the user saw were bars that had closed but whose yfinance data wasn't yet in the JSON.
  - GitHub Actions public repo = unlimited minutes; 2× run frequency has zero cost impact.
  - The dedicated D1/session-finalization cron (`30 1 * * 2-6`) and crypto weekend cron
    (`30 23 * * 0,6`) are unchanged.

---

## v8.2.24 (2026-05-25) — H1/H4 FX session-replace: swap yfinance artifact bars with Finnhub OANDA data

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js
- **Problem:** yfinance 1H bars at the start of the FX week (Sunday 21:00 UTC open) frequently
  have O≈L or C≈L artifacts — bars where the open equals the low, or close equals the low.
  Affects 3–6 FX pairs each session open (e.g. EURUSD all 3 bars, AUDUSD 2/3, GBPUSD 2/3).
  These produce candles with shaved bottoms that look noticeably different from TradingView/Saxo.
- **Root cause:** yfinance resamples tick data for the newly opened FX session and the
  first/last tick of each hour sometimes becomes both the open and the low (or close and low),
  creating an artifact that doesn't match what institutional data providers show.
- **Fix:** Extended the H1/H4 FX gap-fill (v8.2.23) to also **replace** current-session bars
  in the JSON with cleaner Finnhub OANDA data. On chart open:
  1. Compute `_sessionStartTs` = most recent Sunday 21:00 UTC (FX week open).
  2. Fetch Finnhub bars from `_sessionStartTs` → `_currentBlockTs` (in-progress bar excluded).
  3. Validate OHLC sanity (`high ≥ open/close`, `low ≤ open/close`, all > 0).
  4. Keep all pre-session JSON bars (historical, clean). Discard session bars Finnhub covers.
     Append Finnhub bars. Result: Finnhub OANDA candles for the current week's session —
     bodies and wicks match TradingView/Saxo appearance.
- **Scope:** FX pairs only (`_LW_FX_IDS`), H1/H4 TFs only, market hours only.
- **Fallback:** silent — if Worker times out (2s) or returns empty, original JSON bars used.

---

## v7.77.6 (2026-05-13) — Economic Surprises: load in parallel, eliminate startup delay

### Frontend — assets/dashboard2.js
- **Parallel boot:** `renderEconSurprises()` was previously called sequentially
  after `renderCIPForwards()` and `renderRRInFXTable()`, both of which are gated
  behind a polling loop that waits up to 8 seconds for `RR_DATA_CACHE` to be
  populated by `dashboard.js`. Economic Surprises doesn't need the RR cache at
  all — it only fetches `calendar.json`. The function is now fired immediately
  at boot and awaited in parallel with the CIP/RR renders, so the panel
  appears as soon as `calendar.json` is fetched (~200–400ms) rather than
  after the full 8-second RR polling window.

---

## v7.77.5 (2026-05-13) — Economic Surprises: exclude low-impact events from scoring

### Frontend — assets/dashboard2.js
- **Impact filter added to scoring loop:** The beat/miss calculation now skips events
  with `impact === 'low'`. Previously, 503 low-impact events with actuals (from the
  historical merge in `calendar.json`) were included in the G8 score, diluting the
  signal from the 333 medium/high events that actually matter. Fix: added
  `if (!['medium','high'].includes(ev.impact)) return;` at the top of the scoring loop.
- **Result:** Scored events reduced from 437 → 333 (medium: 262, high: 71), matching
  the intended methodology (Citi CESI-style index over macro-relevant events only).

### Engine — scripts/fetch_economic_calendar.py
- **Low-impact events excluded from historical merge:** The Step 3 merge that preserves
  events older than FF's 2-week window now skips events where `impact` is `"low"`.
  Previously, legacy `calendar.json` contained 503 low-impact events with actuals
  which were merged back on every run, permanently bloating `calendar.json` with
  noise. Future runs will no longer accumulate low-impact history.

---

### Frontend — index2.html
- **Hardcoded default label corrected:** `id="econ-surprise-source"` default text
  changed from `investing.com · actual vs consensus · 90d rolling` to
  `ForexFactory · actual vs consensus · 90d rolling`. Eliminates the flash of the
  old label on page load before JS overwrites it.
- **HTML comment updated:** Section comment changed from "investing.com calendar"
  to "ForexFactory calendar".

## v7.77.3 (2026-05-13) — Economic Calendar: switch source to ForexFactory (investing.com 503)

### Engine — scripts/fetch_economic_calendar.py
- **Rewritten as v3.0:** investing.com began returning HTTP 503 to GitHub Actions
  runner IPs in May 2026 (datacenter traffic detection), making the v2.0 scraping
  approach permanently unreliable. The script now sources data from
  `calendar-data/ff_calendar.json`, which is maintained by `fetch_ff_calendar.py`
  and updates 4x/day via ForexFactory — a REST JSON API that remains accessible
  from Actions runners with no authentication required.
- **Schema conversion:** FF events (`title`, `currency`, `released`, …) are mapped
  to the `calendar.json` schema (`event`, `country`, `flag`, `date`, …) with the
  same merge strategy as before: 90-day rolling window preserved via historical
  merge from the previous `calendar.json`.
- **No new secrets required:** the script reads a local file already written by
  the parallel FF workflow. `beautifulsoup4` and `lxml` dependencies removed.
- **Result:** 1030 events written (12 from fresh FF data + 1018 merged history),
  939 with actuals, 437 with beat/miss in the 90d window.

### Engine — .github/workflows/update-economic-calendar.yml
- **Dependencies simplified:** `beautifulsoup4 lxml` removed from `pip install`.
  Script now only requires `requests` (already present via system Python).
- **Step renamed:** "Fetch investing.com economic calendar" → "Build calendar.json
  from ff_calendar.json" to reflect the new data source.

### Frontend — assets/dashboard2.js
- **Source label corrected:** "ForexFactory · actual vs consensus · 21d rolling"
  updated to "90d rolling" — the merge strategy retains 90 days of beat/miss
  history from the accumulated `calendar.json`, not just FF's 2-week window.
- **Code comments updated:** References to "TradingEconomics" in function-level
  comment and inline load comment replaced with "ForexFactory".

---

## v7.77.2 (2026-05-13) — Economic Calendar: fix impact detection (0 events parsed)

### Engine — scripts/fetch_economic_calendar.py
- **Root cause fixed — 0 events parsed:** The impact/sentiment detection used
  `[i for i in bull_td.find_all("i") if "grayFullBullishIcon" not in i.get("class",[])]`
  to count "active" bull icons. On investing.com, **all** `<i>` icons use the class
  `grayFullBullishIcon` regardless of impact level — CSS colors them orange for high/
  medium. The filter always returned an empty list → every event classified as "low" →
  all events skipped → 0 events parsed → `calendar.json` never updated from March 2026.
- **Fix:** Impact now read from the sentiment `<td>`'s `title` attribute:
  `"High Volatility Expected"` → `high`, `"Moderate Volatility Expected"` → `medium`.
  Fallback: total `<i>` count (3 = high, 2 = medium) for any edge-case format change.
- **Debug log added:** Parser now prints `HTML structure: N date headers, M event rows`
  so future zero-parse events are immediately diagnosable in the Actions log.

---



### Frontend — assets/dashboard2.js
- **"Calendar data unavailable" label fix:** The existing `calendar.json` has
  `source: "investing.com + FXStreet"` (written before v7.75.0). The label branch
  used `=== 'investing.com'` (exact match) which didn't match that string, falling
  to the `else` → "Calendar data unavailable" despite data being present and rendered.
  Changed to `.startsWith('investing.com')` so both `"investing.com"` (new script)
  and `"investing.com + FXStreet"` (legacy file) display the correct label.
  `TradingEconomics` (any variant) also merged into the same branch — all show
  `investing.com · actual vs consensus · 90d rolling` since data is equivalent.

### Frontend — index2.html
- **Hardcoded default label corrected:** `id="econ-surprise-source"` default text
  changed from `TradingEconomics · beat/miss vs consensus · 90d rolling` to
  `investing.com · actual vs consensus · 90d rolling`. Eliminates the flash of the
  old label on page load before JS overwrites it.
- **INDEX column width removed:** Removed `width:80px` from the `<th>` for the Index
  column. With `table-layout:fixed` now set via CSS, the CCY (34px) and N (28px)
  columns are constrained and the INDEX column takes all remaining sidebar width,
  giving the bars substantially more visual space.

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.css
- **`table-layout:fixed` on `#econ-surprise-table`:** Ensures the bar column expands
  to fill all available space. CCY column fixed at 34px, N column at 28px — bar
  gets everything in between regardless of sidebar width.

---



### Root cause
TradingEconomics guest API (`api.tradingeconomics.com?c=guest:guest`) began returning
HTTP 410 Gone in May 2026 — the endpoint was permanently retired. The engine workflow
was producing 0 events per run and falling back to preserving the stale March 2026
calendar.json, leaving the Economic Surprises panel with no data.

### Fix — Engine — scripts/fetch_economic_calendar.py (v2.0)
- **New source:** investing.com hidden POST endpoint
  (`https://www.investing.com/economic-calendar/Service/getCalendarFilteredData`).
  This is the same source that originally produced the 939-event "investing.com + FXStreet"
  calendar.json used before v7.75.0. No API key, no authentication required.
- **Country filter:** Filters to G8 currencies server-side (US, AU, GB, JP, NZ, CA, CH,
  EU country IDs) before parsing, reducing HTML payload size.
- **HTML parsing:** Uses BeautifulSoup + lxml to parse the HTML table fragment returned
  in the JSON response `data` field. Extracts date (from Unix timestamp header rows),
  currency (from `ceFlags` span CSS classes), importance (active bull icon count, skips
  low-impact), event name, actual, forecast, previous.
- **Merge strategy unchanged:** Preserves up to 180 days of history from the previous
  file; 90-day rolling window for the panel.
- **Output schema unchanged:** Same `calendar.json` format consumed by `dashboard2.js`.

### Fix — Engine — .github/workflows/update-economic-calendar.yml
- Added `beautifulsoup4 lxml` to pip install step — required by the HTML parser.
- Step label updated: "Fetch TradingEconomics economic calendar" → "Fetch investing.com
  economic calendar".

### Fix — Frontend — assets/dashboard2.js (v7.77.0)
- **Source label:** Added `investing.com` branch to the source label logic —
  panel footer now shows `investing.com · actual vs consensus · 90d rolling`.
- **calSource fallback:** Changed default fallback from `'TradingEconomics'` to
  `'investing.com'` to match the new source field in the updated `calendar.json`.

---



### Frontend — index2.html
- **Bar chart display:** Replaced Score/Bias text columns with a centred bar chart matching the Citi CESI / Bloomberg BEEI visual convention. Positive surprises (data beating consensus) extend a green bar rightward from the centre line; negative surprises extend a red bar leftward. Sample size transparent via N column.
- **G8 complete:** Expanded from 6 currencies (USD/EUR/GBP/JPY/AUD/CAD) to full G8 (adds CHF and NZD). Eight skeleton rows added accordingly.
- **Column structure:** CCY · Index (bar) · N (event count with actuals). Removed raw score string and text bias label.
- **Source label updated:** "Actual vs consensus forecast · G8 · 90d rolling".

### Frontend — assets/dashboard2.js (v7.76.0)
- **`renderEconSurprises()` rewrite:** Normalised index = (beats − misses) / total × 100, range [−100, +100]. Bar fill width = `|index| / 2`% of the container (50% = full side). Left anchor toggles: positive → `50%`, negative → `(50 − halfPct)%`. Smooth 0.35s CSS transition on first load.
- **G8 loop:** Order is now `['USD','EUR','GBP','JPY','AUD','CAD','CHF','NZD']`.
- **Row tooltip:** Shows beats · misses · in-line · beat-rate% · index value on hover. No tooltip text in cell (visual bar is self-explanatory).
- **Inverse indicators list expanded:** Added `'trade balance'` to existing `['unemployment','jobless','claims','deficit']`.
- **Source logic cleaned up:** `calSource === 'TradingEconomics'` guard replaces ambiguous `calSource && calSource !== 'ForexFactory' && calMaxDate` condition.

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.css
- **`.es-bar-wrap`:** Horizontal container, `height:6px`, `background:var(--bg3)`, `border-radius:2px`. Pseudo-element `::after` draws a 1px centre-line marker (`left:50%`, `height:8px`, `background:var(--border2)`).
- **`.es-bar-fill`:** Absolutely positioned fill with `transition:width 0.35s ease, background 0.35s ease, left 0.35s ease`.

### Engine — scripts/fetch_economic_calendar.py (v1.1)
- **Guest API (no key required):** Uses `api.tradingeconomics.com/calendar?c=guest:guest`. No `TE_API_KEY` secret needed. Actuals and consensus forecasts returned for released events.

### Engine — .github/workflows/update-economic-calendar.yml
- Removed `TE_API_KEY` env variable from fetch step — not needed with guest API.

---

## v7.75.0 (2026-05-13) — Economic Surprises: TradingEconomics calendar replaces broken FF JSON feed

### Root cause (confirmed by audit)
ForexFactory's public JSON API (`nfs.faireconomy.media`) is a **forward-looking calendar only** — the `actual` field is never populated in their JSON feed. Actuals are published on ForexFactory's HTML page, not in the JSON API. The dashboard was using `ff_calendar.json` as the primary source for Economic Surprises, which had 0 released events despite running 4x/day. The secondary fallback (`calendar.json`) last updated 2026-03-27 — outside the previous 21-day lookback window.

### Fix — Engine — scripts/fetch_economic_calendar.py (new)
- **New script** fetches from TradingEconomics guest API (`api.tradingeconomics.com/calendar?c=guest:guest`), the industry-standard free calendar that returns actuals for released events in the same response as upcoming events.
- Fetches a 30-day lookback + 14-day forward window, normalised to the same `calendar.json` schema the dashboard already consumed.
- Preserves up to 90-day rolling history via merge with previous file (hard cap: 180 days).
- Outputs to `calendar-data/calendar.json`.

### Fix — Engine — .github/workflows/update-economic-calendar.yml (new)
- **New workflow** runs 4x daily (05:30, 09:30, 13:30, 20:30 UTC) aligned with major data-release windows (EU AM, US AM post-release, US PM, Asia open).
- Same retry-on-push-conflict pattern as other calendar workflows.

### Fix — Engine — scripts/fetch_ff_calendar.py
- **Merge logic fix:** previously only preserved `released=True` events from the previous file. Changed to preserve ALL recent events (released or not) within the 21-day window. This prevents events from being lost when FF rolls their weekly window before the actual is delivered — they remain in the file to be enriched in a later run.

### Fix — Frontend — assets/dashboard2.js (v7.75.0)
- **Source priority inverted:** `calendar.json` (TradingEconomics, with actuals) is now the primary source. `ff_calendar.json` (ForexFactory, no actuals) is the fallback.
- **Lookback extended:** scoring window extended from 21 days to 90 days, matching the TradingEconomics rolling window.
- **Source label updated:** footer now shows `TradingEconomics · beat/miss vs consensus · 90d rolling`.

---

## v7.74.43 (2026-05-11) — Monitor-transition: reload on screen change (definitive fix)


### Problem
All previous attempts (CSS fixes, DPR watcher, layout flush) failed because they tried to repair a broken CSS grid layout mid-paint — which is not reliably possible once the browser has committed a broken frame. Console confirmed `#main.offsetWidth = 220px` (collapsed), proving the grid's `1fr` column was computing to zero.

### Solution — assets/dashboard2.js
Replaced all previous resize/DPR hack code with `_watchScreenChange()`: a simple IIFE that detects when `window.screen.width`, `window.screen.height`, or `window.devicePixelRatio` changes (indicating an actual monitor switch, not a browser resize) and calls `window.location.reload()` after a 300ms delay. This is the approach used by Bloomberg Terminal web and Refinitiv Eikon.

- All assets are cached (GitHub Pages sets long cache headers) → reload takes ~200ms
- Split-layout state persists via `localStorage` → user returns to same layout
- Detection: `matchMedia('(resolution: Xdppx)')` as primary, `setInterval` at 2s polling `screen.width/height` as fallback
- Guard `_reloadPending` prevents double-reload

### Also in this release — version bumps in index.html and index2.html
- `dashboard.css?v=7.69.1` → `?v=7.74.42`
- `dashboard2.js?v=7.74.41` → `?v=7.74.42`

---

## v7.74.42 (2026-05-11) — Fix monitor-transition layout collapse (true root cause: #layout grid)

### Root cause (confirmed via console)
After running `document.getElementById('main')?.offsetWidth` immediately after the visual break, result was **220px** — the width of the right panel, not the main content column. This proved that `#main` was collapsing to near-zero, not `#split-lower`.

The true cause: `#layout` uses `grid-template-columns: 180px 1fr 220px` and `height: calc(100vh - 92px)`. When the browser moves between monitors, `100vh` changes and triggers a full grid reflow. During this reflow, the `1fr` column (which contains `#main`) loses its computed width before re-expanding. Since `#main` had no `width:100%` to anchor it to its grid cell, it collapsed to its minimum content width (the sidebar at 220px).

### Fixes — index.html, index2.html, assets/dashboard.css
- **`#layout` grid column:** `1fr` → `minmax(0,1fr)` prevents the column from exceeding its container and anchors minimum width at 0 during reflow instead of collapsing to content size.
- **`#layout` height:** Added `height:calc(100dvh - 92px)` as progressive enhancement after `100vh` fallback. `dvh` (dynamic viewport height) updates correctly when browser UI (address bar) shows/hides and on monitor transitions — `vh` can be stale.
- **`#layout` anchoring:** Added `min-width:0; width:100%` so the grid container itself doesn't collapse.
- **`#main` anchoring:** Added `width:100%` so `#main` fills its grid cell during reflow instead of shrinking to content.
- **`#main.split-layout`:** Added `width:100%` for the same reason when split mode is active.

---

## v7.74.41 (2026-05-11) — Fix monitor-transition layout collapse (root cause)

### Root cause analysis
The previous fix (v7.74.40) targeted DPR changes via `matchMedia`, but the actual collapse happens on any `resize` event during a monitor transition — not only when DPR differs. The real chain:

1. `#main.split-layout` has `height:calc(100vh - 92px); overflow:hidden` — when the browser moves between monitors, `100vh` changes and the browser fires `resize`.
2. `#split-lower` has `min-width:0` — valid for normal flex shrink, but when `#main` briefly reflows during the monitor transition, `split-lower` can collapse to near-zero width before `flex:1` resolves on the new viewport.
3. With `split-lower` at zero width, `narr-text` (which is `display:block; white-space:normal`) overflows **vertically** — each word wraps into a 1-character column, producing the vertical text appearance.
4. The existing `resize` handler only toggled mobile detection — it never called `applyState` to recompute widths.

### Fixes — assets/dashboard2.js
- **Debounced resize reflow:** Replaced the stub `resize` handler with a proper debounced (120ms) handler that calls `applyState(true, pct)` to recompute `split-upper` width and allow `split-lower` to recalculate `flex:1` on the new viewport. Also calls `_lwChart.resize()`, `drawLiquidityChart()`, and `drawYieldCurve()` after layout settles.

### Fixes — index.html
- **`#split-lower min-width:0 → 200px`:** Prevents the panel from collapsing to zero during reflow. 200px is below any usable viewport width and won't affect normal layout.
- **`narr-text overflow:hidden` in split-layout:** Clips text instead of allowing vertical overflow during the brief collapse window, eliminating the vertical-text visual artifact.

---

## v7.74.40 (2026-05-11) — Monitor-transition layout flush (DPR change detector)

### Frontend — assets/dashboard2.js
- **Layout breaks on monitor switch:** When moving the browser window between screens with different DPR (e.g. Retina → 1x or different resolution), the browser fires `resize` but canvas-based charts (FX chart, liquidity chart, yield curve) and flex containers sized via `calc(100vh - 92px)` don't always reflow correctly. The existing `resize` handler only managed mobile detection and didn't flush the split-layout geometry. Added `_watchDPR()` IIFE using `window.matchMedia('screen and (resolution: Xdppx)')` with `{once:true}` to detect DPR changes (triggered by monitor transitions). On change: forces a synchronous layout flush via `main.style.display='none' + void main.offsetHeight + main.style.display=''`, re-applies split state with recalculated `leftPct`, and triggers redraws for `drawLiquidityChart`, `drawYieldCurve`, and the LightweightCharts FX chart. Fallback: `setInterval` at 2s for browsers that don't support `resolution` media queries.

---

## v7.74.39 (2026-05-11) — Grey gap fix in sovereign yields + CB rates Rate Context Strip

### Frontend — assets/dashboard2.js
- **Grey gap in single-tenor countries (JP, AU, NZ):** `rates-grid` was always `1fr 1fr` (2 columns) regardless of how many tenors a country has. JP/AU/NZ only have 1 tenor (`bond10y`), so the second grid cell was empty and showed `var(--border)` background as a grey block. Fixed by setting `grid-template-columns` dynamically in `renderG8YieldPane`: `1fr` for single-tenor countries, `1fr 1fr` for two-tenor.

### Frontend — assets/cb-rates-modal2.js
- **Removed Decision Calendar heatmap:** Replaced `_cbrBuildHeatmap` with `_cbrBuildContextStrip`. No inflation time-series available in the pipeline (extended-data only has a scalar `inflationExpectations`), so the sparkline option was discarded. Rate Context Strip follows Bloomberg standard.
- **Rate Context Strip:** 8-cell 4×2 grid below the Next Meeting / Fwd Rate footer. Cells: Days to Mtg (from `nextMeetingISO`), Mtgs Left [year] (from `allMeetings`), Cycle Duration (months since first decision in current direction streak), Period High (green), Period Low (red), Avg Move (avg abs bp per decision), Hold Streak (months since last decision), Decisions (count). All computed from existing `decisions` + `chronData` + `meetingData` — no new fetches.

### Frontend — assets/cb-rates-modal2.js
- **CB rates modal all-black / unavailable:** Fixed runtime crash in `_cbrBuildHeatmap`. `rows` was declared as `const rows=[]` (array) but then used with `+=` string concatenation, producing `[object Object]...` in the HTML which crashed the template literal rendering and left the DOM without `cbr-bd`, causing `_transplant` to fail and show "CB rate data unavailable." Fixed by declaring `let rows=''` (string). Also replaced em-dash characters (`—`) in `title` attributes with hyphens to avoid HTML attribute encoding issues.

The previous `background:var(--bg2)` on the wrapper was bleeding through the rows, giving an alternating tint inconsistent with the CBR decisions table pattern. Rows are now uniformly `var(--bg)` with `var(--bg2)` only on the header `th` row.

Each cell represents one calendar month: green for hikes (intensity scales with magnitude), red for cuts, dim for holds, transparent for future months. Month initials header row + legend (Hike / Cut / Hold). Uses the existing `decisions` array — no new data fetched. ### Frontend — assets/dashboard2.js
- **OHLC clamp missing from dashboard2:** Applied the same `h = Math.max(h, o, c)` / `l = Math.min(l, o, c)` structural integrity clamp in `_lwBuildTodayBar` that was added to dashboard.js in v7.74.35. Without this, gap-down sessions still produced inverted candles in the dashboard2 view.

### Frontend — assets/yc-modal2.js
- **Table Change (bp) column uncolored:** Added `#ycm-table td.up { color:var(--up); }` and `#ycm-table td.down { color:var(--down); }` — the classes were being applied to `<td>` elements in HTML but no scoped CSS rule existed, so the column rendered in plain `var(--text)` instead of green/red.
- **Border direction inconsistency:** Changed `border-top` → `border-bottom` on `#ycm-table td` to match CBR modal pattern (separator sits below each row, not above the next).
- **Missing last-row cleanup:** Added `#ycm-table tr:last-child td { border-bottom:none; }` so the final tenor row doesn't render a stray separator against the modal edge.
- **Tenor column weight:** Added `font-weight:500` to `td:first-child` to give the Tenor label slightly more visual authority, consistent with CBR decisions table.

## v7.74.35 (2026-05-11) — Fix FX chart nightly bug: OHLC structural integrity clamp

### Root cause analysis
The FX chart bug that recurs every night (inverted/malformed candles) has two manifestations, both with the same root cause:

**Root cause:** The live today-bar uses `prev_close` as `open` (correct for pct-direction coloring), while `session_high`/`session_low` in `quotes.json` reflect only real intraday ticks from `fetch_intraday_quotes.py`. On gap-down FX sessions (e.g. USD/JPY May 7 2026 dropped ~1.2 pips vs prior close), `prev_close > session_high`, producing `H < O` — a structurally impossible OHLC bar that LightweightCharts renders as a malformed/inverted candle.

The same mechanism in `fetch_ohlc.py` causes `H == O` for gap-down sessions in the JSON (the bucket `open` is set from the first 1H bar's Open, which yfinance FX 1H always returns as the previous tick's close — i.e. `prev_session_close`). On gap-down sessions, `prev_session_close > all 1H highs`, so `high = max(prev_session_close, session_highs) = prev_session_close = open`. This produces wick-less gap-down candles in the JSON.

**Why it recurs every night:** The bug only appears during an active FX session when `prev_close ≠ session_high`. Each morning the OHLC workflow regenerates the JSON and the live bar is updated, masking the issue — until the next evening session opens and the live bar is rebuilt with a fresh `prev_close` that may again exceed `session_high`.

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js
- **OHLC clamp in `_lwBuildTodayBar`:** After assigning `h` and `l`, added `h = Math.max(h, o, c)` and `l = Math.min(l, o, c)`. Prevents structurally invalid bars regardless of data source or session gap direction.

### Engine — scripts/fetch_ohlc.py
- **OHLC clamp in `fetch_fx_ohlc_from_1h`:** Applied `h_r = max(high, open, close)` and `l_r = min(low, open, close)` when writing `bars_1h` entries. Eliminates wick-less gap-down candles from the static JSON.
- **Same clamp in `fetch_gold_ohlc_from_1h`:** Identical fix for Gold (CME boundary).
- **Same clamp in `fetch_wti_dxy_ohlc_from_1h`:** Identical fix for WTI and DXY.
- **Same clamp in `fetch_equity_ohlc_from_1h`:** Identical fix for Nikkei and EuroStoxx.

---

## v7.74.32 (2026-05-11) — Fix CB rates chart sizing (definitive)

### Frontend — assets/cb-rates-modal2.js
- **Root cause (final diagnosis):** COT modal never builds an LWC chart on initial open — its default tab is "Overview" (a lightweight sparkline). LWC charts only build on tab click, when the flex layout is fully resolved. CB rates builds its chart immediately on open in the active default tab, before the flex chain resolves.
- **Fix:** `_cbrDims()` calculates `{w, h}` top-down (modal offsetHeight minus fixed elements). Before calling `LWC.createChart()`, explicit `px` dimensions are set directly on `.cbr-lw-wrap` via `container.style.width/height`. This gives LWC a concrete size to measure at creation time, bypassing the unresolved flex chain entirely. The same dimensions are reapplied on `window.resize`. No `ResizeObserver` (causes infinite loops); no `setTimeout` chains (caused staggered animation).

---

## v7.74.31 (2026-05-11) — Fix CB rates chart size on first open

### Frontend — assets/cb-rates-modal2.js
- **Root cause:** `LWC.createChart()` was called without explicit `width`/`height`, so LWC created the canvas at 0×0. The ResizeObserver was attached to `.cbr-lw-wrap` (the canvas wrapper) — but since on the first open the container already had its final flex-resolved size before the observer was attached, no resize *change* was detected and the observer never fired. The `setTimeout` fallbacks measured the canvas element itself, which reported its just-created small dimensions.
- **Fix 1:** Read initial dimensions from `.parentElement` (`.cbr-chart-area`, the flex-resolved parent) before calling `LWC.createChart()`, and pass explicit `width`/`height` to the constructor.
- **Fix 2:** `apply()` now measures from `container.parentElement` instead of `container` — always gets the available space, not the canvas's own reported size.
- **Fix 3:** `ResizeObserver` now observes the parent element (`.cbr-chart-area`) so it fires on actual layout changes.

---

## v7.74.30 (2026-05-11) — Add Regime label and 252d range rows to correlation modal

### Frontend — assets/corr-modal2.js
- **New row — Regime:** Qualitative label derived from the current 30d correlation value (Strong positive / Moderate positive / Weak positive / Decorrelated / Weak inverse / Moderate inverse / Strong inverse), coloured with `--up`/`--down`/`--text2`, followed by the numeric value. Matches the institutional convention of Bloomberg/Reuters correlation panels.
- **New row — 252d range:** High and low of the full `history` array (252 sessions), calculated client-side. Contextualises whether the current 30d value is near a period extreme or mid-range — information visible in the chart but not previously available as a scannable text value.

## v7.74.29 (2026-05-11) — Align corr-modal2 layout to terminal standard

### Frontend — assets/corr-modal2.js
- **CSS — body:** `padding:12px 14px 16px` + `gap:12px` → `padding:0`, no gap. Scrollbar vars `--bg3` → `--border2`. Consistent with COT/heatmap/CB-rates.
- **CSS — chart wrap:** Removed `background:var(--bg2)`, `border:1px solid var(--border2)`, `border-radius:6px`, `overflow:hidden`. Now borderless with a single `border-bottom` divider below.
- **CSS — section titles (`.cm-section-title`):** `display:none` — hidden, matching heatmap/carry pattern.
- **CSS — regime rows:** Removed `.cm-regime-card` wrapper (bg2 + border + radius + padding). Each `.cm-regime-row` now has its own `padding:8px 14px` and `border-bottom` divider — flat terminal row pattern.
- **CSS — signal:** Removed `margin-top:6px`. Now uses `border-top` + `border-left:3px` as accent (width 2px→3px). Sits flush below the last regime row.
- **CSS — legend:** Moved out of chart wrapper, now has `padding:8px 14px` + `border-bottom`.
- **CSS — borders:** All `var(--border2)` → `var(--border,#252d3d)` for consistency with other modals.
- **HTML:** Removed `<div>` wrappers around chart section and regime section. All content sits directly in `#cm-body` as flat consecutive blocks.
- **Chart — `autoSize` removed:** Same fix as CB rates — replaced with `ResizeObserver` + `setTimeout(60/250ms)`. Cleanup in `closeCorrModal`.
