## v7.74.28 (2026-05-11) — Fix chart sizing (CB rates) and background mismatch (COT)

### Frontend — assets/cb-rates-modal2.js
- **Fix — chart doesn't fill space:** Removed `autoSize:true` (LWC's autoSize requires the container to already have layout dimensions at creation time — it doesn't poll). Replaced the simple `window.resize` listener with the same `ResizeObserver` + deferred `setTimeout(apply, 60/200/500)` pattern used by COT. The chart now correctly measures the container after flex layout settles and fills all available vertical space.
- **Fix — ResizeObserver cleanup:** `closeCBRatesModal()` now calls `_cbrRo.disconnect()` to avoid stale observers on re-open.

### Frontend — assets/cot-modal2.js
- **Fix — chart background mismatch:** `_lwOpts()` background was `#161b22` (`--bg2`) while the surrounding modal uses `--bg` (`#131722`). Net Position, Long/Short, and Participants charts showed a subtly different background. Changed to `#131722` to match `--bg` exactly.

---

## v7.74.27 (2026-05-11) — Fix CB rates chart height — fills available space

### Frontend — assets/cb-rates-modal2.js
- **Fix — chart panel height:** `#cbr-m-body` changed from `overflow-y:auto` to `overflow-y:hidden`. With auto, the body could grow unboundedly so `flex:1` on `.cbr-cw.fill` and `.cbr-chart-area` never resolved a bounded height — LWC rendered at its `min-height:180px` floor and left a large gap below. With `overflow-y:hidden` the flex chain is properly bounded and the chart expands to fill all available vertical space.
- **Fix — decisions tab:** `cbRatesTab()` now sets `body.style.overflowY` to `auto` when switching to Decisions (so the table scrolls) and back to `hidden` when returning to Rate Chart.

---

## v7.74.26 (2026-05-11) — Align cb-rates-modal2 tab/panel layout to terminal standard

### Frontend — assets/cb-rates-modal2.js
- **CSS — tabs:** Font size 10px → 11px, added `font-weight:500` and `font-family:var(--font-ui)`. Hover now `color:var(--text2)` (not `--text`) and transition `.12s` — matches COT/heatmap/carry standard exactly.
- **CSS — body:** Removed `padding:10px 12px` → `padding:0`. Removed `overflow-y:hidden` / `.cbr-body--history` toggle pattern → always `overflow-y:auto`. Scrollbar vars changed from `--bg3` to `--border2` for consistency.
- **CSS — panels:** `#cbr-p-chart.on` gets `overflow-y:hidden` (chart panel never scrolls). `#cbr-p-decisions.on` gets its own `overflow-y:auto` with proper scrollbar rules — decisions panel self-scrolls, body stays static.
- **CSS — card wrapper (`.cbr-cw`):** Removed `background:var(--bg2)`, `border:1px solid var(--border2)`, `border-radius:6px`, `padding:10px 12px`, `margin-bottom:8px` → flat borderless (`background:var(--bg);border:none;border-radius:0;padding:0;margin-bottom:0`).
- **CSS — card title (`.cbr-ct`):** `display:none` — hidden, matching heatmap/carry pattern.
- **HTML — chart panel footer:** Replaced nested card wrapper + rounded grid with flat `border-top` divider row using `--border` and direct column padding — matches COT/heatmap section dividers.
- **HTML — decisions panel:** Table wrapper now `flex:1;min-height:0;overflow:auto` so table scrolls independently. Stats row at bottom uses same flat divider pattern.
- **JS — `cbRatesTab()`:** Removed `body.classList.toggle('cbr-body--history',...)` line — no longer needed since decisions panel self-scrolls.

---

## v7.74.25 (2026-05-11) — Fix sparkline background color mismatch

### Frontend — assets/cot-modal2.js
- **Fix — sparkline background color:** Changed hardcoded `#0d1117` to `#131722` to match the actual `--bg` CSS variable value. The previous value was darker than the surrounding modal, causing a visible rectangle behind the Net Contracts chart.

---

## v7.74.24 (2026-05-11) — Rollback sparkline to working state; minimal bg fix only

### Frontend — assets/cot-modal2.js
- **ROLLBACK — `_buildSparklineChart()`:** Reverted to the original implementation from v7.74.20 which rendered correctly. All changes from v7.74.21–23 (scaleMargins, autoScale, deferred fitContent, canvas CSS override) are removed — they degraded rendering. The only change vs v7.74.20 is replacing `background: 'transparent'` with `background: '#0d1117'` (matches `--bg` of the sparkline row container), which prevents the white canvas bleed on crosshair hover without affecting chart layout.
- **CLEANUP — Remove `#cot-ov-spark-lw canvas` CSS override:** No longer needed with explicit solid background color.

---

## v7.74.23 (2026-05-11) — Fix sparkline line not rendering (price scale / layout timing)

### Frontend — assets/cot-modal2.js
- **BUGFIX — Sparkline line invisible despite data:** Crosshair marker and tooltip were working (data was present) but the area line was not visible. Root cause: with both price scales hidden, LWC's auto-scale had no `scaleMargins` reference and placed the series outside the 72px viewport. Fixed by adding `scaleMargins: { top: 0.1, bottom: 0.1 }` to the hidden `rightPriceScale` and calling `chart.priceScale('right').applyOptions({ autoScale: true })` after `setData()`. Also added two deferred `fitContent()` calls (80ms and 300ms) to handle cases where the container dimensions are not yet final at double-RAF time.

---

## v7.74.22 (2026-05-11) — Fix sparkline not rendering + revert redundant TradingView footer block

### Frontend — assets/cot-modal2.js
- **BUGFIX — Sparkline blank on open:** `_buildSparklineChart()` silently exited when `window.LightweightCharts` was undefined — which happens whenever the COT modal is opened before the main chart panel has lazy-loaded LWC. Added `_cotEnsureLWLib()` (mirrors `dashboard.js` loader, idempotent — resolves immediately if LWC is already present) and wrapped both `openCOTModal` and `cotTab` sparkline calls in `.then(()=>_buildSparklineChart(...))`.
- **BUGFIX — bgColor resolution:** `getComputedStyle(container).getPropertyValue('--bg2')` returned an empty string on freshly-appended elements. Replaced with the hardcoded `#161b22` constant (matches `--bg2` default).

### Frontend — index2.html
- **REVERT — Remove redundant CHARTS · TradingView statusbar block:** The "TV" token in `DATA ECB · CFTC · YF · TV` already denotes TradingView, making the extra `CHARTS` block redundant and causing text overlap with the centred copyright. Statusbar restored to its pre-v7.74.20 state (four info blocks + centred footer-copy).

---

## v7.74.21 (2026-05-11) — Fix sparkline crosshair background + move TradingView attribution to index2 statusbar

### Frontend — assets/cot-modal2.js
- **BUGFIX — Sparkline crosshair background:** LWC v5 does not support `'transparent'` as a layout background colour — the canvas element renders white on crosshair hover. Fixed by resolving the actual `--bg2` CSS variable at chart-init time and passing it as the solid background colour. Added `#cot-ov-spark-lw canvas { background:var(--bg2,#161b22)!important; }` as a CSS guard so all LWC canvas layers (including the pane overlay) inherit the correct dark background.

### Frontend — index2.html
- **ENHANCEMENT — TradingView attribution in statusbar:** Added a `CHARTS · TradingView` block to the statusbar left group (styled with `sb-k`/`sb-v` classes, matching `DATA`, `DELAY`, `SESSION`, `LOCAL`). The TradingView label is an inline link styled to match the surrounding text. The `footer-copy` copyright block remains centred and absolute-positioned as before. No dimensional changes to the statusbar.

---

## v7.74.20 (2026-05-11) — COT Modal Overview: migrate sparkline to Lightweight Charts + TradingView attribution

### Frontend — assets/cot-modal2.js
- **ENHANCEMENT — 12-Week Net Trend sparkline:** Replaced the SVG sparkline with a Lightweight Charts v5 AreaSeries chart. `_cotSparkline()` now returns a `div#cot-ov-spark-lw` placeholder; `_buildSparklineChart()` initialises the LWC chart post-render (after `document.body.appendChild`). Features: gradient area fill (teal/red per direction), `attributionLogo:false`, no axes or time scale visible, crosshair with tooltip showing net contracts, ResizeObserver for responsive width. LWC already loaded from CDN for the Net/Split/Participants tabs — no additional script load. Sparkline container added to `cotTab()` overview handler (lazy-build on first switch back to overview) and to the `closeCOTModal()` cleanup loop.
- **DATA — Sparkline dates:** Synthetic weekly dates generated from `new Date()` working backwards by 7 days per point, since the overview already has access to `history` directly without needing the parsed `dates` array.

### Frontend — index.html
- **ATTRIBUTION — TradingView:** Added "Charts powered by TradingView" link to the statusbar footer `footer-copy` div. This satisfies the Lightweight Charts Apache 2.0 + attribution requirement (NOTICE file method) for the terminal as a whole, covering all LWC chart instances including the new sparkline. Link styled to match existing footer text color with subtle underline.

---

## v7.74.19 (2026-05-11) — COT Modal Overview: enhanced sparkline with gradient fill and glow

### Frontend — assets/cot-modal2.js
- **ENHANCEMENT — 12-Week Net Trend sparkline:** Rebuilt `_cotSparkline()` SVG for visual quality matching financial terminal aesthetics. Changes: (1) viewBox expanded to 400×72 for more height and resolution; (2) area fill uses SVG `linearGradient` (opaque at line, transparent at bottom) instead of flat `rgba`; (3) line renders in two passes — a dimmed base layer + a glow-filtered top layer (`feGaussianBlur` + `feMerge`) for the luminous edge effect; (4) endpoint dot gains a soft halo via a separate `filter` and a bright center pixel; (5) CSS updated to fix sparkline height at 72px and remove lateral padding from the row container so the SVG bleeds edge-to-edge.

---

## v7.74.18 (2026-05-11) — COT Modal Overview: fix sparkline not filling full width

### Frontend — assets/cot-modal2.js
- **BUG FIX — 12-Week Net Trend sparkline width:** The `.cot-spark` class had `max-width:200px` globally. In the Overview context the sparkline row spans the full panel width, but the cap prevented the SVG from expanding. Added `#p-overview .cot-ov-spark-row .cot-spark { max-width:100%;width:100%; }` to override the global cap inside the overview spark row only.

---

## v7.74.17 (2026-05-11) — COT Modal Overview: full KFV redesign (Bloomberg-style)

### Frontend — assets/cot-modal2.js
- **REDESIGN — Overview tab:** Replaced the 2×3 tile grid with a single-column KFV (key-field-value) layout matching institutional terminal standards (Bloomberg / Refinitiv Eikon pattern). Positioning Gauge and Long/Short Split now appear in a shared top row divided by a vertical separator. Below, six thematic sections render as dense horizontal rows: Key Metrics, Participants (with inline magnitude bars and directional badges), 52-Week Range (with range bar and percentile badge), and 12-Week Net Trend (sparkline). Section headers use terminal-style `--text3` uppercase labels with `--border2` top/bottom lines — no brand-color headers. All badges use semantic color tokens: `cot-badge-l` (teal), `cot-badge-s` (red), `cot-badge-n` (blue), `cot-badge-w` (amber). Old grid CSS (`.cot-ov-grid`) and signal-summary tile removed; new scoped CSS added under `#p-overview`.

---

## v7.74.16 (2026-05-11) — COT Modal Overview: fix Participants table clipping

### Frontend — assets/cot-modal2.js
- **BUG FIX — Participants table rows cut off:** The PARTICIPANTS · CURRENT WEEK table in the Overview grid was clipping the last row (Dealers). Fixed by: (1) adding `overflow:visible` to `.cot-ov-grid > .cot-cw` cells so content isn't clipped, and (2) scoping tighter table row padding (`4px 8px` header, `5px 8px` cells) to only the overview grid context, fitting the table within its available cell height.

---

## v7.74.15 (2026-05-11) — COT Modal: Fix mobile CSS overriding desktop 2-col grid

### Frontend — assets/cot-modal2.js
- **BUG FIX — mobile block was unwrapped:** The `@media` wrapper was missing from the mobile CSS block, causing `grid-template-columns:1fr` to apply unconditionally on all viewports — including desktop — collapsing the 2-column overview grid into a single column. Wrapped the entire mobile block in `@media (max-width:480px)` so desktop always shows the 2-col × 3-row layout.

---

## v7.74.14 (2026-05-11) — COT Modal: Overview redesigned as true 2-col × 3-row grid

### Frontend — assets/cot-modal2.js
- **LAYOUT — 2-column grid replaces top/bottom split:** `.cot-ov-top` + `.cot-ov-bottom` removed. Replaced with single `.cot-ov-grid` (`grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr`). Six cells fill three rows symmetrically:
  - Row 1: Gauge | L/S Split
  - Row 2: 12-Week Net Trend | Signal Summary
  - Row 3: 52-Week Range | Participants
- **FIX — Signal Summary no longer orphaned:** Previously occupied a 3rd slot in the top row which caused the Gauge to span full width. Now sits in row 2 right column — no empty space, no layout breakage.
- **CSS — clean border rules:** `nth-child(odd)` gets `border-right`; all cells get `border-bottom` except `nth-last-child(-n+2)` (last row). No wrapper divs needed.
- **Mobile breakpoint:** Single-column stack with `nth-child(odd)` right border removed.

---

## v7.74.13 (2026-05-11) — COT Modal: Overview restructured to 3-col top row

### Frontend — assets/cot-modal2.js
- **FIX — Gauge spanning full width:** Root cause was that `.cot-ov-right` (a wrapper div) was a grid child alongside `.cot-cw` (Gauge), but it was not itself a `.cot-cw`, making CSS grid treat the two columns asymmetrically. Removed `.cot-ov-right` entirely.
- **LAYOUT — Top row now 3-col grid:** `.cot-ov-top` is now `grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr 1fr` with three direct `.cot-cw` children: Gauge | L/S Split | Signal Summary. All cells are symmetric grid children — divisor borders render correctly on all three.
- **LAYOUT — Bottom row unchanged:** `.cot-ov-bottom` stays 3-col: 12-Week Trend | 52-Week Range | Participants. No empty space.
- **Mobile breakpoint simplified:** Removed now-obsolete `.cot-ov-right` mobile overrides.

---

## v7.74.12 (2026-05-11) — COT Modal: Fix Overview grid collapse + chart rAF resize

### Frontend — assets/cot-modal2.js
- **FIX — Overview Gauge full-width:** Added `min-width:0` to all grid and flex children in `.cot-ov-top`, `.cot-ov-right`, and `.cot-ov-bottom`. Without this, CSS grid children can overflow their track, causing the Gauge card to collapse and expand to full-width. Divisor borders (`.cot-ov-right` border-right, bottom-row separators) now render correctly.
- **FIX — `.cot-ov-right` overflow:** Added `overflow:hidden` to prevent flex children from escaping the right column in constrained-width contexts (e.g. narrow inline panel mode).
- **FIX — Chart height fill:** Wrapped `_lwResize` `apply()` in `requestAnimationFrame()` so dimensions are measured after the browser has completed layout and paint, eliminating the residual empty space at the bottom of charts.

---

## v7.74.11 (2026-05-11) — COT Modal: Fix Overview column split + chart height fill

### Frontend — assets/cot-modal2.js
- **FIX — Overview Gauge column:** `.cot-ov-top` border-bottom moved to the grid container (not child `.cot-cw`), so the Gauge card no longer incorrectly spans full width. Gauge now correctly occupies the left column with L/S Split + Signal Summary stacked on the right.
- **FIX — `.cot-ov-right` border:** Removed inherited `border-bottom` from `.cot-ov-right` container (was causing double border); border now only exists on the parent `.cot-ov-top` grid.
- **FIX — Chart panels flex chain:** Added explicit `display:flex;flex-direction:column` to `#p-net.on .cot-cw` and `#p-split.on .cot-cw`, and `flex:1;height:100%` to the `.cot-lw-wrap` inside — completes the flex chain so LWC canvas fills available height.
- **FIX — `_lwResize` uses `getBoundingClientRect`:** Replaced `offsetHeight` with `getBoundingClientRect().height` for accurate height reading on flex-sized containers. Added `setTimeout(apply,500)` third trigger to handle slower layout resolution.
- **FIX — Tab switch resize:** `cotTab` now calls `w._lwResize()` on already-built charts when re-switching tabs, and adds a `setTimeout(120ms)` resize pass after layout settles.

---

## v7.74.10 (2026-05-11) — COT Modal: Overview layout restructure + chart height fix

### Frontend — assets/cot-modal2.js
- **LAYOUT — Overview top row:** Restructured from single 3-column grid to 2-column layout: Gauge (left) | stacked column of L/S Split + Signal Summary (right). Eliminates empty space on the right side of the overview.
- **LAYOUT — Signal Summary repositioned:** Moved from a separate bottom row into the right column of the top row, stacked below L/S Split — no more blank area below 12-Week Net Trend.
- **LAYOUT — Overview bottom row:** Now contains only 3 equal columns: Net Trend | 52w Range | Participants table — tighter and more balanced.
- **CSS — `.cot-ov-top`:** New selector replaces `.cot-ov-grid`; `grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr` with border separators.
- **CSS — `.cot-ov-right`:** New flex column container for the stacked L/S Split + Signal Summary pair.
- **FIX — Chart height (ResizeObserver):** Replaced `window.addEventListener('resize')` in `_lwResize` with `ResizeObserver` on the container. Charts now correctly fill available height without leaving blank space below.
- **FIX — Staggered resize triggers:** Added `setTimeout(apply,60)` and `setTimeout(apply,200)` calls to catch post-paint height resolution.
- **FIX — `closeCOTModal` cleanup:** Disconnects any active `ResizeObserver` instances (`_lwRo.disconnect()`) in addition to removing window resize listeners.
- **CSS — Mobile breakpoint:** Updated to use `.cot-ov-top` (single column) and `.cot-ov-right` (row wrap) for small screens — replaces stale `.cot-ov-grid` references.

---

## v7.74.9 (2026-05-11) — COT Modal: aesthetic alignment with Real Carry and Heatmap Modals

### Frontend — assets/cot-modal2.js
- **STYLE — Header padding:** Reduced from `14px 18px 12px` to `10px 14px 9px`, matching carry/heatmap modal density.
- **STYLE — Header border:** Changed from `var(--border2)` to `var(--border,#252d3d)` — consistent with other modals.
- **STYLE — Title typography:** Added `line-height:1.2` and explicit `font-family:var(--font-ui,...)` — matches heatmap modal title style.
- **STYLE — Close button:** Reduced from 18px/`5px 7px`/radius 5px to 16px/`3px 6px`/radius 3px — consistent with `#rcm-close` and `#hm-close`.
- **STYLE — Metrics strip gap:** Reduced from `gap:2px` to `gap:1px`; border color changed to `var(--border,#252d3d)`.
- **STYLE — Metrics label:** Font-weight raised to `600`, letter-spacing raised to `.09em` — matches carry/heatmap label convention.
- **STYLE — Metrics value/sub:** Added explicit `margin-top` spacing for tighter label-value rhythm.
- **STYLE — Tabs padding:** Changed from `0 18px` to `0 14px`; active tab indicator kept `var(--blue)` per both references; font-weight raised to `500`.
- **STYLE — Tab font:** Added `font-family:var(--font-ui,...)` to tab buttons — consistent with carry modal.
- **STYLE — Body padding removed:** `#cot-m-body` padding reduced from `14px 16px` to `0`; scrollbar updated to `var(--border2,#2e3a50)` at 3px width.
- **STYLE — Card wrappers flat:** `.cot-cw` border, border-radius, and `var(--bg2)` background removed. Sections are now flat on `var(--bg)` separated by a 1px `var(--border,#252d3d)` bottom line, matching carry/heatmap panel style.
- **STYLE — Section labels (`.cot-ct`):** Changed to `font-ui 8.5px var(--text3) weight:600` — matches `.hm-ct` and `.rcm-tbl thead th` label convention.
- **STYLE — Overview panels:** Adapted to flat card layout — gap/margin replaced with border-bottom separators; scrollbar updated to `var(--border2,#2e3a50)` at 3px.
- **STYLE — Chart area:** `flex:1;min-height:0` layout so charts fill available height without empty space below.
- **STYLE — Participants panel:** Scrollbar updated to `var(--border2,#2e3a50)` at 3px.
- **STYLE — Table rows:** Added `tbody tr:nth-child(even)` subtle alternating background and `tbody tr:hover` highlight; `thead th` font-size raised to `9px` with `8px 10px` padding; `td` padding increased to `7px 10px`.
- **STYLE — Scrollbars:** All scrollbar colors updated from hardcoded `#444c56` to `var(--border2,#2e3a50)`; widths reduced to 3px matching carry/heatmap modals.
- **FIX — Chart height:** `_lwResize` now uses `parentElement.offsetHeight` fallback when the container itself reports 0 — prevents charts rendering at minimum height with empty space below.
- **FIX — Mobile breakpoint:** Removed old `padding:8px`/`gap:6px` refs; adapted to flat card layout with `padding:0` body and `padding:10px 12px` card.

---

## v7.74.8 (2026-05-11) — Heatmap Modal: aesthetic alignment with Real Carry Modal

### Frontend — assets/heatmap-modal2.js
- **STYLE — Remove Currency Strength badge:** Eliminated the blue dot badge above the title. Matches the header style of the Real Carry Modal.
- **STYLE — Header padding:** Reduced from `14px 18px 12px` to `10px 14px 9px`, matching carry modal density.
- **STYLE — Close button:** Reduced from 18px to 16px, padding from `5px 7px` to `3px 6px`, border-radius from 5px to 3px — consistent with `#rcm-close`.
- **STYLE — Tabs padding:** Changed from `0 18px` to `0 14px`; active tab indicator uses `var(--text)` instead of `var(--blue)` — matches carry modal tab convention.
- **STYLE — Body padding removed:** `#hm-body` padding reduced from `14px 16px` to `0`; each `.hm-cw` section now owns its own `padding:14px` — consistent with carry modal's section-level padding model.
- **STYLE — Card wrappers flat:** `.hm-cw` border, border-radius, and `var(--bg2)` background removed. Sections are now flat on `var(--bg)` separated by a 1px `var(--border)` line, matching carry modal panel style.
- **STYLE — Section labels:** `.hm-ct` font changed from `font-mono 9.5px var(--text2)` to `font-ui 8.5px var(--text3) weight:600` — matches `.rcm-tbl thead th` label convention.
- **STYLE — Scrollbars:** All scrollbar colors updated from hardcoded `#444c56` to `var(--border2,#2e3a50)`; widths reduced to 3px matching carry modal.
- **STYLE — Typography:** `#hm-title` gains `line-height:1.2` and explicit font-family; `#hm-sub` font-family updated to full JetBrains Mono stack.
- **CLEAN — Bloomberg references removed:** Removed 5 Bloomberg references from comments and inline notes.

---

## v7.74.7 (2026-05-10) — Real Carry Modal: cache bust, scrollbar force-hide, matrix fixed width, row-head color parity

### Frontend — index2.html
- **FIX — Browser cache not busted:** `real-carry-modal2.js` version string updated from `v=2.1.0` to `v=2.7.5`. All previous visual fixes since v2.1.0 were being served from browser cache, making them appear to have no effect. This is the root cause of "no visual change after uploading".

### Frontend — assets/real-carry-modal2.js
- **FIX — Scrollbar width force:** `#rcm-body::-webkit-scrollbar` now uses `width:3px!important` to prevent OS/browser overrides. At ≤640px the scrollbar is fully hidden with `display:none!important` on webkit and `scrollbar-width:none` — eliminating the wide native scrollbar visible in the Pair Detail tab on small monitors.
- **FIX — Matrix row-head color/size parity with column headers:** `td.row-head` font-size corrected to `9px` + `letter-spacing:.04em` — identical to `th` column headers. Corner cell (L↓/S→) inline `color:var(--text3)` removed, now inherits `color:var(--text2)` from CSS like all other row headers. Consistent with Bloomberg FX matrix convention where row and column labels are visually equivalent.
- **FIX — Matrix horizontal scroll on small screens:** Changed from `width:100%;min-width:580px` (which caused cells to be crushed on narrow viewports) to fixed `width:625px`. The `#rcm-matrix-wrap` uses `overflow-x:auto;overflow-y:auto` explicitly — on screens narrower than 625px the matrix scrolls horizontally rather than squeezing columns. NZD column no longer gets cut off.

---

## v7.74.6 (2026-05-10) — Real Carry Modal: fix left margin gap, scrollbar width, matrix responsive

### Frontend — assets/real-carry-modal2.js
- **FIX — Thin left border gap:** `#rcm-bd` lacked `position:fixed;inset:0` — it was sitting in normal DOM flow adjacent to the sidebar's `border-right:1px solid var(--border)`, creating a visible gap on the left edge. Added `position:fixed!important;inset:0!important;z-index:9200` matching the v1 modal pattern. Modal now covers the full viewport including sidebar area.
- **FIX — Pair Detail scrollbar width:** `#rcm-body` webkit scrollbar reduced from 4px to 3px, matching the dashboard sidebar scrollbar convention (dashboard.css `#sidebar::-webkit-scrollbar{width:3px}`). On ≤640px screens the scrollbar is hidden entirely (`scrollbar-width:none`) to reclaim space on small monitors.
- **FIX — Matrix responsive scaling:** Added `@media(max-width:900px)` and `@media(max-width:640px)` rules for `.rcm-matrix` — cell height, font size, and `min-width` scale down progressively. At 640px cells shrink to 26px height / 8.5px font; `min-width:400px` keeps the table scrollable rather than broken. Matrix wrap padding also reduced at breakpoints.
- **DECISION — "Pair Detail" tab name kept:** Bloomberg FX Carry and Refinitiv do not have a canonical equivalent tab name. "Pair Detail" is precise and industry-appropriate; the internal header "LONG X / SHORT Y" is the institutional standard. No change.

---

## v7.74.5 (2026-05-10) — Real Rate Matrix: fix diagonal color + full-width layout

### Frontend — assets/real-carry-modal2.js
- **FIX — Matrix diagonal: remove green/red color:** Diagonal cells (absolute real rate) were incorrectly colored green/red via an inline JS style introduced in v7.74.2. Per the reference design, diagonal cells show the real rate value in neutral grey (`--text2`) against a dimmed background (`--bg2`) — the color coding applies only to the off-diagonal spread cells. Removed inline color style from JS; `.rcm-matrix td.diag` now uses `color:var(--text2)` explicitly.
- **FIX — Matrix layout: fixed width → 100% fluid:** Table was hardcoded to `width:620px`, causing it to overflow on narrower modals or appear cramped on wider ones. Changed to `width:100%;min-width:580px` with `table-layout:fixed` so the 8 data columns share available width equally and the wrap scrolls horizontally only on small screens.

---

## v7.74.4 (2026-05-10) — Real Carry Modal: footnote legibility + rate bar font consistency

### Frontend — assets/real-carry-modal2.js
- **FIX — Source notes font size: 8px → 10px:** `.rcm-src-note` (used in both Rates Breakdown and Real Rate Matrix) was at 8px — below the terminal's minimum readable threshold. Terminal standard for body/data text is 10px (per dashboard.css: `.hm-val`, `.cot-pct`, `.rate-chg`, etc). Bumped to 10px. Same fix applied to the matrix legend inline `<div>` and `.rcm-matrix-legend span`.
- **FIX — Rate bar label and value font: inherit → font-mono:** `.rcm-rb-label` and `.rcm-rb-val` (the currency label and `+1.14%` percentage value in "Real rate positioning — G8") had no `font-family` set, inheriting `--font-ui` (Inter) from the modal base. All numeric values in this terminal use `--font-mono` (JetBrains Mono). Added explicit `font-family:var(--font-mono)` to both. Font size bumped from 9px to 10px for consistency with terminal data rows.

---

## v7.74.3 (2026-05-10) — Real Carry Modal: restore metrics strip size, fix matrix layout uniformity

### Frontend — assets/real-carry-modal2.js
- **FIX — Metrics strip: mm-val size restored to 18px/700:** Previous session incorrectly reduced `rcm-mm-val` from 18px to 13px based on a cross-modal comparison that didn't apply here. The 18px bold size was intentional for this panel's header metrics (Best real / Worst real / Max spread). Restored to 18px/font-weight:700, responsive override restored to 14px at ≤640px. Label and sub-label corrected to `font-ui` (Inter) — only the numeric value uses `font-mono`.
- **FIX — Real Rate Matrix: uniform cell layout:** Added `table-layout:fixed;width:620px` to `.rcm-matrix` so all 8 data columns are exactly equal width (72px each) regardless of content. Removed `width:auto` from `row-head` (now fixed 44px). Removed inline `padding` overrides from JS-rendered `<th>` and corner `<td>` that were competing with CSS. Matrix now renders with Bloomberg-style uniform grid matching reference image.

## v7.74.2 (2026-05-10) — Real Carry Modal: Real Rate color fix, matrix diagonal colors, Infl. Exp. metric

### Frontend — assets/real-carry-modal2.js
- **FIX — Rates Breakdown: Real Rate column color not rendering:** CSS specificity conflict resolved — `.rcm-tbl tbody td { color:var(--text) }` was overriding `.rr-pos1/.rr-neg1/.rr-pos2/.rr-neg2` classes. Fixed by adding `!important` to the color rules and adding `.rcm-tbl tbody td.rr-*` compound selectors. Real Rate column now correctly renders green (positive) / red (negative) per the reference image.
- **FIX — Real Rate Matrix: diagonal cells not colored:** `.rcm-matrix td.diag` was hardcoding `color:var(--text3)` grey, overriding the per-cell inline color set by JS. Removed fixed color from CSS; JS inline style (`color:var(--up)` / `color:var(--down)`) now renders correctly. Diagonal font size updated to 10.5px for visual parity with off-diagonal cells.
- **REDESIGN — Metrics strip: "Data source" → "Infl. Exp.":** The "Data source" metric showing "Batch / extended-data batch" was non-standard (no institutional terminal exposes internal pipeline names). Renamed to "Infl. Exp." and values updated to "FRED · IMF" (live breakeven mode) / "IMF · OECD" (batch mode) with sub-label "USD/EUR: live breakeven" / "CPI proxy · weekly" — consistent with Bloomberg/Eikon data source attribution conventions.

## v7.74.1 (2026-05-10) — Carry Ranking visual redesign (index2) + Real Carry Modal font audit & Bloomberg-style Breakdown/Matrix

### Frontend — assets/dashboard2.js
- **REDESIGN — Carry Trade Ranking visual format:** Reverts the row layout from the 4-column table design (PAIR · NOM · CIP · SIGNAL) back to the compact bar-chart format consistent with index.html (rank number · pair · nominal spread label · proportional bar · CIP-adj value). Maintains CIP-adjusted carry as primary sort metric (Bloomberg FXFR standard) with carry-to-vol tiebreak. Bar width is proportional to CIP-adj carry of the top pair; value coloring: green ≥+0.5%, dim <−0.1%. Header tooltip updated to "CIP-Adjusted Carry". Header `carry-rank-hdr` row removed — design returns to the compact sidebar-native style.
- **FIX — Carry Ranking: subtitle normalization:** Static HTML subtitle in index2.html updated to "G8 · CIP-adjusted · click for real rate"; dynamic JS subtitle updated to "G8 · CIP-adjusted · annualised" or "G8 · CB rate differential" as fallback.

### Frontend — assets/real-carry-modal2.js
- **FONT AUDIT — Uniform typography aligned to terminal standard:** `#rcm-modal` base font corrected from `var(--font-mono)` to `var(--font-ui)` (Inter). Mono font now applied only to numeric values (table `td`, `rcm-mm-val`, `rcm-pd-cell-val`, `rcm-vol-val`, `rcm-rb-val`). UI labels, tab names, column headers, source notes, sustain body, bias chips, legend text all set to `var(--font-ui)`. Consistent with rest of terminal.
- **REDESIGN — Rates Breakdown (Tab 1):** Adopts Bloomberg-style layout matching the reference image: rank column (#), Central Bank name + CCY code side by side, Nominal, Infl.Exp. (with live dot when FRED), Real Rate (green/red bold), OIS Bias pill. Removes "Data Age" column. Top row has green left-border accent; bottom row red left-border. Larger font (11px mono on values, 8.5px UI on headers).
- **REDESIGN — Real Rate Matrix (Tab 2):** Full symmetric 8×8 matrix replacing lower-triangle-only design. All 64 cells rendered: diagonal shows absolute real rate (colored positive/negative), off-diagonal shows signed real rate spread. L↓/S→ corner label. Column headers in UI font. Cell size increased to 68×34px. Color thresholds: ≥+1.5% = strong green (rgba 26%), ≥+0.10% = light green (rgba 12%), ≤−0.10% = light red, ≤−1.5% = strong red — matching the reference image density. Legend relocated inside matrix-wrap, uses UI font.
- **FIX — Color vars:** All references to `--dn` / `#26c9a0` / `#e05252` replaced with terminal-canonical `--down,#ef5350` and `--up,#26a69a` (matching dashboard.css `:root`).

### Frontend — index2.html
- **FIX — Carry sidebar static subtitle:** Updated from "click for real rate analysis" to "G8 · CIP-adjusted · click for real rate". Static footer div removed (subtitle now managed by JS dynamically).

## v7.74.0 (2026-05-10) — YC modal inline fix + Real Carry Modal v3.0 Bloomberg-style tabless redesign

### Frontend — assets/inline-panel.js
- **FIX — BUG-23 (yc-modal2):** El YC modal no estaba interceptado por el sistema de inline-panel, lo que causaba que `yc-modal2.js` intentara posicionarse con `position:absolute; left:50%` dentro de `#main` y se superpusiera con el contenido del panel. Se agrega intercepción `openYCModal → #split-lower` usando el mismo patrón de transplant que COT/CB Rates/Correlations. El botón close del modal original se oculta (el shell de inline-panel provee el propio).

### Frontend — assets/real-carry-modal2.js
- **REDESIGN v3.0 — Arquitectura Bloomberg-style tabless:** Elimina el sistema de tabs como navegación primaria. Todo el contenido ahora aparece en una sola vista sin necesidad de pestañas: (1) header compacto + metrics strip, (2) layout de dos columnas — columna izquierda con el Carry Ranking completo G8 (clickeable), columna derecha con el Pair Detail que aparece al seleccionar dos filas, (3) Real Rate Differential Matrix full-width al pie. Tabs solo se muestran en pantallas < 640px (convención Bloomberg mobile). Interacción: primer click en ranking = selecciona long leg (borde verde), segundo click = selecciona short leg (borde rojo) y abre el Pair Detail automáticamente. Click en la misma fila = deselecciona.

## v7.73.19 (2026-05-10) — Real Carry Modal v2: rediseño Pair Detail con rate bars y métricas

### Frontend — assets/real-carry-modal2.js
- **REDESIGN — Pair Detail tab:** Reemplaza el grid de 8 KV cards por una estructura de filas a nivel industria: (1) header con par activo prominente, (2) 3-column metric grid (nominal carry / real carry / long real rate), (3) real rate bar chart de los G8 ordenados — longitudes proporcionales al máximo absoluto, zero-line centrada, long/short leg resaltados en verde/rojo, (4) vol row (short real rate / nom carry-vol / real carry-vol), (5) OIS bias row de 2 columnas, (6) sustainability banner con borde izquierdo de color en lugar de box background.
- **MEJORA — Header dinámico:** El título cambia a `LONG / SHORT` cuando está en tab Pair Detail, y vuelve a "Real Rate Carry Analysis" en los otros tabs.
- **MEJORA — Metrics strip:** Pasa de 4 a 5 columnas; se agrega `Pairs w/ + real` (conteo de G8 con real rate positivo). Grid de columnas iguales en lugar de `flex:1`.

- **ESTILO — Sustain banner:** Deja de ser un box redondeado con background coloreado; adopta borde izquierdo de 3px y background muy sutil — menos intrusivo, más legible.

## v7.73.18 (2026-05-10) — YC Modal (index.html): inline panel sobre mitad derecha de #main

### Frontend — assets/yc-modal.js
- **BUG-22 — YC Modal (producción index.html) ocupaba toda la pantalla como overlay full-screen.** El `#ycm-bd` usaba `position:fixed;inset:0` con backdrop oscuro y el modal centrado a `width:min(800px,100%)` — misma estética que un modal popup genérico, desalineada con el resto del terminal. Fix: se adopta el mismo patrón de posicionamiento que `yc-modal2.js` (index2): `position:absolute; top:0; bottom:0; left:50%; right:0` sobre `#main` como containing block. El modal se inserta como hijo de `#main` en lugar de `document.body`. Todos los colores hardcodeados (`#161b22`, `#30363d`, `#6e7681`) se reemplazan con CSS variables del terminal. Padding y tamaños de fuente ajustados al estilo compacto del panel (alineados con `yc-modal2.js`).

---

## v7.73.17 (2026-05-10) — YC Modal: ocupa mitad derecha de #main sin solapar contenido izquierdo

### Frontend — assets/yc-modal2.js
- **BUG-21 — YC Modal se solapaba con el contenido izquierdo de #main al usar position:fixed con left calculado.** Cualquier cálculo de `left` basado en `getBoundingClientRect` o `offsetWidth` es frágil ante resize y scroll. Fix arquitectónico: se abandona `position:fixed` y el append a `body`. En su lugar: (1) `#ycm-bd` usa `position:absolute; top:0; bottom:0; left:50%; right:0` — coordenadas relativas al contenedor padre. (2) El modal se inserta como hijo de `#main` (no de `body`). (3) Se añade `position:relative` a `#main` vía el CSS del modal para que sea el containing block. Con este enfoque, el modal siempre ocupa exactamente la mitad derecha de `#main` sin ningún cálculo manual de píxeles, y se adapta automáticamente a cualquier ancho del panel, sea por resize de ventana o por layout-resizer.

---

## v7.73.16 (2026-05-10) — Carry Ranking v2: ordenar por CIP-adjusted (estándar industria)

### Frontend — assets/dashboard2.js
- **UX — Carry Trade Ranking ahora ordena por CIP-adjusted real carry como métrica primaria.** El sort anterior usaba `carryVol` (diferencial nominal / HV30) como primario, con el nominal bruto como fallback — el CIP-adjusted solo se calculaba en el render para visualización. Problema: el ranking mostraba los pares con mayor diferencial de tasas nominales ajustado por volatilidad, ignorando el costo real de hold (inflación diferencial). Esto es incorrecto institucionalmente: JPMorgan, Deutsche Bank y Bloomberg usan real carry (o CIP-adjusted) como métrica de ranking primaria desde los 90s. Fix: (1) `cipVal` se calcula en el loop de construcción de pares (no en el render) para estar disponible como sort key. (2) Sort: CIP-adjusted primario → carry-to-vol como tiebreaker → nominal bruto como último fallback cuando no hay datos de inflación. (3) Se elimina el cálculo duplicado de `cipVal` en el render (ahora viene de `p.cipVal`). (4) Tooltip del header actualizado para reflejar la nueva lógica.

---

## v7.73.15 (2026-05-10) — YC Modal: overlay sobre #main + gradiente en chart

### Frontend — assets/yc-modal2.js
- **BUG-20a — Yield Curve modal emergia sobre el rightpanel en lugar del área central (#main).** El `#ycm-bd` tenía `right:0` fijo, superponiéndose sobre el panel derecho. Fix: se elimina el `right:0` y el `width` fijo del CSS. En `openYCModal()` se leen dinámicamente `sidebar.offsetWidth` y `rightpanel.offsetWidth` y se asignan a `bd.style.left` y `bd.style.right` respectivamente, de modo que el modal ocupa exactamente la columna central independientemente del estado del layout-resizer.
- **BUG-20b — Yield Curve modal aparecia al fondo de la página con ancho completo.** Causa raíz documentada en v7.73.15 previo: `#ycm-bd` sin `position:fixed`. Corregido con `position:fixed; top:92px; height:calc(100vh - 92px); z-index:500; border-left/right:1px solid var(--border2)`.
- **UX — Chart de la yield curve: gradiente top-to-bottom en lugar de fill sólido.** Se reemplaza `backgroundColor:blue.replace(...)` (opacidad fija 0.08 en todo el área) por un `CanvasGradient` creado con `ctx.createLinearGradient()`: azul a 35% opacidad en la parte superior → 8% a 60% del alto → 0% al fondo. Se añade `hexAlpha()` helper para convertir correctamente el valor CSS `--blue` (que puede venir como hex `#4f7fff`) a `rgba()` sin depender de string-replace frágil.

---

### Frontend — index2.html
- **UX — Espacio libre al fondo del Carry Ranking rellenado con footer de metodología.** Se agrega `<div>G8 · CIP-adjusted · annualised · carry-to-vol</div>` como elemento estático debajo de `#carry-rank-rows`, separado con `border-top`. Esto ocupa el espacio visible cuando el sidebar tiene más altura que las 10 filas de datos, y comunica la metodología a nivel industria. Se simplifica el subtítulo del header de `"G8 · carry-to-vol · click for real rate"` a `"click for real rate analysis"` ya que la info de metodología ahora vive en el footer.

## v7.73.13 (2026-05-10) — Carry Ranking v2: tipografía alineada con Key Correlations

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.css
- **UX — Carry Ranking v2 adopta el sistema tipográfico de Key Correlations** para mejor legibilidad en el espacio estrecho del sidebar. Cambios: (1) `.cr-pair` baja de 10.5px → 10px igualando los `td` de correlations. (2) `.cr-nom`, `.cr-cip` y `.cr-sig` unificados en 10px (vs 9–10px anteriores). (3) Headers `.crh-*` mantienen 8px, igual que los `th` de correlations. (4) Padding de rows v2 reducido a `2px 4px` y del header a `3px 4px`, idénticos a `td`/`th` de correlations. (5) `padding-right` en NOM./CIP reducido de 6px → 4px para ganar espacio útil.

## v7.73.12 (2026-05-10) — Carry Ranking v2: columnas uniformes en todo el rango de ancho del sidebar

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.css
- **BUG-19 — SIGNAL queda desalineada con espacio vacío al ajustar ancho del sidebar.** La causa: `.carry-rank-hdr` y `.carry-rank-row` usaban definiciones de grid separadas y la columna SIGNAL tenía `display:flex; justify-content:flex-end` que empuja el contenido al extremo derecho independientemente del ancho real. Al comprimir el sidebar, NOM. y CIP se achicaban pero SIGNAL quedaba flotando con espacio vacío antes de ella. Fix: (1) `.carry-rank-hdr` y el selector `:has()` para las rows ahora comparten **una sola regla de grid** (`grid-template-columns: 46px 1fr 1fr minmax(54px, max-content)`) para garantizar alineación perfecta. (2) La columna SIGNAL pasa de `display:flex` a `text-align:right` — más simple y no crea un formatting context separado. (3) `minmax(54px, max-content)` le da a SIGNAL un mínimo de 54px (suficiente para "▲ Positive") y crece con el espacio disponible sin robarle espacio a NOM./CIP. (4) Se añade `overflow:hidden; text-overflow:ellipsis` en NOM., CIP y SIGNAL para evitar desbordamiento en sidebar muy estrecho.

---

## v7.73.11 (2026-05-10) — Carry Trade Ranking: coexistencia v1/v2 en dashboard.css sin dashboard2.css

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.css
- **FEATURE — Carry Ranking v2 (index2) coexiste con v1 (index) en el mismo dashboard.css.** En lugar de crear un `dashboard2.css`, se aprovecha el selector CSS `:has()` para activar la grid de 4 columnas (`46px 1fr 1fr 68px`) únicamente cuando `#carry-rank-rows` contiene un `.carry-rank-hdr` como hijo directo — condición que solo ocurre cuando `dashboard2.js` renderiza el componente. `index.html` + `dashboard.js` (sin header) siguen usando la grid de 5 columnas original sin cambios. Se agregan las clases v2 (`crh-*`, `.cr-nom`, `.cr-cip`, `.cr-sig`, `.cr-sig-*`) sin colisionar con las clases v1 (`cr-rank`, `cr-spread`, `cr-bar-wrap`, `cr-diff`). Ambas versiones operan desde el mismo archivo CSS compartido.

---

## v7.73.10 (2026-05-10) — Carry Trade Ranking: restaurar CSS al diseño original (fix desacople con JS)

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.css
- **BUG-18 — Carry Trade Ranking visualmente roto en producción.** Las versiones v7.73.7–v7.73.9 actualizaron el CSS del Carry Ranking a una nueva grid de 4 columnas (PAIR | NOMINAL | CIP ADJ. | SIGNAL) con clases nuevas (`crh-*`, `cr-sig`, `cr-nom`, `cr-cip`), pero el JS de producción (`fetchCarryRanking`) sigue emitiendo el HTML original de 5 columnas (`cr-rank`, `cr-pair`, `cr-spread`, `cr-bar-wrap`, `cr-diff`). El desacople entre el CSS nuevo y el HTML viejo causaba el layout bugueado visible en producción. Fix: se restaura el bloque CSS del Carry Trade Ranking exactamente al diseño original de 5 columnas (`10px 46px 36px 1fr 34px`) que es lo que el JS de producción genera. El rediseño (index2) continúa en su rama separada sin afectarse.

---

## v7.73.9 (2026-05-10) — Carry Ranking: SIGNAL column alignment fix + inline panel system

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.css
- **BUG-17 — SIGNAL column header misaligned vs content.** `.crh-sig` and `.cr-sig` used `text-align:right` but the content spans (`.cr-sig-strong`, etc.) are `display:inline` inside a block container, so they flow left-to-right regardless of the parent's text-align. Fixed by replacing `text-align:right` with `display:flex; justify-content:flex-end` on both the header and row cells — ensures the inner span always right-aligns correctly at all sidebar widths.
- **LAYOUT — adjusted carry table column proportions.** PAIR column reduced from `52px` to `46px`, SIGNAL column widened from `56px` to `68px` (enough for "▲ Positive" at 9px mono), numeric columns remain `1fr 1fr`. Padding on numeric cols reduced to `4px` to maximise usable space in 180px sidebar.

### Frontend — assets/inline-panel.js (new file)
- **FEATURE — Inline panel system.** Replaces floating modal overlays with inline content rendered directly inside the split-layout center columns. Carry Trade Ranking and Currency Strength Heatmap open into `#split-upper` (left center); Correlations, CB Rates, and COT Positioning open into `#split-lower` (right center). Automatically activates split-layout if not already enabled. Each inline panel has a compact header bar with title and ✕ close button that restores the panel to its original state. The underlying modal DOM is preserved intact so fallback and keyboard navigation remain functional.

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js
- **MERGE — dashboard2.js merged into dashboard.js.** All changes from v7.73.4 through v7.73.8 (previously delivered as `dashboard2.js` in session outputs) are now in the canonical `assets/dashboard.js` loaded by `index.html`. `dashboard2.js` kept as identical copy for continuity.

### Frontend — index.html
- Added `<script src="assets/inline-panel.js?v=1.0.0" defer></script>` after `share.js` (last in load order — must intercept after all modal scripts define their `open*` globals).

---

## v7.73.8 (2026-05-10) — Carry Ranking: fix CIP ADJ. always equal to NOMINAL + fix column layout

### Frontend — assets/dashboard2.js
- **BUG-14 — CIP ADJ. always displayed nominal value.** The v7.73.7 implementation read `_rcmData.realRates` (the real-carry-modal's cache), but `_rcmData` is `null` at boot — it only populates when the user opens the modal. The fallback was `nomStr`, making NOMINAL and CIP ADJ. identical for every row. Fixed: `fetchCarryRanking()` now fetches `inflationExpectations` directly from `extended-data/{CCY}.json` (step 2b, parallel `Promise.all` over G8), using the same field written weekly by `update-inflation-expectations.yml`. This mirrors exactly what `real-carry-modal.js` Phase 1 does. If `_rcmData` is already populated (modal was opened earlier), its cached values are reused to avoid duplicate fetches. Real carry formula: `cipVal = nomDiff − (inflLong − inflShort)`.
- **BUG-15 — CIP ADJ. shows `—` when infl.exp. unavailable**, instead of falling back to the misleading nominal value. This is more honest — `—` signals "data unavailable" rather than implying real carry equals nominal carry.

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.css
- **BUG-16 — "NOMINALCIP ADJ." header columns crushed together.** The fixed-width grid `52px 42px 50px 1fr` allocated only 42px to NOMINAL and 50px to CIP ADJ., insufficient for the column labels at the panel's narrow sidebar width. Changed to `52px 1fr 1fr 56px` — pair column fixed-width, SIGNAL fixed-width, NOMINAL and CIP ADJ. share remaining space equally. Added `padding-right:6px` to numeric columns for separation.

---

## v7.73.7 (2026-05-10) — Rates panel: unified tile design for G8 countries + Carry Ranking reformulated as CIP-adjusted table

### Frontend — assets/dashboard2.js
- **DESIGN-1 — G8 yield panes now use the same tile format as the US panel.** `renderG8YieldPane()` previously rendered a minimal flex row with no change indicator. Replaced with `class="rates-grid"` (same grid used by the US panel) so DE, GB, CA tiles show: label row (`rate-cty`) / value row (`rate-val`) / change row (`rate-chg flat`). Change indicator is always `—` with `flat` class because extended-data has no intraday delta (consistent with how US tiles show `—` when `fromRepo=true`). `G8_YIELD_MAP` updated: added subtitle strings per country (e.g. `GERMANY · SOVEREIGN BOND YIELDS`) and added `bond2y` tenor to CA (`2Y CGB` via BoC Valet, confirmed working). JP, AU, NZ retain only the 10Y tenor where no 2Y source is available.
- **DESIGN-2 — Carry Trade Ranking reformulated as PAIR / NOMINAL / CIP ADJ. / SIGNAL table.** Replaces the bar-chart row layout (`cr-rank`, `cr-bar-wrap`, `cr-bar`, `cr-diff`). New columns: PAIR (unchanged), NOMINAL (gross CB rate differential, e.g. `+3.75%`), CIP ADJ. (real carry = `_rcmData.realRates[long] − _rcmData.realRates[short]` when real-carry-modal data is available; falls back to nominal), SIGNAL (`▲ Strong` ≥+2.0% / `▲ Positive` ≥+0.5% / `— Neutral` > −0.5% / `▼ Weak` > −2.0% / `▼ Negative` ≤ −2.0%). Panel subtitle updated to `G8 · CIP-adjusted · annualised`. Signal thresholds are consistent with `real-carry-modal.js` real-rate coloring.

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.css
- **DESIGN-2 CSS — carry ranking layout updated.** Old grid `10px 46px 36px 1fr 34px` replaced with `52px 42px 50px 1fr` (4-column: PAIR / NOMINAL / CIP ADJ. / SIGNAL). Added `.carry-rank-hdr` for column header row, `.crh-*` column header spans, `.cr-nom` / `.cr-cip` / `.cr-sig` value spans, and `.cr-sig-strong` / `.cr-sig-pos` / `.cr-sig-neu` / `.cr-sig-weak` / `.cr-sig-neg` signal chips. Legacy `.cr-rank` / `.cr-bar-wrap` / `.cr-bar` / `.cr-diff` / `.cr-spread` kept for backwards compatibility with any external reference.

---

## v7.73.6 (2026-05-10) — fetch_bond_yields v1.8: evict stale cached bond2y written by discontinued series

### Engine — scripts/fetch_bond_yields.py
- **BUG-13 — JPY bond2y: v1.7 detected the stale series correctly but preserved a corrupted cache.** The v1.6 run had written `bond2y: 0.04, date: 2017-05-01` to `JPY.json`. When v1.7 ran, it detected `INTGSBJPM193N` as stale and skipped the write — but then fell through to the "keeping cached" branch, which read back the 2017 value from the JSON and left it in place. Root cause: cache-preservation is only valid for *transient network failures* (source exists but is temporarily unreachable). It must not apply when the source is confirmed discontinued. Fixed: when `_is_stale()` returns `True`, `data.pop("bond2y")` and `dates.pop("bond2y")` are called before saving, evicting the corrupt cached value. The field is unconditionally `None` after this path.
- **IMPROVE-4 — NZD bond2y: applied the same eviction pattern** to the `INTGSBNZM193N` stale branch as a precaution, consistent with JPY fix.
- Simplified else-branch logic: removed the redundant `existing_2y is not None` fork — after a confirmed-discontinued series there is no valid fallback regardless of what the cache contains.
- Bumped internal version string to `v1.8`.

---

## v7.73.5 (2026-05-10) — fetch_bond_yields v1.7: reject stale FRED IMF IFS observations for JPY/NZD bond2y

### Engine — scripts/fetch_bond_yields.py
- **BUG-12 — JPY bond2y: FRED `INTGSBJPM193N` returns HTTP 200 but its last observation is `2017-05-01` (0.04%).** IMF discontinued this series for Japan ~2017. The v1.6 guard only checked the value range (`-5 < val < 20`), so 0.04% passed and was written to `JPY.json`, producing a nonsensical yield-curve calculation in the dashboard. Fixed: added `_is_stale(date_str, max_months=18)` helper that rejects any observation older than 18 months from today. The series is now treated the same as a 404 — field remains `None` (OPTIONAL soft failure, no `exit(1)`).
- **IMPROVE-3 — NZD bond2y: applied the same staleness guard to `INTGSBNZM193N`** as a precautionary measure. The series returned 404 in the v1.6 run, but if it ever returns a discontinued-series tail observation, it will be rejected rather than written.
- **New helper `_is_stale(date_str, max_months=18)`:** computes `datetime.utcnow() - timedelta(days=max_months * 31)` and returns `True` if the observation predates that cutoff. Used for OPTIONAL monthly series from FRED where series retirement results in a stale last value rather than a 404. Not applied to REQUIRED daily series (DGS*, BOE SDIE, ECB SDMX) which would surface genuine staleness via the existing soft-failure threshold.
- Bumped internal version string to `v1.7`.

---

## v7.73.4 (2026-05-10) — fetch_bond_yields v1.6: add FRED IMF IFS sources for JPY and NZD bond2y

### Engine — scripts/fetch_bond_yields.py
- **IMPROVE-1 — JPY bond2y: added FRED `INTGSBJPM193N` as primary source.** Series belongs to the IMF International Financial Statistics (IFS) Government Securities Yields family, already confirmed working for EUR (`INTGSBEAM193N`) and GBP (`INTGSBGBM193N`). Monthly frequency. Falls back to `None` (soft optional failure, no `exit(1)`) if the series is absent or returns 404.
- **IMPROVE-2 — NZD bond2y: added FRED `INTGSBNZM193N` as primary source.** Same IMF IFS pattern as IMPROVE-1. RBNZ remains blocked by Cloudflare from GH Actions runners; this FRED series is the only viable path for NZD 2Y without a country-specific API. Falls back to `None` if unavailable.
- Bumped internal version string to `v1.6`. Updated module docstring source cascade table.

---

## v7.73.3 (2026-05-10) — fetch_bond_yields v1.5: correct sources for JPY/AUD/NZD bond2y + fix exit policy

### Engine — scripts/fetch_bond_yields.py
- **BUG-10 — JPY/AUD/NZD bond2y: ECB FM series JP2YT_RR, AU2YT_RR, NZ2YT_RR return HTTP 404 from the GH Actions runner.** These series do not exist in the ECB Financial Markets dataflow — ECB FM carries non-EU sovereign data for 10Y benchmarks only. The v1.4 sources were incorrect. Resolution per currency:
  - **JPY bond2y** — no accessible public source from GH Actions IPs: MOF Japan CSV returns 403 from runner, ECB FM series absent, FRED `IRLTST01JPM156N` retired, yfinance has no JGB 2Y ticker. Field reclassified as **OPTIONAL** — existing cached value preserved; `None` accepted without triggering `exit(1)`.
  - **AUD bond2y** — Australia does not issue a 2-year government bond benchmark. The RBA's shortest benchmark is the 3-year ACGB. `None` is the correct/expected value for this field. Field reclassified as **OPTIONAL**.
  - **NZD bond2y** — NZ does issue 2Y bonds, but RBNZ is behind Cloudflare (403 from runner), ECB FM has no `NZ2YT_RR` series, and FRED carries no NZD 2Y series. Field reclassified as **OPTIONAL** — existing cached value preserved; `None` accepted.
  - **CAD bond2y** — BoC Valet `BD.CDN.2YR.DQ.YLD` confirmed working from GH Actions runner (HTTP 200, 2.94% on 2026-05-10). Remains **REQUIRED**.
- **BUG-11 — Exit policy over-sensitive:** The `≥2 soft failures → exit(1)` guard used a single counter for all fields. With JPY/AUD/NZD bond2y now confirmed as having no accessible sources, 3 expected-None fields were reliably triggering the degraded-run exit on every run. Fixed: separate `req_failures` (REQUIRED: USD/EUR/GBP bonds, CAD bond2y) and `opt_failures` (OPTIONAL: JPY/AUD/NZD bond2y) counters. Only `≥2 req_failures → exit(1)`. Optional failures emit `::notice::` (not `::warning::`) to avoid noise in the GH Actions summary.
- Bumped internal version string to `v1.5`. Updated module docstring with full source cascade table and revised exit policy documentation.

---

## v7.73.2 (2026-05-10) — fetch_bond_yields v1.4: fix wrong/retired FRED series for JPY/CAD/AUD/NZD 2Y

### Engine — scripts/fetch_bond_yields.py
- **BUG-7 — JPY bond2y: FRED IRLTST01JPM156N returns HTTP 404.** Series retired from FRED. `IRLTST` = OECD short-term 3M T-bill, not 2Y government bond — wrong series conceptually and unavailable. Replaced with ECB FM SDMX `M.JP.JPY.4F.BB.JP2YT_RR.YLDA` (period average) + `M.JP.JPY.4F.BB.JP2YT_RR.YLD` (end-of-period fallback) — same endpoint family as the working JPY bond10y series.
- **BUG-8 — CAD bond2y: FRED IRLTST01CAM156N returns HTTP 404.** Same retirement issue as BUG-7. Replaced with Bank of Canada Valet API `BD.CDN.2YR.DQ.YLD` (daily, no key — same source the old `update_extended_data.py` v13.0 used for CAD bond10y). FRED `IRLTLT01CAM156N` (monthly 10Y — imprecise but available) added as fallback.
- **BUG-9 — AUD and NZD bond2y: FRED IRLTLT01AUM156N / IRLTLT01NZM156N are the 10Y long-term series**, not 2Y. Both fields would have been identical to bond10y. Replaced with ECB FM SDMX `M.AU.AUD.4F.BB.AU2YT_RR.YLDA` (AUD) and `M.NZ.NZD.4F.BB.NZ2YT_RR.YLDA` (NZD). Both use period-average → end-of-period cascade.
- Added `_boc_valet_latest()` helper (mirrors `boc_latest()` in old `update_extended_data.py` v13.0).
- Bumped internal version string to `v1.4`. Updated docstring cascade table and CHANGELOG block.

---

## v7.73.1 (2026-05-10) — Fix sovereign spreads 2Y: JPY bond2y source + AUD/CAD/NZD commit gap

### Engine — scripts/fetch_bond_yields.py
- **BUG-5 — JPY bond2y never fetched:** `fetch_jpy()` only produced `bond10y`; `bond2y` was always `None` in `extended-data/JPY.json`, causing the Sovereign Spreads table to show `—` in the 2Y column for JP. Fixed: added `FRED IRLTST01JPM156N` (Japan 2Y government bond yield, monthly) as the JPY `bond2y` source — consistent with AUD/CAD/NZD treatment. Soft-failure path annotates GitHub Actions with `::warning::` and falls back to cached value.
- **BUG-6 — AUD/CAD/NZD bond2y discarded:** `fetch_aud_2y`, `fetch_cad_2y`, `fetch_nzd_2y` computed values correctly but the workflow `git add` only staged USD/EUR/GBP/JPY.json; AUD/CAD/NZD writes were discarded on every run. Fixed in `update-bond-yields.yml` (see below).
- Bumped internal version string to `v1.3`. Updated module docstring to document BUG-5/BUG-6 fixes and the new JPY `bond2y` source in the cascade table.

### Engine — .github/workflows/update-bond-yields.yml
- **BUG-6 fix:** Expanded `git add` to include `extended-data/AUD.json`, `extended-data/CAD.json`, `extended-data/NZD.json`. These files were already being written to disk by the script but never staged, so the 2Y values for AU/CA/NZ never reached the public repo.
- Updated workflow header comment to document `bond2y` output for EUR/GBP/JPY/AUD/CAD/NZD.

## v7.73.0 (2026-05-10) — Economic Surprises live data; cross pairs in CIP Forwards; UI label polish; G8 bond2y pipeline

### Frontend — assets/dashboard2.js
- **`renderEconSurprises()` calendar fallback:** When `ff_calendar.json` has no released events with actuals (happens weekly at week start since ForexFactory only provides live-week data), the function now falls back to `calendar.json` which carries historical actuals. Schema differences are normalised at load time. Source footer updates dynamically to reflect which dataset is active and its coverage date.
- **`CIP_CCY_RATES` + `renderDerivativesSection()` — Cross pairs in Implied Forwards:** Extended `CIP_CCY_RATES` with EUR/GBP, EUR/JPY, GBP/JPY, AUD/JPY, EUR/AUD, EUR/CHF. All have CB rates available (ECB, BOE, BOJ, RBA, SNB) in `rates/*.json`. Added corresponding rows to `fwd-tbody` with a visual separator after major pairs. Changed JS row lookup from index-based to `data-pair` attribute query to handle the separator row correctly.
- **`renderG8YieldPane()` label fix:** Removed internal filename (`extended-data/EUR.json`) from the visible pane header and source footer. Header now reads `{Country} · Sovereign Bond Yields`; footer reads `Daily sovereign yield pipeline · {date}`.
- **`renderSovereignSpreads()` — country names:** Changed Country column labels from two-letter codes (DE, GB) to full country names (Germany, UK, Japan, Australia, Canada, New Zealand). Emoji flags were not used as they render as letter pairs in most web fonts.

### Frontend — index2.html
- **Rates & Yield Curve `panel-sub`:** Changed from `Nominal yields · yfinance · extended-data` to `Nominal yields · yfinance · daily pipeline` — removes raw directory reference from visible UI.
- **Economic Surprises footer:** Added `id="econ-surprise-source"` so JS can update the label dynamically. Default text changed to `ForexFactory · calendar beat/miss vs consensus · 21d rolling`.
- **Derivatives — Cross pairs in `fwd-tbody`:** Added 6 cross pair rows (EUR/GBP, EUR/JPY, GBP/JPY, AUD/JPY, EUR/AUD, EUR/CHF) with a separator after NZD/USD. Added `data-pair` attributes to all rows for robust JS lookup.

### Engine — scripts/update_extended_data.py
- **Bond 2Y pipeline extended to G8:** Added EUR 2Y via ECB SDMX (`YC/B.U2.EUR.4F.G_N_A.SV_C_YM.SR_2Y`, daily), GBP 2Y via FRED `IRLTST01GBM156N` (monthly), CAD 2Y via FRED `IRLTST01CAM156N` (monthly). Enables yield curve slope (10Y−2Y) display for DE, GB, CA tabs and Sovereign Spreads panel.

## v7.72.2 (2026-05-10) — Derivatives restore: yield curve redraw + G8 pane repaint; remove brand names from UI

### Frontend — assets/dashboard2.js
- **`hideDerivatives()` canvas fix:** After restoring sibling visibility, a `requestAnimationFrame` callback redraws the yield curve canvas using cached `_lastDrawnYields` / `_lastDrawnPrior`. Without this, `wrap.clientWidth` was 0 at draw time (panel was hidden) and the canvas stayed blank after restore.
- **`hideDerivatives()` G8 pane repaint:** After the rAF, the currently active Rates tab is re-triggered. If it is a G8 country tab, `dataset.loaded` is cleared and `renderG8YieldPane()` is called so the pane re-renders with correct dimensions. If it is the Spreads tab, `renderSovereignSpreads()` is called. US tab requires no action (its content is static HTML populated on page load).
- **"Bloomberg BVOL" removed from ATM IV tooltip body:** Replaced with "Institutional benchmark for FX options pricing" — retains the technical description without brand reference in user-visible text.

### Frontend — index2.html
- **"Bloomberg" removed from two user-visible strings:** Trend column `title` attribute and HV term table footnote now read "HV 10d vs HV 30d" without brand attribution. The methodology description is accurate on its own.

---

 — Vol Trend arrow: Bloomberg HV 10d vs HV 30d convention

### Engine — scripts/fetch_intraday_quotes.py
- **`compute_hv10()` added:** Identical methodology to `compute_hv30()` (annualized std of log-returns, sample variance n−1, ×√252) using the last 12 closes → 11 returns. Requires ≥12 data points. The 3-month history already fetched by `fetch_hv30_fx()` is reused at zero additional cost.
- **`fetch_hv30_fx()` updated:** Now computes `hv10` alongside `hv30` for each pair and includes it in the result dict. All three result dict assignments (success, insufficient-data, and exception paths) updated.
- **PASO 5 injection updated:** `hv10` is now injected into each pair's `quotes[pair_id]` entry and printed in the run log. No schema change to the top-level `hv30` map (unchanged for backward compatibility).

### Frontend — index2.html
- **Trend column added to HV term table:** 5th column header "Trend" with tooltip explaining the Bloomberg HV 10d vs HV 30d convention. Skeleton rows updated to 5 cells per row (was 4).
- **Footnote updated:** Now reads "Trend = HV 10d vs HV 30d (Bloomberg)".

### Frontend — assets/dashboard2.js
- **`td[4]` Trend rendering added to `renderDerivativesSection()`:** Reads `q?.hv10` from `intraday.quotes`. Logic: HV10 − HV30 > +1pp → ↑ red (vol expanding), < −1pp → ↓ green (contracting), within 1pp → → grey (stable). The 1pp neutral band matches Bloomberg's rounding tolerance. Each cell carries a `title` tooltip with the raw HV10/HV30 values.
- **Dead `tds[6]` block removed:** Stale code that wrote RR 1M into a non-existent 7th cell (leftover from the old 7-column schema) deleted.
- **`hv10` sourced from `intraday.quotes` only** (not from `STOOQ_RT_CACHE`, which does not carry HV 10d). If `hv10` is null (first pipeline run after deploy), cell shows `—` with tooltip noting it will populate on next run.

---

 — Remove redundant HV panel, fix Derivatives restore bug, add D shortcut

### Frontend — index2.html
- **Removed redundant "Realized Vol — HV (yfinance OHLC)" panel from Risk Monitor:** The panel duplicated HV 30d and RR 1M data already present in the FX Pairs table (same source: `quotes.json` + `rr.json`). The Trend column was always `—` (HV 10d not computed by pipeline). Consolidated analysis belongs in the Derivatives section ("Realized Vol · HV 30d vs RR Skew"), which adds the RR/HV ratio — a distinct signal not in the FX table. Removing avoids displaying the same data twice, per institutional terminal standards.
- **Derivatives nav link normalized:** Removed inline `style="color:var(--blue);font-weight:600;"` and `★` suffix. Derivatives now renders identically to all other nav links. Added `· Shortcut: D` to the `title` attribute for discoverability.

### Frontend — assets/dashboard2.js
- **`hideDerivatives()` restore bug fixed:** Previously, `el.style.display = ''` removed the inline style on all hidden siblings, which reverted them to the browser's CSS default (`block`). The Cross-Asset + Risk wrapper div used an inline `display:grid` — restoring to `block` broke the 2-column grid layout. Fix: `showDerivatives()` now stores each sibling's original inline `display` value in `data-deriv-hidden`; `hideDerivatives()` restores it exactly.
- **`renderDerivativesSection()` guaranteed RR data:** Added a direct `fetch('./rr-data/rr.json')` fallback at the top of the function (same pattern as `renderRRInFXTable`). Previously the function used `window.RR_DATA_CACHE || {}` with no fallback, so if the user opened Derivatives before the 8s polling loop completed, RR 1M and RR/HV columns showed `—`. Now populates `RR_DATA_CACHE` on first open regardless of timing.
- **`D` keyboard shortcut for Derivatives:** Added `d: 'section-derivatives'` to `NAV_KEYS`. `navTo()` has a dedicated branch for Derivatives that calls `window._derivNavShow/Hide` (the custom toggle) instead of a `.click()` on the nav link — `D` acts as a toggle (press again to return to normal view). `initDerivativesNavFixed()` now exposes `_derivNavShow`, `_derivNavHide`, and `_derivNavSection` on `window` for the keyboard handler.
- **Keyboard shortcut legend updated:** Added `D · Derivatives (toggle)` entry.
- **`renderHVMultiWindow()` removed:** Function deleted; the panel it populated (`#hv-multitf-tbody`) no longer exists. Removed its calls from the boot `run()` function and from the 5-minute refresh interval.

---



### Frontend — index2.html
- **Risk Monitor restructured to match original layout:** Removed the 2-column side-by-side grid (HV table left / Indicators right) introduced in v7.71.3. Risk Indicators are now full width inside `#section-risk`, below the VIX/MOVE/HV30d/Regime grid. The Realized Vol HV table is now a standalone full-width block placed directly above the Historical VaR table (same width), matching the layout visible in image 2 of the session.
