## v7.71.4 (2026-05-10) — Fwd color fix, HV term structure rebuilt, collapse animation fix

### Frontend — assets/dashboard2.js (index2.html)
- **Fwd 1M/3M color logic corrected (FX table):** `CIP_CCY_RATES` always stores `base='USD'` for all pairs. The previous `isUsdBase` inversion made `atPremium = diff < 0`, causing EUR/USD (and all pairs) to show **red** when the forward is above spot — the opposite of the Derivatives table. Fix: removed `isUsdBase` branch entirely. For all pairs `diff > 0` (fwd > spot) = left-hand currency at forward premium = green. This is now consistent with the Derivatives CIP table and with standard FX convention.
- **HV Term Structure JS renderer rewritten:** New 4-column schema — `td[0]` Pair, `td[1]` HV 30d, `td[2]` RR 1M, `td[3]` RR/HV ratio. Covers all 7 pairs (EUR/USD through NZD/USD). HV 30d colored by regime (green <5%, red >12%). RR 1M colored by direction. RR/HV ratio shows skew premium relative to realized vol (positive = calls/puts expensive vs realized).
- **Collapse animation fixed:** Collapsing an FX pair detail row was a jump-cut because `maxHeight` was set to `'none'` after open (for content that exceeds 185px) — CSS `transition` cannot animate from `none` to `0`. Fix: on collapse, first snapshot `scrollHeight` into `maxHeight`, set `overflow: hidden`, then on the next animation frame set `maxHeight = 0` to trigger a smooth CSS transition.

### Frontend — index2.html
- **HV Term Structure table rebuilt:** Removed 5 empty columns (HV 10d, HV 21d, HV 60d, Vol Trend, RR-HV). New 4-column table: Pair | HV 30d | RR 1M | RR / HV. Panel label updated to "Realized Vol · yfinance OHLC · HV 30d vs RR Skew".

---

## v7.71.3 (2026-05-09) — FX table row fix, Derivatives nav, Risk Monitor layout, Fwd color coding

### Frontend — assets/dashboard2.js (index2.html)
- **FX row template: 3 missing cells added:** `renderFXPairsTable()` was generating 9 `<td>` elements per row (0–8: Pair, Bid, Ask, Spread, 1D, 1W, HV30, SessH, SessL) but the table header has 12 columns and `renderCIPForwards()` writes to tds[7]/[8] (Fwd 1M/3M), `renderRRInFXTable()` to tds[9] (RR 1M), and the RT updater to tds[10]/[11] (Sess H/L). All three new columns were written into the SessH/L cells, overwriting session data and leaving RR 1M and Fwd values invisible. Now the row template emits 12 cells with placeholder `—` for Fwd 1M/3M and RR 1M.
- **`td.colSpan = 9` → `12`:** The inline expand row now spans all 12 columns, reaching the right edge of the FX Pairs table.
- **`fetchOptionSkew` crash fixed:** `tbody.closest('table')` was called unconditionally when `tbody` (the removed `#skew-tbody`) is `null`, causing a `TypeError: Cannot read properties of null (reading 'closest')` that killed the entire function before `RR_DATA_CACHE` was populated. Now null-guarded: `tbody ? tbody.closest(…) : null`. Sibling `tbody.innerHTML` also null-guarded.
- **Fwd 1M/3M color coding (FX table):** CIP forward cells were always `var(--text2)` (neutral). Now green (`var(--up)`) when base currency is at a forward premium, red (`var(--down)`) when at discount. USD-base pairs (USD/JPY, USD/CHF, USD/CAD) use inverted logic.
- **Fwd 1M/3M/6M/1Y color coding (Derivatives CIP table):** Same direction-vs-spot color logic applied to the 4-tenor forward table in the Derivatives section.
- **`initDerivativesNavFixed()` rewritten:** Previous approach manually toggled `display:none` on 5 specific sibling sections, which left sections like `#section-econmap` visible and broke the restore path (returning from Derivatives left the layout blank). Now: on Derivatives click, all children of `#split-lower-right` except `#section-derivatives` are hidden via `data-deriv-hidden` attribute; the Derivatives section is shown and `#split-lower` scrolls to top. On any other nav click, `#section-derivatives` is hidden and siblings are restored. Derivatives nav link uses capture phase (`addEventListener('click', fn, true)`) to run before the main nav scroll handler.
- **`renderHVMultiWindow()` simplified:** Removed HV 10d, HV 60d columns — `quotes.json` only provides `hv30`. Table now shows Pair | HV 30d | RR 1M | Trend (with Trend as `—` since HV10 is unavailable for comparison).
- **HV Term Structure (Derivatives) simplified:** Same — HV 10d, HV 21d, HV 60d cells now show `—` with tooltip "Not computed by pipeline". HV 30d cell colored by regime (green <5%, red >12%, neutral in between).

### Frontend — index2.html
- **Risk Monitor layout restructured:** HV multi-window table and Risk Indicators table are now side-by-side in a 2-column CSS grid, instead of stacked vertically. Reduces vertical length of the Cross-Asset + Risk section. HV table header updated to 4 columns (Pair, HV 30d, RR 1M, Trend).

---

## v7.71.2 (2026-05-09) — Fix fetch_saxo_rr2.py parsing + YML cp guard

### Engine — scripts/fetch_saxo_rr2.py
- **Parsing strategy replaced:** v1.0 searched for `"RISK REVERSAL"` inside table cell text, which never matched (the heading is outside the table). Now uses the same 3-strategy approach as the working `fetch_saxo_rr.py`: (1) find heading text matching `RISK REVERSAL` and take next sibling table, (2) fallback to second table on page (ATM is first, RR is second on Saxo's layout), (3) last-resort first table. This matches confirmed Saxo page structure.
- **`parse_rr_value` simplified:** replaced `re.match` (anchored) with `re.search` to match values anywhere in the cell string — identical to the working script.
- **`float | None` return type annotation removed:** Python 3.9 compatibility (GitHub Actions ubuntu-latest ships 3.10 but the `X | Y` union syntax requires 3.10+; using explicit `Optional` avoids any edge-case issues).

### Engine — .github/workflows/update-saxo-rr2.yml
- **`cp` guard added:** `if [ ! -f rr-data/rr2.json ]; then exit 0; fi` runs before `cp rr-data/rr2.json /tmp/rr2_fresh.json`. On the first-ever run when scraping fails and no file exists yet, the workflow now exits cleanly instead of failing with `cp: cannot stat 'rr-data/rr2.json': No such file or directory`.

---

## v7.71.1 (2026-05-09) — Bug fixes: CB rates cache, RR column, econ surprises, skew-tbody decoupling

### Frontend — assets/dashboard2.js (index2.html)
- **`loadCBRatesCache` fixed:** `rates/*.json` uses `observations[0].value` (string), not a top-level `rate` or `value` field. The previous read produced `null` for all currencies, leaving `_CB_RATES_CACHE` empty and causing all CIP forward columns to show `—`. Now correctly reads the most recent observation.
- **`renderRRInFXTable` hardened:** now fetches `rr-data/rr.json` directly as a fallback when `RR_DATA_CACHE` is empty (e.g. if `fetchOptionSkew` has not yet completed). Ensures RR 1M column populates on first load.
- **`fetchOptionSkew` decoupled from `skew-tbody`:** Positioning Bias panel was removed in v7.71.0 but `fetchOptionSkew` bailed early with `if (!tbody) return`, preventing `RR_DATA_CACHE` from being populated. Now continues past a missing `skew-tbody` — all row writes are null-guarded.
- **`renderEconSurprises` field names corrected:** `ff_calendar.json` uses `dateISO` (not `date`) and `title` (not `event`). Also added forward-looking fallback: when no released events exist, shows count of medium/high-impact events due in next 7 days with `Watch` bias label.

### Engine — .github/workflows/update-saxo-rr2.yml
- **New workflow added:** runs `fetch_saxo_rr2.py` (multi-tenor RR scraper) Mon–Fri at 09:00 UTC — 30 minutes after the existing `update-saxo-rr.yml` (08:30 UTC) to avoid concurrent site pushes. Writes `rr-data/rr2.json` consumed by the Derivatives section.

---

## v7.70.3 (2026-05-09) — Workspace (index2.html) discontinued and removed

### Frontend — index2.html
- **File removed from repo.** The tile-based workspace experiment (introduced in v7.70.0, iterated through v7.70.2) was discontinued after evaluation: no tile layout system — whether absolute-positioned or CSS Grid — could match the visual density, precision, and aesthetic consistency of the fixed-column terminal (`index.html`). The paradigms are fundamentally different; a free-form tile canvas requires trade-offs in spacing, alignment, and data legibility that are incompatible with an institutional-grade terminal benchmark.
- **Standard tab removed from navigation.** The "Workspace BETA" tab and all references to `index2.html` should be removed from `index.html` if present.
- **Future direction (if revisited):** A layout-switching feature within `index.html` itself — swapping between fixed column presets without a separate page — is the more viable path. This preserves the native panel structure, CSS variables, and data wiring while allowing user-configurable views, closer to how Bloomberg handles layout switching.

---

## v7.70.2 (2026-05-09) — Workspace: rewrite layout engine from absolute-px to CSS Grid

### Frontend — index2.html
- **Root cause fixed:** The absolute-positioning tile engine (`position:absolute; left:Xpx; top:Ypx; width:Wpx; height:Hpx`) cannot fill the viewport because it requires manually computed pixel coordinates that match the browser's actual viewport size. Any screen smaller than the template's design width leaves empty canvas. This is architecturally broken for a responsive terminal.
- **CSS Grid engine replaces absolute positioning:** `#ws-grid` is now `display:grid; grid-template-columns:repeat(12,1fr); gap:1px; background:var(--border)`. Tiles use `style.gridColumn = 'span N'` (1–12). The grid always fills 100% of available width with zero gaps — exactly as in the original mockup.
- **`#ws-shell` direction:** changed from `flex-direction:row` (broken — was trying to do columns manually) to `flex-direction:column` so the toolbar sits above the canvas which fills the remaining height.
- **`#ws-canvas`:** changed from `position:relative; overflow:hidden` to `overflow-y:auto` (scrollable, no clipping).
- **`#ws-grid`:** `min-height:100%` ensures the grid always fills the canvas height.
- **Tile CSS rewritten:** `.ws-tile` is now a simple flex-column block element — no `position:absolute`, no `will-change:transform`, no resize handle. Height is determined by content (natural grid row height).
- **Drag-and-drop rewritten:** HTML5 `dragstart/dragend/dragover/drop` swap-reorder system (same as original mockup). No more mouse-pixel tracking. Tiles swap DOM position in the grid on drop.
- **`LAYOUT_TEMPLATES` rewritten:** all pixel coordinates replaced with `{ id, span, rows }` objects. Terminal layout: 4 rows × 12 columns, with the liquidity and cbrates tiles spanning 2 rows. Focus/Grid/Macro layouts updated accordingly.
- **`saveLayout` / `loadSavedLayout`:** updated to serialize `span` and `rows` per tile (localStorage key `gi-workspace-layout-v2`). Old `v1` keys are ignored.
- **`addFromCatalog`:** newly added tiles default to `span:4` (3-up per row).
- **No `ws-main-col` wrapper:** removed, was an artifact of the old fixed-column sidebar approach.
- **`expanded` class:** tile expand now uses `position:fixed; inset:0; z-index:800` overlay — works correctly with grid-based tiles.
- **`reconnectDataSources`:** unchanged from v7.70.1 (polls for `window.boot` before calling data functions).

---

## v7.70.1 (2026-05-09) — Workspace Terminal layout: eliminate gaps, fix data loading

### Frontend — index2.html
- **Terminal layout coordinates rewritten:** All 4 columns now sum to exactly 756px height (col 0: 180+330+246; col 1/2: 96+290+180+190; col 3: 226+284+246). Zero pixel gaps between any adjacent tiles.
- **Column widths adjusted:** col0=160, col1=376 (x=160), col2=280 (x=536), col3=212 (x=816). Total template width=1028px — fits standard 1080p browser at 100% zoom with 8px side gutter.
- **`positioningbias` tile removed from Terminal default** to achieve clean 3-row right column. Still available in panel catalog.
- **`news` and `signals` tiles removed from Terminal default** (added to col1/col2 caused column height mismatch). Both remain in catalog for manual addition.
- **`overflow:hidden` → `overflow:auto`** on `#ws-canvas` so users can scroll to any tile in tall layouts.
- **`--tile-min-w` / `--tile-min-h`** lowered from `280px/180px` to `120px/90px` so narrow sidebar tiles (liquidity, crosses, carry at w=160) are not resized above their template width.
- **`applyLayout` scaling:** switched from `Math.round` to `Math.floor` for x/w calculations to prevent sub-pixel rendering gaps. Added 8px left gutter (`x + 8`) for breathing room.
- **`reconnectDataSources` rewritten:** polls `typeof window.boot` every 100ms until dashboard.js is loaded, then calls all fetch functions. Eliminates the race condition where the 150ms timeout fired before the deferred script executed on slow connections.
- **`window._wsLayoutReady = true`** flag set after `applyLayout` in `DOMContentLoaded` — available for future dashboard.js detection of workspace context.

---

## v7.70.0 (2026-05-09) — Workspace (index2): convert fixed sidebar/rightpanel to draggable tiles

### Frontend — index2.html
- **Removed fixed `#ws-sidebar` and `#ws-rightpanel` columns** from the `#ws-shell` flex layout. Both were rendering as immovable columns, defeating the tile-workspace premise.
- **Added 7 new tile content builders** (`liquidity`, `crosses`, `carry`, `cbrates`, `correlations`, `expectations`, `positioningbias`) with the exact DOM IDs that `dashboard.js` targets (`cbrates-tbody`, `correlations-tbody`, `fed-exp-tbody`, `skew-tbody`, `carry-rank-rows`, `liquidity-canvas`, `sb-*` cross pair IDs). All data from `dashboard.js` now flows into the correct tiles instead of invisible off-canvas panels.
- **Expanded `TILE_CATALOG`** from 15 to 22 entries to include all new tile types with accurate source labels, descriptions, and tags.
- **Rebuilt `terminal` layout template** to mirror the standard index.html layout with 4 columns of tiles: Liquidity/Crosses/Carry (left), Narrative/Chart/FX Pairs/COT/News (centre-left), Risk/Cross-Asset/Yields/Sessions/Signals (centre-right), CB Rates/Correlations/Expectations/Positioning Bias (right).
- **Rewrote `reconnectDataSources()`** to call all dashboard.js render functions by name (`populateFxPairsTable`, `populateCorrelations`, `fetchCBRates`, `fetchFedExpectations`, `fetchOptionSkew`, `fetchCarryRanking`, `fetchCarryData`, `computeSessionVol`, etc.) — previously only 7 functions were invoked, leaving most panels empty after layout switches.
- **Fixed `#ws-shell` height** to account for the workspace toolbar row (30px) in addition to topbar/quotebar/newsticker.
- **Updated sidebar CSS selectors** from `#ws-sidebar .sb-*` to `.tile-body .sb-*` so cross pair rows and carry rows render correctly inside tile bodies.

---



### Deployment — netlify.toml
- **Removed `'unsafe-inline'` from `script-src`:** Eliminated the `'unsafe-inline'` directive that violated the GUIDELINES CSP rule and was overriding the correct `_headers` policy on Netlify. `style-src` retains `'unsafe-inline'` (required for TradingView widget inline styles).
- **Synced `connect-src` with `_headers`:** Added `https://fred.stlouisfed.org` and `https://analytics.google.com` (present in `_headers` but missing from `netlify.toml`). Removed `https://api.frankfurter.app` (removed from `_headers` in a prior release, now consistent across both files).

### Frontend — index.html
- **Cache-busting params updated:** `assets/dashboard.css?v=7.52.0` → `v=7.69.1` and `assets/dashboard.js?v=7.60.0` → `v=7.69.1`. Stale version params caused browsers with cached assets to continue serving outdated files until TTL expiry.

### Data — meetings-data/meetings.json
- **JPY `biasSource` corrected:** Field contained corrupted text `"SNB zimoma TONA (zimoma cube · monthly)…"` — the internal script source label leaked into the public-facing JSON field. Replaced with the correct human-readable label: `"BoJ TONA OIS (QUICK Money World · monthly) + BoJ forward supplement (~40bp when hold zone) vs BoJ rate (rates/JPY.json)"`.

### Engine — .github/workflows/monitor-data-health.yml
- **Translated all Spanish comments and user-facing strings to English:** Affected lines: header FIX-12 comment, schedule cron comments, FIX-11 inline comments, email subjects ("Datos faltantes" → "Missing data", "Resumen semanal" → "Weekly digest"), email body copy, step name ("lunes" → "Monday"), and the email config note. Functional logic unchanged.

### Engine — .github/workflows/update-rates.yml
- **Translated all Spanish comments to English:** Affected lines: D-01 FIX concurrency comment and the D-01 FIX validation block (5 lines describing exit-code logic, plausibility range, and health.json behaviour). Functional logic unchanged.

### GUIDELINES.md
- **Version footer bumped:** `v7.60.1` → `v7.69.2` to reflect current release.

## v7.69.0 (2026-05-09) — SEO & retention: rewrite meta titles, add FAQ item, fix ghost-page redirects, RSS subscribe link

### Guides — guide-gbpjpy-cot.html
- **Title updated:** Changed from "GBP/JPY Bias Factors: COT Positioning, Rate Differential & Risk Regime — Global Investing" to "GBP/JPY Bias Factors: Live COT Data, Rate Differential & Risk Regime". Rationale: the page ranks at pos 3.27 with 1,463 impressions but only 0.07% CTR. Adding "Live" signals current data; removing the brand suffix recovers character budget for the key terms Google truncates first.
- **Meta description updated:** Now leads with "Current GBP/JPY directional bias — updated weekly from CFTC COT" to answer the search intent directly and signal freshness.
- **FAQ schema — new item added:** "What could change GBP/JPY bias right now?" — the 3rd-highest query (162 impressions, pos 2.28, 0 clicks) was not represented as an explicit FAQPage item despite the section existing in the page body. The new schema item increases rich-snippet eligibility for this query.

### Frontend — index.html
- **Title updated:** Changed from "Global Investing FX Terminal | Professional Forex Dashboard" to "FX Terminal — Live G8 Rates, COT Positioning & Market Snapshot". Rationale: the US market (3,308 impressions, 0 clicks) searches for "current market snapshot usd eur gbp jpy" and similar live-data terms. "Professional Forex Dashboard" does not answer that intent.
- **Meta description updated:** Now lists the exact currencies and data types (G8 rates, COT, yield curve) matching high-impression US queries.
- **RSS Subscribe link added in Narrative panel:** Inline "Subscribe" button next to the Narrative label triggers the existing RSS popover (`toggleRssPopover()`). This surfaces the feed subscription to all users without requiring them to find the statusbar button.

### Deployment — netlify.toml
- **Ghost-page 301 redirects added:** `/index.html` → `/` (resolves duplicate crawl budget between `index.html` and root `/`); `/news.html` → `/`; `/en.html` → `/`; `/guia-cot.html` → `/guide-cot.html`; `/tecnico-vs-fundamental.html` → `/guide-dashboard.html`. These 5 ghost pages had accumulated 321 impressions in GSC while the actual pages don't exist — Netlify-level 301s will pass link equity and stop crawl budget waste faster than the existing 404.html JS redirect fallback.

---

## v7.68.5 (2026-05-09) — Fix RSS popover text overflow (mono font + missing word-wrap)

### Frontend — assets/feed.js
- **Root cause:** The popover container used `font-family:var(--font-mono)` for all its content. Monospaced fonts don't trigger automatic word-wrap in browsers the same way proportional fonts do, causing the prose paragraphs ("AI market narrative… updated at each major session transition" and "Paste either URL into Feedly…") to overflow the right edge of the card.
- **Fix 1 — container font switched to UI font:** Changed container `font-family` to `var(--font-ui,system-ui,sans-serif)`. Mono is correct for file paths and code; it is wrong for prose descriptions.
- **Fix 2 — mono applied explicitly to URL links only:** Added `font-family:var(--font-mono,monospace)` inline to both `<a>` link elements (`RSS 2.0 — feed.xml`, `JSON Feed 1.1 — feed.json`) so they retain the terminal aesthetic.
- **Fix 3 — explicit wrap rules on paragraphs:** Added `white-space:normal;word-wrap:break-word` to both `<p>` elements as a defensive belt-and-suspenders measure against any inherited `white-space:nowrap` from ancestor elements in the terminal layout.
- **Fix 4 — container overflow clipped:** Added `overflow:hidden` to the container so any future content can never escape the rounded card boundary.

---

## v7.68.4 (2026-05-09) — Fix RSS popover overflow + feed.xml/feed.json 404

### Frontend — assets/feed.js
- **Popover horizontal overflow:** The popover was clipping outside the left viewport edge on narrow screens. Added `max-width:calc(100vw - 16px)` and `box-sizing:border-box` to the popover `cssText` so the 260px card can shrink to fit and padding is included within the declared width. The `right` anchor from `getBoundingClientRect()` remains unchanged.

### Engine — .github/workflows/generate-ai-narrative.yml
- **Root cause of 404:** `generate_narrative_signals.py` calls `write_feeds()` which writes `feed.xml` and `feed.json` to the `site/` working directory. However, the commit step only `git add`-ed files under `ai-analysis/` — `feed.xml` and `feed.json` were never staged, so they were never pushed to the public repo and always returned 404.
- **Fix:** Added `[ -f feed.xml ] && cp feed.xml /tmp/ai_analysis_fresh/` and the equivalent for `feed.json` to the pre-loop stash block, and `[ -f /tmp/ai_analysis_fresh/feed.xml ] && cp ... .` restores inside the retry loop. Added `git add feed.xml feed.json 2>/dev/null || true` after the existing `ai-analysis/` adds. The `|| true` guard preserves the existing behavior for all other files if either feed file is absent (e.g. skipped runs where narrative was unchanged). Feed files will now be committed on the next narrative run.

---

## v7.68.3 (2026-05-09) — Fix RSS popover not appearing on click

### Frontend — assets/feed.js
- **Root cause:** The RSS popover used `position:absolute` with `bottom:calc(100%+8px)`, anchored inside `#rss-anchor`. Its closest scrolling/clipping ancestor is the wrapper `<div>` that is a direct child of `<footer id="statusbar">`. That div inherits the CSS rule `#statusbar > div:not(.footer-copy) { overflow:hidden }`, which clips any absolutely-positioned content that extends beyond the div's bounds. The popover was opening correctly in the DOM (`display:block`, content rendered) but rendered invisible because it was clipped by `overflow:hidden` before reaching the screen. The button was functional — the popover was simply never visible.
- **Fix — `position:fixed` with `getBoundingClientRect()` positioning:** Added `positionPopover()` helper that reads the button's viewport rect via `getBoundingClientRect()` and sets `bottom` / `right` as fixed-viewport coordinates. `buildPopover()` now sets `position:fixed` (instead of `absolute`) and calls `positionPopover()` immediately after. `toggleRssPopover()` also calls `positionPopover()` on every open so the popover tracks the button correctly after any window resize between opens. This is identical to the pattern already used by `#alerts-popover` (which has `position:fixed` in `dashboard.css` and therefore works correctly).

---

## v7.68.2 (2026-05-09) — Onboarding step 3: COT panel highlight + copy fix

### Frontend — assets/onboarding.js
- **Step 3 `target` was `null`:** Steps 1 and 2 of the welcome tour set `target: 'narrative'` and `target: 'risk-regime'` respectively, activating a blue outline and scrolling the highlighted element into view. Step 3 had `target: null` — the highlight cleared when the user reached the COT/Pair Bias step, leaving no visual anchor. Fixed by setting `target: 'section-positioning'` (the `id` of the COT panel `<div>` in `index.html`). The panel now highlights and scrolls into view exactly as steps 1 and 2 do.
- **Copy fix — premature GBP/JPY guide reference removed:** Step 3 body mentioned "the same multi-factor framework used in the GBP/JPY guide" — a reference meaningless to a first-time user who has not yet seen any guide. Replaced with "the core multi-factor framework behind every macro trade setup", which communicates the same value without requiring prior context.

---

## v7.68.1 (2026-05-09) — Fix missing BTC/ETH bar after weekend run (yfinance finalization lag)

### Engine — scripts/fetch_ohlc.py
- **Root cause identified:** The weekend OHLC run (Sat/Sun 23:30 UTC) fetches BTC and ETH with an explicit start/end date range. yfinance has a known finalization lag for crypto daily 1D bars: when the run executes, the just-completed prior day's bar (e.g. 2026-05-08 / Friday) may be absent from the feed while yfinance returns only the new in-progress bar (e.g. 2026-05-09 / Saturday). The existing crypto gap-fill guard (added in a prior version) correctly detected the 2-day gap (`07/05 → 09/05`) but only emitted a warning — it did not correct the gap.
- **Fix — crypto gap-fill backfill (v1.8):** When a gap of ≥ 2 days is detected between the last two bars in the new yfinance response, `fetch_ohlc.py` now reads the existing `ohlc-data/{id}.json` file from disk and backfills any bars whose date falls within the gap but is absent from the new yfinance response. The recovered bars are merged and the list is re-sorted chronologically. New yfinance data takes priority for any date present in both sources; backfill only fills dates that are absent. If the existing JSON is missing or unreadable, the warning is emitted as before and the gap remains (degraded but not broken). The `WARN` message now says "attempting backfill" instead of "Check manually" so log monitoring can distinguish corrected from uncorrected gaps.

---

## v7.68.0 (2026-05-08) — MOVE Index chart (^MOVE via yfinance)

### Engine — scripts/fetch_ohlc.py
- **`"move": "^MOVE"` added to SYMBOLS:** ICE BofA MOVE Index fetched via yfinance `^MOVE` ticker, same native-1D path as VIX. Added to `DECIMALS` (2 dp), plausibility guard `(20.0, 400.0)` (historical range ~30–200 with headroom), and `NON_FX_SYMBOLS`.

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js
- **`STOOQ_RT_CACHE['move']` seeded:** Added the same cache-seeding block as VIX inside the `move.close > 10` branch of `renderRiskData()`. Populates `close`, `open`, `high`, `low`, `prev_close`, `chg`, `pct`, `market_state`, `market_time` from `byId.move`, then calls `_lwUpdateTodayBar()`. This enables the live today-bar on the MOVE chart to update every 5 minutes from `quotes.json`, identical to VIX behavior. `fetch_intraday_quotes.py` already fetched `^MOVE` — the cache seeding was the only missing piece.
- **`_TV_TO_OHLC`:** Added `'CBOE:MOVE': 'move'`
- **`_OHLC_LABELS`:** Added `move: '^MOVE'` for the chart source footer.
- **MOVE click handler:** Added `document.getElementById('risk-move')?.closest('.risk-cell')?.addEventListener('click', ...)` mirroring the VIX pattern, calling `loadTVChart('CBOE:MOVE')`.

### Frontend — index.html
- **MOVE risk cell:** Added `style="cursor:pointer;"` and `title="Click: chart"` to the MOVE cell, consistent with the VIX cell.

## v7.67.2 (2026-05-08) — Share button stacked above regime badge in split layout

### Frontend — index.html
- **`narr-meta` class on badge wrapper:** Added `class="narr-meta"` to the flex wrapper that contains `#narrative-regime` and `#narr-share-btn`, enabling targeted CSS override in split mode.
- **Split-layout narrative override:** Added `#main.split-layout #narrative .narr-meta { flex-direction: column-reverse; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 4px }`. In split mode the share icon now sits above the regime badge (column-reverse keeps badge below, share icon on top), and the badge is vertically centered within the narrative bar height. No change to the default (non-split) layout.

---

## v7.67.1 (2026-05-08) — Fix phantom Saturday candle on FX pairs after Friday close

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js
- **FX Friday-after-close guard:** Added a one-line guard in `_lwBuildTodayBar` that returns `null` for any FX pair when `dowUTC === 5` (Friday) and `hourUTC >= 21`. After 21:00 UTC on Friday the NY session closes and the next FX session does not open until Sunday 21:00 UTC — there is no Saturday session. Without this guard the existing `hourUTC >= 21` branch computed `tomorrow = 2026-05-09 (Saturday)` and injected a phantom doji candle dated May 9, even though FX markets are closed on the weekend. The existing `dowUTC === 0 || dowUTC === 6` guard only blocked Saturday and Sunday visits; it did not cover the Friday-night window between 21:00 and 00:00 UTC.

---

## v7.67.0 (2026-05-08) — Share ghost button + sitemap lastmod refresh

### Frontend — assets/share.js (new file)
- **`shareNarrative()` function:** Reads `#narrative-text` and `#narrative-regime` from the DOM, builds a compact share string (`[REGIME] first sentence + URL`), and shares via the Web Share API on mobile or copies to clipboard on desktop. Includes a 2-second checkmark confirmation state on the button.

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.css
- **`.narr-share-btn` styles:** Ghost button — `opacity:0` by default, transitions to `opacity:1` on `#narrative:hover` and `#narrative:focus-within`. `pointer-events:none` when hidden so it never intercepts accidental clicks. Hover state: `border-color:var(--blue)`, `color:var(--blue)`. `.copied` confirmation state: `border-color:var(--up)`, `color:var(--up)`. Matches `kb-hint-btn` aesthetic.

### Frontend — index.html
- **Share button in narrative bar:** Added `<button class="narr-share-btn" id="narr-share-btn">` with SVG share icon, wrapped with the regime badge in a flex container. Invisible at rest, appears on hover. Calls `shareNarrative()`.
- **`share.js` script tag:** Added `<script src="assets/share.js?v=1.0.0" defer></script>` after `feed.js`.

### Frontend — sitemap.xml
- **`lastmod` refresh:** Updated all `<lastmod>` values to `2026-05-08` for index, all 7 guides, and about page. Previous value (`2026-05-02`) was stale, suppressing Google's crawl frequency signal for pages that have since been modified.

---

## v7.66.0 (2026-05-08) — RSS / JSON Feed loop-de-regreso

### Engine — scripts/generate_narrative_signals.py
- **`write_feeds()` function:** New function appended to `generate_narrative_signals.py`. After each intraday narrative run, writes two static feed files to the site root: `feed.xml` (RSS 2.0 with `<atom:link>` self-reference, TTL 120) and `feed.json` (JSON Feed 1.1). Each entry title is `[REGIME] first 80 chars of narrative`. Each entry body includes the full narrative plus up to 3 critical/warning signals. Maintains a rolling window of 20 items. Change-detection guard: skips update if the narrative text is identical to the most recent feed entry (prevents duplicate items on `checked_at`-only updates). Zero new dependencies — uses stdlib `xml.etree.ElementTree` + `json`.
- **`main()` call:** `write_feeds()` is called just before `save_snapshot()`, reading `index.json` and `signals.json` from `ai-analysis/` after they have been committed for that run.

### Frontend — index.html
- **`<link rel="alternate">` discovery tags:** Added two `<link>` tags to `<head>` — `type="application/rss+xml"` pointing to `feed.xml` and `type="application/feed+json"` pointing to `feed.json`. Browsers and RSS extensions (Feedly, NetNewsWire, etc.) auto-detect these tags and surface the subscription option.
- **RSS button in statusbar:** Added `<button id="rss-btn">` and `<div id="rss-popover">` anchor to the statusbar flex row, to the left of the alerts bell. Calls `toggleRssPopover()` on click.
- **`feed.js` script tag:** Added `<script src="assets/feed.js?v=1.0.0" defer></script>` after `onboarding.js`.

### Frontend — assets/feed.js (new file)
- **RSS popover:** Self-contained IIFE. Builds a 260px popover lazily on first open with two subscription links (RSS 2.0 and JSON Feed 1.1), each styled as terminal-palette cards with hover `border-color` transition. Closes on outside click or Escape. Exports `window.toggleRssPopover()`. No external dependencies.

---



### Frontend — assets/onboarding.js (new file)
- **Welcome tour:** New 3-step first-visit overlay (`gi_welcome_done` localStorage key) that guides new users through the terminal's three core value propositions: AI Market Narrative, Macro Regime, and COT Positioning. Displayed 2.8s after page load to let data panels populate. Auto-dismissed after 60s. Uses `gi-tour-highlight` CSS class to outline the targeted element with a blue ring. Keyboard-navigable (arrow keys, Escape). Addresses week-1 retention of 1.8% (industry benchmark: 20–30%) by closing the 60-second value-gap for new users.
- **Live regime in step 2:** Step 2 reads `#risk-regime` from the live DOM at render time and builds a contextual sentence keyed to the current value (RISK-ON / MIXED / CAUTION / RISK-OFF), so the user sees a real statement about today's market. Falls back to a generic description if the value has not yet loaded.
- **Active "Get started" CTA:** The final step's "Get started" button scrolls to the first row in `#fx-pairs-tbody` and fires `.click()` on it (350ms after scroll settles), opening the inline pair detail. The user exits the tour already interacting with live data.
- **Alert onboarding gate:** Modified `giOnboardShouldShow()` in `dashboard.js` to return `false` until `gi_welcome_done` is set, preventing the alerts tooltip from competing visually with the welcome tour on first visit. When the tour completes, `gi_ob_done` is cleared so the alerts tooltip can surface on the next visit.

### Frontend — index.html
- **Script tag:** Added `<script src="assets/onboarding.js?v=1.0.0" defer></script>` before `#sr-announce`.

### Guides — guide-cot.html
- **Title:** "CFTC COT Report Explained: How to Read Institutional FX Positioning — Global Investing" (was: "CFTC COT Report Guide: Read Speculative Positioning for FX — Global Investing"). Targets the "how to read" and "institutional" intent signals in the query population around this page's 2,219 organic impressions at position 6.9.
- **Meta description:** Rewritten to answer the query directly ("Read COT data the way institutional traders do…") rather than describe the guide academically. Same change applied to og:title, og:description, twitter:title, twitter:description.

### Guides — guide-gbpjpy-cot.html
- **Title:** Added "Global Investing" branding suffix and expanded "COT" to "COT Positioning" for keyword match. Was: "GBP/JPY Bias Factors: COT, Rate Differential & Risk Regime Explained". Now: "GBP/JPY Bias Factors: COT Positioning, Rate Differential & Risk Regime — Global Investing".
- **Meta description:** Rewritten as a question ("What is the current GBP/JPY directional bias — and why?") to match the informational intent of the 421 impressions at position 2.5 for "gbpjpy bias factors". Adds "live from the terminal" to signal fresh data. Same change applied to OG/Twitter tags.

---



### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js
- **ETH STOOQ_RT_CACHE missing:** `STOOQ_RT_CACHE['eth']` was never populated — `_lwBuildTodayBar('eth')` always returned `null` because the cache key was `undefined`. Added early seed (`_caEthEarly`) and final assignment (`_ethIntraday`) mirroring the existing BTC pattern. ETH charts now show a live today-bar correctly.
- **BTC May-07 gap:** Inserted the missing May-07 bar in `btc.json` and `eth.json`, reconstructed from surrounding data and confirmed via `prev_close` in `quotes.json`. H/L set to O/C range pending the next 22:30 UTC workflow run.

### Engine — scripts/fetch_ohlc.py
- **Crypto gap detection:** Added a post-dedup gap guard for `CRYPTO_SYMBOLS`: if the last two bars are ≥2 days apart, a `WARN` is emitted in workflow logs identifying the missing date and likely cause (yfinance finalization lag after midnight ET runs).

Updated `_hmBuildDOM` header HTML and title renderer to use `#hm-hd-left` / `#hm-title-row` / `#hm-badge` structure. Flag span now inserted dynamically into `#hm-title-row` beside the title.
- **Metrics strip:** Background updated to `#0d1117`, cell dividers via `border-right: 1px solid #30363d` (was CSS grid `gap:1px` with overlay background), value font-size bumped to 15px.
- **Tabs:** Active tab underline updated to `#388bfd`, font bumped to IBM Plex Sans 500.

## v7.64.12 (2026-05-08) — Fix: deformed FX candles root cause — bad H/L in OHLC historical bars

### Engine — scripts/fetch_ohlc.py (public repo)
- **Root cause (systematic — all 28 FX pairs, ~55% of bars):** `fetch_fx_ohlc_from_1h()`, `fetch_gold_ohlc_from_1h()`, and `fetch_equity_ohlc_from_1h()` all initialized the day bucket as `{"open": o, "high": h, "low": l}` using the raw 1H bar values. In yfinance, a 1H bar's `Open` is the **last tick of the prior hour**, while `High`/`Low` only reflect ticks **within** that hour. When the session opens with a price jump (common at the 21:00 UTC FX session open), `Open > High` or `Open < Low` within the first 1H bar — and since `open` was stored but never included in the `max(highs)`/`min(lows)` accumulation, the final day bucket ended up with `high < open` or `low > open`. These are structurally impossible OHLC bars that Lightweight Charts renders as deformed candles with inverted or missing wicks.
- **Scale of damage:** 40–62% of bars in the last 90 days were structurally invalid across all 28 FX pairs in the shipped `ohlc-data/*.json` files. The same bug affected Gold and Nikkei (the other 1H-aggregated instruments).
- **Fix:** Changed all 4 `day_buckets` initializations from `{"high": h, "low": l}` to `{"high": max(o, h), "low": min(o, l)}`. This ensures the session's opening price is always within the H/L range, matching how Bloomberg and TradingView construct OHLC from tick data.
- **Why this wasn't caught earlier:** The deformation only becomes visible when `open` falls outside the `[low, high]` range of the first 1H bar — which happens ~55% of sessions at the 21:00 UTC FX open where gap-style price moves are common. Day sessions where the open is within the first bar's range look correct, masking the systematic nature of the bug.
- **Action required:** Run the OHLC workflow (`Update OHLC Chart Data`) immediately after deploying this fix to regenerate all `ohlc-data/*.json` files with corrected bars.

---

## v7.64.11 (2026-05-08) — Fix: FX candle wicks deformed every night 21:00–00:00 UTC (two root causes)

### Engine — scripts/fetch_intraday_quotes.py
- **Root cause 1 — wrong session_start branch (v7.64.11a):** `compute_fx_session_hl()` used `session_start = now.replace(hour=21) − 1 day` unconditionally. At `hour >= 21` (new FX session open) this computed yesterday 21:00 UTC instead of today 21:00 UTC, aggregating 25h of 1H bars (full prior session + current session) and producing wildly oversized wicks on the live today-bar candle every night 21:00–00:00 UTC. **Fix:** conditional branch — `if now_utc.hour >= 21: session_start = today 21:00; else: session_start = yesterday 21:00`.
- **Root cause 2 — yfinance date format error (v7.64.11b):** `yf.download()` was called with `start`/`end` as `"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M"` strings. yfinance's internal date parser only accepts `"%Y-%m-%d"` — the time component caused `ValueError('unconverted data remains:  HH:MM')` in all 28 FX batch downloads, resulting in `[SessionHL] 0/28 FX session H/L computed` and the fallback to stale `dayHigh`/`dayLow` (UTC-midnight cutoff), which reproduced the bad-wicks symptom independently of root cause 1. **Fix:** pass `fetch_start_str` / `fetch_end_str` as date-only strings; `fetch_end` gets `+1 day` to preserve inclusive coverage.

---

## v7.64.10 (2026-05-07) — Real Rate Carry Modal: fix loading latency

### Frontend — assets/real-carry-modal.js
- **Root cause (P1 — main bottleneck):** `compute_fx_session_hl()` used the formula `session_start = now.replace(hour=21) − 1 day` unconditionally for all hours. At `hour >= 21` (when the new FX session has just opened at 21:00 UTC), this set `session_start` to **yesterday 21:00 UTC** instead of **today 21:00 UTC**. The function then aggregated 25 hours of 1H bars (the full prior session + the first few hours of the new session) instead of only the bars since the new session opened (~1–3 hours). Result: `session_high` / `session_low` spanned the previous session's complete range, producing absurdly long wicks on the live today-bar candle — visible every night between 21:00 UTC (17:00 ET) and 00:00 UTC (20:00 ET), exactly the "deformed FX candles at night" symptom reported nightly.
- **Fix:** Added an explicit branch: `if now_utc.hour >= 21: session_start = today 21:00 UTC; else: session_start = yesterday 21:00 UTC`. The fix is a one-line conditional; all other logic (fetch window, bar filtering, H/L aggregation) is unchanged.
- **Why it regressed repeatedly:** The old comment incorrectly stated "in both cases the formula is identical: now.replace(hour=21) − 1 day." That was wrong for `hour >= 21`. The prior fix attempts addressed symptoms (today-bar date logic, strip logic) without identifying this root cause in the Python intraday script.

---



### Frontend — assets/real-carry-modal.js
- **Root cause (P1 — main bottleneck):** `_rcmFetchData()` fetched FRED T5YIE and T5YIFR as a blocking `await` *before* fetching the 6 remaining `extended-data/*.json` files. FRED is a cross-origin CSV endpoint that returns the full historical dataset (~several hundred KB). From South America, round-trip latency is 800–2500ms. Because `extended-data/USD.json` and `extended-data/EUR.json` already contain fresh `inflationExpectations` (written by the weekly engine pipeline), the FRED fetch was blocking the modal for 1–2.5 seconds to obtain data that was already available same-origin.
- **Root cause (P2 — serial await chain):** Steps 3 (extended-data), 4 (meetings.json), 5 (quotes.json) were serial `await` calls that queued behind step 2 (FRED). All four could run in parallel; none depends on the others.
- **Root cause (P3 — blind cache invalidation):** The 15-minute `setInterval` unconditionally set `_rcmData = null` even when the modal was closed. A user opening the modal at T+14:59 after the last interval always got a cold start (full re-fetch + spinner), even though the data was only 14:59 stale.
- **Root cause (P4 — in-flight dedup):** `_rcmFetching` was a boolean flag that returned `void` on concurrent calls. Rapid double-clicks spawned two parallel fetch trees that both tried to write `_rcmData`.
- **Root cause (P5 — no-store cache header):** FRED fetches used `cache:'no-store'`, bypassing the browser HTTP cache. Each modal open downloaded the full FRED CSV from scratch, even within the same browser session.
- **Fix — Phase 1/2 architecture:** `_rcmFetchData()` now runs all same-origin fetches (8×rates + 8×extended-data + meetings.json + quotes.json = 18 requests) in a single `Promise.all`. Phase 1 completes in ~40-80ms (HTTP/2 multiplexed, same-origin). The modal renders immediately from Phase 1 data. FRED T5YIE/T5YIFR is now a non-blocking Phase 2 fire-and-forget that upgrades USD/EUR `infl.exp` to live market-implied values and silently re-renders the metrics bar if/when it resolves. Typical perceived open time: <100ms (cache hit) or ~80ms (cold Phase 1).
- **Fix — TTL-based cache:** `_rcmFetchedAt` timestamp replaces blind nulling. Cache is valid for `_RCM_TTL` (15 min). The interval now fires every 60s but only triggers a re-fetch when the cache is actually stale. `_rcmData` is never nulled before new data is committed — a user opening the modal mid-background-refresh sees the previous data instantly.
- **Fix — in-flight dedup:** `_rcmFetchPromise` stores the active `Promise`. Concurrent `openRealCarryModal` calls or rapid tab switches share one fetch tree.
- **Fix — cache header:** Removed `cache:'no-store'` from FRED fetch. Browser HTTP cache now reused within session (FRED responses typically cached 5–30 min by CDN headers), eliminating redundant full-CSV downloads.
- **Fix — spinner suppressed on cache hit:** `openRealCarryModal` only shows "Fetching…" on a true cold start (`_rcmData` is null). Subsequent opens within the TTL window render instantly without flashing the loading state.

## v7.64.9 (2026-05-07) — Guide: document Real Rate Carry Modal (3 tabs, sources, mocks)

### Guides — guide-dashboard.html
- **New section under "Carry Trade — Top Pairs":** Added full documentation of the Real Rate Carry Analysis modal (`real-carry-modal.js`), which was previously undocumented in the guide.
- **Modal overview:** Describes the header metrics bar (best/worst real rate, max spread, data source status) and the three-tab structure.
- **Tab 1 — Rates Breakdown:** Documents all six columns (Nominal, Infl. Exp., Data Age, Real Rate, OIS Bias), the star marker for the top real rate, the green dot live-source indicator for USD/EUR FRED breakevens, and the color coding scale (matching Bloomberg FXFR convention).
- **Tab 2 — Real Rate Matrix:** Documents the 8×8 differential matrix, cell = row real rate − column real rate convention, diagonal = absolute real rate, and the two-tier shading (≥1.5% high intensity, ≥0.15% standard).
- **Tab 3 — Pair Detail:** Documents all eight KV metrics (nominal carry, real carry, per-leg real rates, nominal carry/vol, real carry/vol, OIS bias per leg), the three-tier sustainability assessment (Sustainable / Moderate / Carry trap risk), and the carry/vol calculation (|spread| / HV30).
- **Inflation source table:** Added a reference table listing source, type, and cadence for all eight G8 currencies — USD/EUR (FRED daily market-implied breakevens), GBP/JPY/AUD/CAD/CHF (IMF SDMX 3.0 CPI YoY weekly), NZD (OECD Data Explorer weekly).
- **Mocks added:** Header+metrics bar mock, Rates Breakdown table mock (8 rows with realistic rates/real rates), condensed Real Rate Matrix mock (3×5 excerpt), Pair Detail mock (USD/JPY with sustainability assessment).
- **Click behavior corrected:** Guide previously stated clicking a carry row opens the price chart. Corrected: clicking opens the Real Rate Carry Modal on the Pair Detail tab for that pair.

## v7.64.8 (2026-05-07) — CB Rate Expectations: fix stale meeting dates across all 8 banks

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js
- **Root cause:** `fetchFedExpectations()` was reading `meetings?.allMeetingsFormatted?.[0]` to display the next meeting date in the CB Rate Expectations panel. This always shows the *first* entry of the full calendar list (e.g. "29 Jan"), which is a past date once any meeting has elapsed. The `meetings.json` engine already computes and stores the correct upcoming date in the `nextMeeting` field — the frontend was simply ignoring it.
- **Fix:** Changed line 6656 to read `meetings?.nextMeeting` instead of `meetings?.allMeetingsFormatted?.[0]`. All 8 banks now display the actual next upcoming meeting date.

### Data — meetings-data/meetings.json
- **AUD stale nextMeeting:** The RBA meeting of 5 May 2026 has already passed (today is 7 May 2026). Updated `nextMeeting` → "16 Jun" and `nextMeetingISO` → "2026-06-16" (next scheduled RBA meeting per rba.gov.au).

## v7.64.7 (2026-05-07) — Narrative EM filter: dual-layer guard against non-G8 CB pollution

### Engine — scripts/generate_narrative_signals.py
- **Root cause:** On 2026-05-07 the narrative cited "Banxico cuts 25bp to 6.50% — NZD/CAD bilateral divergence extends." Banxico (Mexico's CB) is not a G8 driver. The article was additionally mis-tagged by the publisher as `cur=NZD`, bypassing a simple currency-tag filter.
- **Fix — Layer 1 (cur tag):** `build_context()` and `build_session_news_block()` now filter `high_impact` to only articles where `cur` is in `{"USD","EUR","GBP","JPY","AUD","CAD","CHF","NZD"}`. Rejects correctly-tagged EM articles (MXN, BRL, CNY, INR, etc.).
- **Fix — Layer 2 (title keywords):** Both functions additionally reject articles whose title contains any known non-G8 CB name: Banxico, Banco de Mexico, BCB, PBOC, RBI, SARB, Norges Bank, Riksbank, NBP, CBRT, Banco Central de Chile/Colombia. This layer catches publisher mis-tags regardless of the `cur` field.
- **Fix — NARRATIVE_SYSTEM Rule 13 (G8 SCOPE):** New mandatory rule inserted before Rule 14 (was 13). Explicitly forbids the model from mentioning non-G8 central banks in the narrative body or the forward-looking close — even if a headline slips through the Python filter. Two-line example block: correct (WTI/CAD framing) vs wrong (Banxico in narrative).
- **Rule renumbering:** Former Rule 13 (ABSOLUTE LEVELS) → Rule 14; former Rule 14 (BOND VOL) → Rule 15. All subsequent rules renumbered accordingly.

---

## v7.64.6 (2026-05-07) — Session context regenerated at each session open (not once daily)

### Engine — scripts/generate_narrative_signals.py
- **Fix:** `generate_session_context()` moved from `RUN_STRUCTURAL` block to `RUN_INTRADAY` block. Now runs on every intraday execution (12×/day), with fresh context at each session open: Sydney 22:00 UTC, Tokyo 00:00, London 06:30, NY 14:30. Previously ran once at 06:00 UTC — making Sydney notes 15 hours stale by the time the session opened.
- **Cost impact:** ~2,800 tokens/run × 12 runs = ~33,600 additional tokens/day. Under 2% of daily budget.

### Engine — .github/workflows/generate-ai-narrative.yml
- Commit step updated to include `session-context.json` in the intraday commit.

### Engine — .github/workflows/generate-structural-context.yml
- Removed references to session context generation (structural layer no longer writes this file).

### Site — assets/heatmap-modal.js

## v7.64.5 (2026-05-07) — Session panel: honest data labeling + state-first note layout

### Site — assets/heatmap-modal.js
- **Bar section — misleading per-session values removed:** The previous implementation multiplied the same full-day composite `pct` by different volume weights for each session row, producing four different-sized bars despite all sharing the same underlying data. This gave the false impression that each session had a distinct measured performance. Fixed: `populateSession()` now computes the full-day intraday composite once (`dayComposite`) and shows it at full width for all active/past session rows. Bar brightness differentiates state — active (70% opacity), past (30% opacity, dimmed), upcoming (no bar). A data note below the grid explicitly reads "Day % vs prev close · session-specific OHLC not available" (Bloomberg/Eikon transparency convention).
- **Bar section — section title corrected:** Was "COMPOSITE STRENGTH BY SESSION" (implying per-session measurement). Now "INTRADAY COMPOSITE · SESSION WINDOW STATUS" — accurately describes what is shown.
- **Bar section — upcoming rows:** Now show local open time in the value column as a hint (previously showed —). Label uses ○ dot. Orange color consistent with UPCOMING chip in notes section.
- **Notes section — state-first layout (Bloomberg convention):** Session header now reads: `NAME● LIVE` / `NAME CLOSED` / `NAME○ UPCOMING` on its own line, with the note text indented below. Previously the CLOSED badge was appended inline at the end of the note text, making sentences like "EUR/JPY +0.19% during session. BoJ passivity keeps cross bid CLOSED" — grammatically broken and hard to scan.
- **Notes section — UPCOMING chip:** Now suppresses the AI outlook text for sessions that have not opened, replacing it with "Opens HH:MM — context generated daily at 06:00 UTC". Previously used "context publishes at open" which was inaccurate (context is generated once at 06:00 UTC regardless of session open time).
- **Notes section — color palette:** Upcoming sessions now use `--orange` (`#f6941c`) consistently in both bar labels and note headers. Removes the undefined `--amber` CSS variable that was previously referenced.

---



### Site — assets/heatmap-modal.js
- **Problem:** The Session tab rendered all four session notes (Sydney, Tokyo, London, New York) with identical visual weight regardless of whether each session had already occurred, was currently open, or had not yet started. AI-generated outlook notes for Tokyo/London/New York (e.g. "GBP/USD -0.33% session") were displayed at full brightness at 22:44 UTC when only Sydney was open, making future projections read as accomplished facts — a materially misleading presentation on a professional terminal.
- **Fix:** Added `getSessionState(sName)` — a pure front-end classification function that maps each session to one of three states at render time based on the current UTC hour and the industry-standard session close boundaries (Sydney 06:00, Tokyo 09:00, London 16:00, New York 21:00). No change to `session-context.json` schema or the Gemini prompt.
- **Rendering per state (Bloomberg/Eikon convention):**
  - `active` — blue label + `●` dot + full-brightness text. Session is open; note describes the live move.
  - `past` — muted gray label + muted gray text. Session closed; result is historical fact (same dimming Bloomberg uses for closed sessions).
  - `upcoming` — amber label + `○` dot + slightly muted text + 8px `UPCOMING` badge. Session has not opened; note is an outlook/watch, not a result.
- **Zero token impact:** Classification runs entirely in JS at render time. Gemini continues to generate all four session notes per currency in a single call; the front end decides how to style them. No prompt changes, no extra API calls.

---

## v7.64.3 (2026-05-07) — generate_narrative_signals.py: fix UnboundLocalError crashing --mode structural

### Engine — scripts/generate_narrative_signals.py
- **Root cause:** `narrative_prompt` and `signals_prompt` were initialised inside `if RUN_INTRADAY:` (line 7981) but referenced unconditionally at lines 7991 and 8004. In `--mode structural` (`RUN_INTRADAY=False`) the assignment block was skipped, so any code path that reached those references raised `UnboundLocalError: cannot access local variable 'narrative_prompt' where it is not associated with a value`. This caused every run of `generate-structural-context.yml` to crash with exit code 1 after the change-detection pass, before writing `session-context.json` or `currency-drivers.json`. The workflow had been silently failing since at least 2026-05-06 — confirmed by the `session-context.json` timestamp stuck at 2026-05-05T08:00:08Z.
- **Fix:** Hoisted `narrative_prompt` and `signals_prompt` initialisation to unconditional scope (before the `if RUN_INTRADAY:` guard), so both variables are always bound regardless of run mode. Wrapped the entire narrative + signals execution block (Gemini call, Groq fallback, file writes) inside `if RUN_INTRADAY:` so it is genuinely skipped in structural mode — not just partially guarded. This also prevents the signals `time.sleep()` and `_write_signals()` closure from executing unnecessarily in structural-only runs.
- **Regression note:** `currency-drivers.json` being dated 2026-05-05 is **not** a bug — structural change detection correctly found no change (COT weekEnding still 2026-04-28, biasUpdated still 2026-05-04). It will auto-update when the CFTC publishes the May 5 week report (Friday 2026-05-09).

---

## v7.64.2 (2026-05-07) — EuroStoxx 50: fix missing yesterday bar via 1H aggregation routing

### Engine — scripts/fetch_ohlc.py
- **Root cause identified:** `yfinance.history(period="3y")` for `^STOXX50E` computes its end-date in UTC. The workflow runs at 22:30 UTC, which is already the next calendar day in CEST (UTC+2). yfinance was excluding the current UTC-date's bar for European exchanges even when the session had completed hours earlier (Euronext closes ~15:30 UTC), causing `stoxx.json` to end at 2026-05-05 instead of 2026-05-06 after the May 6 run. 1H bars do not share this lag — they are available immediately after each hourly candle closes.
- **Fix:** Added `stoxx` to `EQUITY_1H_SYMBOLS` set alongside `nikkei`. `fetch_ohlc()` now routes `stoxx` to `fetch_equity_ohlc_from_1h()` instead of the native 1D path. The 21:00 UTC session boundary guard is safe for EuroStoxx (closes 15:30 UTC, well before the 22:30 UTC run).
- **`fetch_equity_ohlc_from_1h` docstring updated:** Added detailed root-cause explanation for the EuroStoxx yfinance native-1D availability lag.
- **`NON_FX_SYMBOLS` unchanged:** `stoxx` remains in this set to keep it out of `FX_SYMBOLS`. The `EQUITY_1H_SYMBOLS` check runs after the FX/CME checks in `fetch_ohlc()` — routing is unambiguous.
- **Routing comment updated** (Non-FX block): count corrected from "Non-FX (9)" to "Non-FX (8)"; `Nikkei` and `Stoxx` moved to the Equity 1H description.

---
