## v7.64.0 (2026-05-07) — Inflation fetch v4.3: IMF SDMX 3.0 as G6 primary + quarterly date parser fix

### Site — scripts/fetch_inflation_expectations.py
- **New G6 primary: IMF SDMX 3.0 API** (`api.imf.org/external/sdmx/3.0`). Endpoint: `dataflow/IMF.STA/CPI/~/{ISO3}.CPI._T.IX.M`. Returns monthly CPI index for all G6 countries including Japan (which is absent from OECD DF_PRICES_ALL). YoY computed as 12-month pct change on the index series. No API key required. Lag ~4-6 weeks.
- **Root cause: JPY HTTP 404** — Japan migrated to COICOP 2018 and is only available in `DF_PRICES_N_CP01_*` dataflows, not in `DF_PRICES_ALL` (COICOP 1999). The IMF API has JPN in its CPI dataset. Fixed.
- **Root cause: AUD/NZD "no parseable rows"** — OECD returns quarterly time periods as `2024-Q4`. The v4.2 `_parse_date()` added `-01` suffix producing `2024-Q4-01`, which fails `fromisoformat`. Fixed: parser now detects `Q` in 7-char strings and converts to last month of quarter (Q1→Mar, Q2→Jun, Q3→Sep, Q4→Dec).
- **Root cause: CHF stale** — OECD last obs Dec 2025, today May 2026 = 157 days > 120d threshold. IMF monthly index resolves this since it has CHF through ~Mar 2026.
- **Source cascade updated**: IMF → OECD Explorer (with fixed parser) → FRED MEI → World Bank.

---

## v7.63.1 (2026-05-07) — Inflation workflow: self-contained in public repo (no engine checkout)

### Site — .github/workflows/update-inflation-expectations.yml
- **Bug fix:** Workflow was checking out the private engine repo and running `python engine/scripts/...`, but `fetch_inflation_expectations.py` v4.2 already lives in the public repo at `scripts/`. Removed the engine checkout step entirely. Workflow now runs `python scripts/fetch_inflation_expectations.py .` from the public repo root — no `PAT_TOKEN` required, no cross-repo dependency.
- **Secret requirement:** Only `FRED_API_KEY` is needed (already available in public repo secrets for FRED breakeven fetching). `PAT_TOKEN` is no longer required by this workflow.

---

## v7.63.0 (2026-05-07) — Inflation fetch v4.2: OECD Data Explorer as G6 primary source

### Engine — scripts/fetch_inflation_expectations.py
- **Root cause of all G6 failures identified:** FRED OECD MEI series (GBRCPIALLMINMEI,
  CANCPIALLMINMEI, CHECPIALLMINMEI etc.) have a **structural ~12–14 month lag** as of 2026.
  They are updated ~annually by OECD MEI publications; FRED mirrors them without a faster
  update cycle. These series will always fail the 120-day stale guard and are not a viable
  primary source. This is not a network or parsing issue — the data simply does not exist
  beyond ~Mar/Apr 2025 in those series.
- **New G6 primary: OECD Data Explorer SDMX REST API** (`sdmx.oecd.org`). This is the
  official OECD API that replaced `stats.oecd.org` and publishes the same CPI data with
  only ~4–6 weeks lag. Dataflow: `OECD.SDD.TPS,DSD_PRICES@DF_PRICES_ALL,1.0`.
  Key pattern: `{country}.{M|Q}.N.CPI.PA._T.N.GY` (TRANSFORMATION=GY = annual % change).
  Format: `csvfilewithlabels`. No API key required.
- **AUD/NZD frequency:** quarterly (Q), all others monthly (M). Stale threshold for
  quarterly raised from 200d → 210d (one extra quarter buffer for release cycle).
- **All v4.0/v4.1 national-office API code removed** (ONS, e-Stat, ABS, StatsCan, SNB,
  Stats NZ). OECD Data Explorer covers all 6 G6 currencies from a single endpoint pattern
  with consistent parsing, eliminating per-currency brittle scrapers.
- **Output format unchanged:** patches `extended-data/{CCY}.json` updating only
  `data.inflationExpectations` and `dates.inflationExpectations`; all other fields preserved.
- **Site root argument:** script now accepts optional `argv[1]` as site root path
  (used by the new workflow: `python fetch_inflation_expectations.py site`).

### Engine — .github/workflows/update-inflation-expectations.yml
- **New dedicated workflow** (replaces the inflation section of the disabled
  `update-extended-data.yml`). Runs weekly on Wednesdays 07:00 UTC.
- Sources documented in comments: OECD Data Explorer primary, FRED MEI cold spare,
  World Bank last resort.
- Patches `extended-data/{CCY}.json` for all 8 G8 currencies.

---

## v7.62.1 (2026-05-07) — Inflation fetch v4.1: fix 6 bugs from first v4.0 GHA run

### Engine — scripts/fetch_inflation_expectations.py
- **Bug 1 — headers TypeError (ABS, Stats NZ):** `_get()` hardcoded `headers=BASE_HEADERS`
  in `requests.get()`, so callers passing `headers=custom_headers` via `**kwargs` triggered
  `TypeError: got multiple values for keyword argument 'headers'`. Fixed: `_get()` now accepts
  a `custom_headers` kwarg and merges it with `BASE_HEADERS` internally; `**kwargs` no longer
  includes `headers`.
- **Bug 2 — ONS URL wrong path:** `api.ons.gov.uk/v1/datasets/mm23/timeseries/D7G7/data`
  returns 404. Correct v1 path is `/v1/timeseries/D7G7/dataset/mm23/data` (series before
  dataset). Now tries both paths sequentially.
- **Bug 3 — SNB cube ID discontinued:** `pkcpival` is no longer a valid cube.
  Correct cube: `plkopr` ("Consumer prices – total"). Switched to CSV format
  (`/api/cube/plkopr/data/csv/en`) which is more stable than the JSON endpoint.
- **Bug 4 — JPY -1.5% wrong value:** e-Stat CSV (`cpim.csv`) parser matched the first
  `総合` row in the file, which belongs to the index section (not the 前年同月比/YoY section).
  Fixed by: (a) switching primary to e-Stat REST API (statsDataId 0003427113) which returns
  structured JSON; (b) rewriting CSV fallback to first locate the `前年同月比` section header,
  then find the `総合` row within that block only; (c) adding a validity guard: `-3.0% < val < 8.0%`
  (Japan's modern CPI range — deflation floor prevents accepting implausible values).
- **Bug 5 — StatsCan WDS connection failure:** `getDataFromCubePidCoordAndLatestNPeriods` endpoint
  silently fails (likely GHA network timeout). No change to cascade position — FRED
  `CANCPIALLMINMEI` remains the immediate fallback; stale guard will skip it if >120d old.
- **Bug 6 — Stats NZ headers TypeError:** same root cause as Bug 1, fixed by same change.

---

## v7.62.0 (2026-05-07) — Inflation fetch v4.0: national stat office APIs replace OECD SDMX

### Engine — scripts/fetch_inflation_expectations.py
- **Root cause confirmed:** `CPALTT01` is not a valid SUBJECT in the OECD `DSD_KEI@DF_KEI,4.0`
  dataflow (which only contains interest rate subjects such as `IRLTLT01`). All OECD SDMX CPI
  requests return HTTP 422 regardless of MEASURE code. OECD SDMX removed entirely.
- **FRED MINMEI/QINMEI structural lag:** These series mirror OECD MEI annual publications with
  a ~12–16 month lag. Demoted to fallback only; not suitable as primary sources.
- **New primaries — national statistics office APIs (no API keys required):**
  - **GBP:** ONS Timeseries API (`api.ons.gov.uk`) — series D7G7, CPI All Items YoY, monthly, <6w lag.
  - **JPY:** Statistics Bureau Japan e-Stat CSV (`stat.go.jp/data/cpi/sokuhou/tsuki/zuhyou/cpim.csv`)
    — CPI All Items YoY (前年同月比), Shift-JIS encoded, monthly, <4w lag. BOJ HTML table as sub-fallback.
  - **AUD:** ABS SDMX-JSON API (`api.data.abs.gov.au`) — CPI All Groups quarterly index → YoY, ~6w lag.
  - **CAD:** Statistics Canada WDS API (`www150.statcan.gc.ca`) — Table 18-10-0004-01, monthly CPI
    index → YoY, <4w lag. Vector ID resolution fallback included.
  - **CHF:** SNB Data Portal (`data.snb.ch/api/cube/pkcpival/data/json`) — CPI index → YoY, <6w lag.
  - **NZD:** Stats NZ OData API (`api.stats.govt.nz`) — CPI All Groups quarterly index → YoY, ~6w lag.
    RBNZ Survey of Expectations CSV as sub-fallback (1-year ahead expectations).
- **Fallback chain preserved:** FRED MINMEI/QINMEI → World Bank for all G6 currencies.
- **Stale thresholds unchanged:** monthly 120d, quarterly (AUD/NZD) 200d, market-implied (USD/EUR) 10d.

### Engine — .github/workflows/update-inflation-expectations.yml
- Updated source cascade comment to document v4.0 primary sources.
- Commit message updated to name the actual APIs used: `ONS/e-Stat/ABS/StatsCan/SNB/StatsNZ`.

---

## v7.61.5 (2026-05-07) — Fix OECD SDMX 422: CPALTT01.GY → CPALTT01.ST + index-to-YoY; per-currency stale thresholds

### Root cause (v2.0 regression)
`fetch_inflation_expectations.py` v2.0 used OECD SDMX key `CPALTT01.GY` — but `GY`
(growth year) is a *transformation* code, not a *MEASURE* code. The `DSD_KEI@DF_KEI,4.0`
dataflow uses a `MEASURE` dimension, not a `TRANSFORMATION` dimension. The valid measure
for the CPI series is `ST` (standardised), identical to the `IRLTLT01.ST` bond yield key
that fetch_bond_yields.py has used successfully in GHA. This mismatch caused HTTP 422
for all six G6 currencies, falling through to stale/404 FRED fallbacks.

Additionally, `CPALTT01AUM659N` and `CPALTT01NZM659N` do not exist on FRED (404), and
the other `CPALTT01*M659N` YoY-direct series ended in 2021 (stale data). The 120-day
stale guard was also too strict for AUD/NZD, whose quarterly CPI can legitimately produce
~120-day-old observations in May (Q4 data released in January).

### Site — scripts/fetch_inflation_expectations.py → v3.0
- **OECD SDMX key: `CPALTT01.GY` → `CPALTT01.ST`** — correct MEASURE code for the
  standardised CPI index series (base 2015=100). Fetches last 16 observations.
- **Added `index_to_yoy(obs)`:** Computes YoY % from index observations by finding the
  reading closest to 12 months prior (75-day tolerance, covers both monthly and quarterly).
  Identical approach to `update_extended_data.py` v13.0's `_index_to_yoy()`.
- **Added FRED MINMEI/QINMEI as cascade step 2** for G6 currencies:
  GBP→`GBRCPIALLMINMEI`, AUD→`AUSCPIALLQINMEI`, CAD→`CANCPIALLMINMEI`,
  CHF→`CHECPIALLMINMEI`, NZD→`NZLCPIALLQINMEI`. These are the active OECD MEI index
  series; `CPALTT01*M659N` YoY-direct series all ended 2021.
- **Removed non-existent FRED series:** `CPALTT01AUM659N` and `CPALTT01NZM659N` (404 —
  never existed). Replaced with `AUSCPIALLQINMEI` / `NZLCPIALLQINMEI` → index-to-YoY.
- **Per-currency stale thresholds (`MAX_AGE`):**
  - AUD/NZD: 120d → **200d** (quarterly CPI; Q4 released Jan → May run sees ~120d data,
    years with Feb release would exceed old guard).
  - GBP/JPY/CAD/CHF: unchanged at 120d.
  - USD/EUR: set to 10d (market-implied daily series; staleness here is a signal problem).
- **JPY fallback simplified:** `JPNCPIALLMINMEI` ended Jun-2021 and is not usable.
  Cascade is now: OECD SDMX → World Bank (direct, no dead FRED series).

### Site — .github/workflows/update-inflation-expectations.yml
- Updated source cascade comment to reflect v3.0 key (`CPALTT01.ST`) and MINMEI/QINMEI fallbacks.
- Commit message: `… — G8 (FRED/OECD-SDMX-ST/ECB/WB)`.

### Site — assets/real-carry-modal.js → v1.4
- Header and source comments updated: `CPALTT01 YoY` → `CPALTT01.ST index → YoY`.
  Source labels in `_RCM_IE_SRC` and `liveNote` unchanged (OECD SDMX remains accurate).

### Site — index.html
- Cache-bust: `real-carry-modal.js?v=1.3.0` → `?v=1.4.0`.

---

## v7.61.4 (2026-05-07) — Fix stale G6 CPI: replace broken economic-data cascade with OECD SDMX direct fetch

### Root cause
`fetch_inflation_expectations.py` v1.1 used `economic-data/{CCY}.json` as its primary
CPI source for G6. That file was maintained by `update_pmi_from_calendar.py`, which
depended on `calendar.json` — produced by `update-extended-data.yml` (disabled v7.50.0).
`calendar.json` froze at 2026-03-28. All April/May CPI releases (GBP +0.3pp, JPY +2.3pp,
AUD +0.7pp, CAD +0.5pp, CHF +0.5pp) were missing from the modal.

### Site — scripts/fetch_inflation_expectations.py → v2.0
- **Removed `read_econ_data()` and `economic-data/` dependency entirely.**
- **Added `oecd_cpi_yoy(country_code, freq)`:** Fetches CPI YoY directly from
  OECD SDMX REST API (`sdmx.oecd.org/public/rest/`), same domain confirmed working
  in GHA via `fetch_bond_yields.py`. Uses `DSD_KEI@DF_KEI` dataflow, `CPALTT01.GY`
  indicator (MEI CPI all items, year-on-year growth rate). Typical lag: 2–4 weeks.
- **Added `ecb_hicp_yoy(area_code)`:** ECB ICP SDMX endpoint for EUR/GBP HICP flash
  fallback. Same API domain as existing bond yield fetches.
- **Per-currency cascades (G6):**
  - GBP: OECD SDMX monthly (GBR) → ECB HICP (GB) → FRED `CPGRLE01GBM659N` → WB
  - JPY: OECD SDMX monthly (JPN) → FRED `CPALTT01JPM659N` → WB
  - AUD: OECD SDMX quarterly (AUS) → OECD monthly → FRED `CPALTT01AUM659N` → WB
  - CAD: OECD SDMX monthly (CAN) → FRED `CPALTT01CAM659N` → WB
  - CHF: OECD SDMX monthly (CHE) → FRED `CPALTT01CHM659N` → WB
  - NZD: OECD SDMX quarterly (NZL) → OECD monthly → FRED `CPALTT01NZM659N` → WB
- **Removed `index_to_yoy()` and FRED index series** (MINMEI/QINMEI — all 403 in GHA).
  All G6 now use YoY-direct series; no index arithmetic needed.
- **USD/EUR unchanged:** FRED T5YIE / T5YIFR (daily market-implied breakevenss).

### Site — .github/workflows/update-inflation-expectations.yml
- Removed `ECON_DATA_DIR` env var (no longer read by v2.0 script).
- Updated source cascade comment in file header.
- Commit message: `… — G8 (FRED/OECD-SDMX/ECB/WB)`.

### Site — assets/real-carry-modal.js → v1.3
- `_RCM_IE_SRC` for GBP/JPY/AUD/CAD/CHF/NZD: `'CPI YoY · calendar actuals'` →
  `'CPI YoY · OECD SDMX'`. Accurate source attribution for tooltips and Tab 1 note.
- `liveNote`: `'ForexFactory calendar, weekly batch'` → `'OECD SDMX MEI, weekly batch'`.
- Header comments updated to remove stale references to `economic-data/` and
  `update_pmi_from_calendar.py`.

### Site — index.html
- Cache-bust: `real-carry-modal.js?v=1.2.0` → `?v=1.3.0`.

## v7.58.4 (2026-05-04) — Fix phantom candle for EuroStoxx and other non-FX indices at week open (PREPRE state not recognised as closed)

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js
- **Root cause:** `_lwBuildTodayBar()` had a non-FX phantom-bar guard that only suppressed today-bar injection when `market_state` was `'CLOSED'` or `'POSTPOST'`. Yahoo Finance returns `'PREPRE'` for European indices (EuroStoxx, FTSE, etc.) during the Sunday-night / Monday early-morning window — before their pre-market session begins. `PREPRE` was not in the allowed-closed list, so the guard was bypassed and a today-bar dated `2026-05-04` was injected using Friday's stale close/H/L, producing an extra phantom candle after the last real Friday bar.
- **Fix:** Replaced the explicit `CLOSED | POSTPOST` allowlist with a positive check: the guard now applies to **any** `market_state` that is not `'REGULAR'`. If the exchange is in an active trading session (`REGULAR`), the bar is always allowed through. For all other states (`CLOSED`, `POSTPOST`, `PREPRE`, `PRE`, `POST`, or any future undocumented state), the `lastTradeDate < dateStr` check is applied — if the last trade was on a prior date, the today-bar is suppressed. `PRE`/`POST` states are safe because their `market_time` is same-day, so the date comparison resolves to `false` and the bar is correctly shown.

---

## v7.58.3 (2026-05-04) — Fix FX phantom candle at Sunday-night session open (stale Yahoo dayHigh/dayLow)

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js
- **Root cause (complete diagnosis):** At the start of the FX week, `_lwBuildTodayBar()` built the today-bar using `q.high` / `q.low` from `STOOQ_RT_CACHE`, which are Yahoo's `dayHigh` / `dayLow` fields. Yahoo does **not** clear these fields at the 21:00 UTC FX session open — it keeps serving the prior Friday session's H/L range until enough intraday ticks accumulate (typically 2–4 hours into Monday). The result was a Monday today-bar with `O:157.033 H:157.326 L:155.453 C:157.033` — identical wicks to the last Friday bar (`O:156.561 H:157.326 L:155.453 C:157.033`), visually indistinguishable from a duplicate candle.
- **Fix 1 — STOOQ_RT_CACHE now stores `session_high` / `session_low`:** Added `session_high` and `session_low` from `quotes.json` to the cache object populated in `fetchQuoteBarRT()`. These fields are computed by `fetch_intraday_quotes.py` using 1H bar aggregation over the real 21:00 UTC FX session boundary, and are only present when real session activity has been recorded.
- **Fix 2 — FX today-bar H/L sourced from session data, never from stale dayH/dayL:** In `_lwBuildTodayBar()`, FX bars now use `session_high` / `session_low` when available. When session H/L are absent (as is normal at the very start of the session before 1H bars accumulate), the bar falls back to `Math.max(o, c)` / `Math.min(o, c)` — giving a narrow o/c body with no phantom wicks. Combined with the doji guard (`o===h&&h===l&&l===c`) added in the same fix, a zero-movement early-Monday bar is suppressed entirely until real price action exists.
- **Non-FX unaffected:** Non-FX instruments continue to use `q.high` / `q.low` (Yahoo `dayHigh`/`dayLow`), which are reliable for exchange-traded instruments that have a real session open broadcast.

---

## v7.58.2 (2026-05-03) — Fix narrative placeholders shown verbatim in frontend (Gemini path missing token resolution)

### Engine — scripts/generate_narrative_signals.py
- **Root cause:** `_apply_narrative_guards()` — the post-processing step for the Gemini-primary narrative path (v7.54.0) — applied safe-haven, modal verb, carry-bp, and TIER-1/2 guards but never called `resolve_tokens()` or `fix_direction_mismatch()`. The Groq path resolves tokens inside `_parse_and_process()`, but this function is not called for Gemini output. As a result, `{{gold_pct}}`, `{{spx_pct}}`, `{{dxy_pct}}`, and `{{vix}}` were written verbatim into `ai-analysis/index.json` and displayed as literal placeholder strings in the frontend narrative panel.
- **Fix:** Added Guard 4 (token resolution) to `_apply_narrative_guards()`: calls `extract_tokens_from_intraday()` (or accepts a pre-computed `tokens` dict via new optional parameter), then runs `resolve_tokens()` followed by `fix_direction_mismatch()` on the narrative string before returning. The function signature is backwards-compatible — existing call sites with no `tokens` argument are unaffected.
- **No change to Groq fallback path:** `generate_narrative()` still resolves tokens inside `_parse_and_process()` as before. The fix is scoped entirely to the Gemini path.

---

## v7.58.1 (2026-05-03) — Fix BTC/ETH weekend candle bugs (partial today-bar + open mismatch + weekend schedule)

### Engine — scripts/fetch_ohlc.py
- **Bug 1 — partial today-bar written for crypto on weekends:** The workflow runs Mon–Fri only (`cron: '30 22 * * 1-5'`). The crypto path used an explicit `start/end` date range with `end = tomorrow`, causing yfinance to return an in-progress bar for the current UTC day (only ~22.5h of data at the 22:30 UTC run). This partial bar was written to `btc.json` / `eth.json` and persisted all weekend with artificially truncated H/L wicks. **Fix:** strip any bar whose `time >= today_utc` before writing. dashboard.js injects the live today-bar from `quotes.json` as usual.
- **Bug 2 — candle body colour could contradict net daily direction for crypto:** The native yfinance 1D open field for crypto is the first tick of the UTC day, which sometimes diverges slightly from the previous bar's close due to exchange aggregation. This produced candle bodies where green/red did not match the actual daily pct sign. **Fix:** apply `prev_close` open correction to the crypto series (same convention already used for FX pairs and legacy 1D FX bars), with H/L clamped to maintain OHLC integrity after the open override.

### Frontend — .github/workflows/update-ohlc.yml
- **Added Sat–Sun schedule:** `30 23 * * 0,6` (23:30 UTC). BTC and ETH trade 24/7 — without a weekend run the partial Friday bar persisted all weekend. The 23:30 UTC timing ensures the UTC calendar day is nearly complete (23.5h) before `fetch_ohlc.py` strips the in-progress today-bar and writes the final JSON. All non-crypto symbols are also refreshed on each run (no additional cost — same script, same public-repo unlimited minutes).

---



### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js
- **Root cause fixed:** Previous indicator implementation used three non-existent LWC v5 APIs: `_lwChart.addPane()`, `pane.addSeries()`, and `_lwChart.removePane(paneObject)`. All indicators silently failed on activation.
- **`_buildIndicatorPane` rewritten:** Oscillator sub-panes now created via `_lwChart.addSeries(Type, opts, paneIndex)` with `paneIndex = _lwChart.panes().length` — LWC v5's documented pattern for creating additional panes. Pane height set via `pane.setHeight()` after first series is added.
- **`_destroyIndicatorPane` rewritten:** All series removed via `_lwChart.removeSeries(s)`; pane removed via `_lwChart.removePane(paneIndex)` with numeric index. Ref-line series tracked separately in `_indRefSeries` and cleaned up on destroy.
- **`_addRefLines` rewritten:** Now takes `paneIndex` (number) instead of a pane object, uses `_lwChart.addSeries(LWC.LineSeries, opts, paneIndex)`.
- **State tracking updated:** Replaced `_indPanes` (pane object dict) with `_indPaneIndex` (numeric index dict) and added `_indRefSeries` (ref-line series per pane).
- **Legacy MA toolbar removed:** Removed `_buildAllMAs`, `_renderMaToolbar`, `_openMaDropdown`, `_closeMaDropdown`, `_MA_PALETTE`, `_MA_PERIODS`, `_MA_MAX`, `maLegendEl`, `_updateAllMALegend`, and `_updateMALegend` (~200 lines). No-op shims kept for `_updateAllMALegend` / `_updateMALegend` to avoid crosshair handler errors.

### Frontend — index.html
- **Legacy MA toolbar removed:** Removed `lw-ma-bar` div and `INDICATORS` label from the chart toolbar. `Indicators ▾` button now stands alone.

---



### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js
- **Indicator catalogue (29 indicators):** Replaced the 3 individual oscillator buttons with a full indicator library managed via `_IND_CATALOGUE`. Grouped into Overlays, Oscillators, Volatility, and Volume.
- **Overlays (11):** EMA 20/50/200, WMA 20, HMA 20, VWAP, Bollinger Bands (20,2σ), Keltner Channel (20,1.5×ATR), Donchian Channel (20), Parabolic SAR, Ichimoku Cloud (9/26/52). All rendered on the main price pane.
- **Oscillators (12):** RSI(14), Stochastic(14,3,3), MACD(12,26,9), CCI(20), Williams %R(14), ROC(12), Momentum(10), MFI(14), Awesome Oscillator, TRIX(18), DPO(21), Ultimate Oscillator(7,14,28). Each in its own sub-pane.
- **Volatility (4):** ATR(14), ADX/DMI(14), Aroon(25), Choppiness Index(14).
- **Volume (2):** OBV, CMF(20).
- **Shared math helpers:** `_iSMA`, `_iEMA`, `_iWMA`, `_iRMA` (Wilder), `_iStdev`, `_iTR`. All deterministic — no `Math.random()`.
- **State persistence:** `window._lwIndState` (object keyed by indicator id) replaces `window._lwOscState`. State survives symbol switches.
- **Pane management:** `_buildIndicatorPane()` / `_destroyIndicatorPane()` handle both overlay series (removed from pane 0) and sub-panes (removed via `removePane`). No pane leaks.

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js — Dropdown UX
- **`Indicators ▾` button** opens a grouped dropdown (`_openIndDropdown`). Each row shows the indicator name, description, and a checkbox. Clicking toggles the indicator on/off and re-renders the dropdown in place.
- **Active pills bar** (`#lw-ind-pills`) shows all active indicators with a × to remove each one, displayed inline next to the Indicators button.
- **No indicator limit** — user manages chart space. Industry standard (Bloomberg, Eikon) imposes no hard limit.

### Frontend — index.html
- Replaced 3 individual RSI/STOCH/MACD buttons with `<button id="lw-ind-btn">Indicators ▾</button>` and `<div id="lw-ind-pills">` container. Consistent with existing `.lw-tb-btn` styling and ARIA pattern.

---

## v7.56.0 (2026-05-02) — Add RSI, Stochastic, MACD oscillators to LW chart

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js
- **RSI(14) sub-pane:** Relative Strength Index calculated with Wilder smoothing on close prices. Renders in a dedicated pane below the main chart with 30/50/70 reference lines. State persists across symbol switches via `window._lwOscState`.
- **Stochastic(14,3,3) sub-pane:** Fast %K smoothed to slow %K and %D. Blue line = %K, orange = %D. 20/50/80 reference lines. Standard Bloomberg/Eikon stochastic configuration.
- **MACD(12,26,9) sub-pane:** Histogram (green above / red below zero), MACD line (blue), Signal line (orange). EMA-based calculation on close prices.
- **Calculation helpers added:** `_calcRSI()`, `_calcEMA()`, `_calcMACD()`, `_calcStochastic()` — all deterministic, no `Math.random()`, mirroring standard institutional conventions.
- **Pane management:** `_buildRSIPane()`, `_buildStochPane()`, `_buildMACDPane()` cleanly remove and recreate panes on toggle. No pane leaks on symbol switch.
- **Button handlers:** Clone-and-replace pattern removes stale listeners on chart re-render.

### Frontend — index.html
- **Toolbar:** Added RSI, STOCH, MACD toggle buttons in the INDICATORS section of `#lw-range-bar`, consistent with existing `.lw-tb-btn` styling and `aria-pressed` accessibility pattern.

---

## v7.55.4 (2026-05-02) — Fix fetch_rates.py gap-fill: CB hold periods no longer produce stale history

### Engine — scripts/fetch_rates.py
- **Root cause identified and fixed:** `merge_observations()` used the CB source's last *decision* date as the month key. When a central bank holds its rate, the source returns the decision date (e.g. ECB SDMX returned `2025-06-11` every day for 10 months), so every daily run of `update-rates.yml` overwrote the same `2025-06` slot. Months `2025-07` through `2026-04` were never populated despite the workflow running and reporting `overallStatus: ok`. Identical issue for JPY, CHF, NZD via BIS-CBPOL (missing Apr–May 2026).
- **Fix — gap-fill in `merge_observations()`:** After building the month map, the function now walks forward from the latest populated month to today's month, filling any missing slots with the current rate. This is correct by definition: a "hold" decision means the rate was unchanged throughout. Uses stdlib date arithmetic only (`date`, integer month increment) — no `dateutil` dependency added.
- **Currencies affected:** EUR (10 months behind, most severe), JPY/CHF/NZD (1–2 months behind). USD, GBP, CAD, AUD were unaffected — their sources (NYFed-EFFR, BoE-CSV, BoC-Valet, RBA-HTML) return the current or near-current date rather than the last decision date.
- **No workflow changes needed:** `update-rates.yml` pip install line (`requests beautifulsoup4 lxml`) is sufficient — fix is stdlib-only.
- **Silent failure by design:** The workflow correctly reported `ok` because it was successfully fetching and storing the current rate — the gap was in the *history accumulation*, not the current value. No alerting change required.

---

## v7.55.3 (2026-05-02) — Fix rateMomentum data integrity: EUR rates gap + USD alignment

### Data — rates/EUR.json
- **Missing 10 months backfilled:** `rates/EUR.json` was last updated in June 2025 (28 observations vs expected 36). Months Jul 2025 – Apr 2026 were absent because the `update-rates` workflow silently stopped committing new data for EUR. All 10 missing months added at 2.0% (ECB held the deposit facility rate unchanged from Jun 2025 through Apr 2026). Without this fix, `update_extended_data.py` was computing `rateMomentum` against a stale 2025-06 reference, not the current 2026-04 window.

### Data — extended-data/USD.json
- **rateMomentum corrected:** Stored value was `-0.69` (stale from a previous run with a different date-alignment). Recalculated from `rates/USD.json`: 3.75% (Apr 2026) − 4.5% (Apr 2025) = **-0.75**. `rateMomentum24M` corrected from `-1.69` → `-1.75`.

### Data — extended-data/EUR.json
- **rateMomentum recalculated:** After backfilling `rates/EUR.json`, the 12M reference point moved from Jun 2025 to Apr 2026. Recalculated: 2.0% (Apr 2026) − 2.25% (Apr 2025) = **-0.25**. Previously stored -0.25 was coincidentally correct for the wrong reference window; now confirmed correct against the complete dataset. `rateMomentum24M` confirmed at -2.0 (2.0% vs 4.0% in Apr 2024).

### Note — AUD rateMomentum
- **0.0 is mathematically correct:** AUD 12M lookback compares Mar 2026 (4.10%) vs Mar 2025 (4.10%) — identical rates despite the RBA cutting to 3.60% in between and hiking back. The formula is correct; the CB Rate History modal correctly labels the cycle as "Hiking cycle". `rateMomentum` is not consumed by any frontend panel (confirmed in CHANGELOG v7.49.x audit).

## v7.55.2 (2026-05-02) — Remove CoinGecko, fix about.html font preload, update SW cache

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js
- **Removed `fetchCryptoQuotes()` and all CoinGecko calls:** Function deleted entirely. The `api.coingecko.com` fetch was being blocked by CORS in production (confirmed in Google Rich Results Test console) and was already redundant — `fetchCrossAssetData()` via yfinance intraday JSON already populates BTC in both the topbar and the cross-asset panel, overwriting CoinGecko values immediately anyway. Boot call and 90s setInterval also removed.

### Site — _headers, netlify.toml
- **Removed `api.coingecko.com` from CSP `connect-src`:** Both the GitHub Pages `_headers` CSP and the `netlify.toml` CSP had CoinGecko whitelisted. Removed to keep the allowlist tight now that no code calls it.

### Site — dashboard.js (root, stale artifact)
- **Deleted `/dashboard.js`:** A stale copy of an older dashboard build existed at the repo root. It was not referenced by any HTML page, never served to users, but appeared in file audits and grep results. Removed to prevent future confusion.

### Site — about.html
- **Fixed broken font preload warning:** `about.html` had a `<link rel="preload" as="style">` pointing to a subset font URL (Inter 400/700 only) followed by a separate `<link rel="stylesheet">` loading the full set (300–700 weights). The preloaded URL was never used, triggering a Lighthouse/Googlebot warning. Replaced the three-tag pattern (preload + deferred load + noscript) with a single direct `<link rel="stylesheet">`, matching the pattern used by all other pages.

### Site — sw.js
- **Updated `CACHE_VERSION` to `gi-v7.55.2`:** Was still at `gi-v7.50.5`, causing returning users to be served a stale shell from cache. Bumping the version forces the SW to invalidate the old cache and re-fetch the current assets on next visit.
- **Updated precache entry:** `dashboard.css?v=7.26.3` → `dashboard.css?v=7.52.0` to match the version string used in `index.html`.

## v7.54.11 (2026-05-02) — Health monitor v2.1: business-day thresholds (FIX-13/14)

### Engine — scripts/monitor_data_health.py
- **FIX-13 — check_bond_yields falso positivo en festivos/fin de semana:** El umbral de 2 días calendario disparaba críticos cada sábado y el día siguiente a un festivo (ej. May 1 Labour Day: ECB/BOE no publican → dato de Apr 29 pasa a tener 3d el May 2 → CRÍTICO falso). Añadida función `business_days_since()` que cuenta solo días hábiles Mon-Fri excluyendo festivos EU/UK codificados (Jan 1, May 1, Dec 25, Dec 26). Nuevo umbral: 2 business days para USD/EUR/GBP. JPY mantiene 35 días calendario (fuente mensual, sin cambio).
- **FIX-14 — check_structural_context falso positivo en fin de semana:** `generate-structural-context.yml` corre diariamente a las 06:00 UTC; si el run del sábado falla por datos upstream stale, el dato tiene ~30-32h el sábado → disparaba aviso con umbral de 26h. Threshold ahora weekend-aware: 52h en sábado/domingo, 26h en días hábiles. Un fallo de >2 runs consecutivos (>52h) sigue alertando como aviso real.

## v7.54.10 (2026-05-02) — fetch_bond_yields v1.1: BOE endpoint fix + fallbacks + GHA annotations

### Engine — scripts/fetch_bond_yields.py (site repo)
- **BUG-1 fix — EUR/GBP/JPY fallbacks missing:** Added full source cascade for all three currencies. EUR: ECB SDMX daily → FRED IRLTLT01EZM156N monthly. GBP: BOE SDIE daily → FRED IRLTLT01GBM156N monthly. JPY: ECB FM SDMX monthly → OECD SDMX → FRED IRLTLT01JPM156N.
- **BUG-2 fix — BOE wrong endpoint:** `fromshowcolumns.asp` (HTML, returns 403/bot-challenge) corrected to `_iadb-fromshowcolumns.asp` with `csv.x=yes` + `CSVF=TN` params. This was the root cause of GBP bond10y being stale since v7.50.0 launch.
- **BUG-3 fix — silent failure mode:** Failed fetches now emit `::warning::` / `::error::` GitHub Actions annotations. USD bond10y failure → `exit(1)`. ≥2 other fields failed → `exit(1)` (degraded run). Previously the workflow turned green while writing stale data.
- **BUG-4 fix — EUR EU holiday gap:** ECB SDMX returns empty observations on EU public holidays (May 1, Dec 25, etc.). FRED monthly fallback added so the workflow completes on those days instead of keeping a stale value.

### Engine — .github/workflows/update-bond-yields.yml (site repo)
- **Node.js 24 opt-in:** Added `FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24: true` env to avoid the forced migration breaking the workflow on June 2, 2026.

## v7.54.9 (2026-05-02) — TIER-1 label leak fix (3 layers) + regime mismatch tooltip

### Engine — scripts/generate_narrative_signals.py
- **Guard 5 (new) — TIER-1/2 internal label strip in `_apply_narrative_guards`:** Gemini occasionally echoes the context-block processing directive `TIER-1` into narrative output (e.g. "the primary TIER-1 focus"). Added Guard 5 with three ordered substitution passes: (a) `"primary TIER-1 focus"` → `"primary event risk"`, (b) `"the/a TIER-1 [noun]"` → `"the key [noun]"`, (c) any remaining bare `TIER-1`/`TIER-2`/`TIER1`/`TIER2` → `"high-impact"`. Verified: exact failing text `"primary TIER-1 focus"` → `"primary event risk"` ✅. Log: `INFO _apply_narrative_guards: TIER-1/2 label stripped from narrative output`.
- **NARRATIVE_FORBIDDEN — added TIER-1/2 pattern:** `r'TIER[-\s]?[12]'` added to the forbidden-language list used for retry detection. If the LLM returns TIER-1 in output, the retry fires before `_apply_narrative_guards` runs — providing two layers: prompt-level retry + deterministic post-processing strip.
- **NARRATIVE_SYSTEM Rule 2 — added internal label prohibition:** Added `FORBIDDEN INTERNAL LABELS` clause explicitly naming `TIER-1`, `TIER-2`, `TIER1`, `TIER2` as forbidden in narrative output, with a wrong/correct example pair. Reduces model tendency to echo the label in the first place.
- **Retry message — added TIER-1 label substitution note:** When the forbidden-language retry fires, the retry prefix now explicitly states that TIER-1/2 labels must not appear in output and gives the correct substitute phrasing.

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js
- **Regime mismatch tooltip:** Added `_narrativeAiRegime` module-level variable to store the regime label from the AI JSON separately from the live-computed regime. When `renderRiskData()` renders the narrative badge and the live score differs from the AI-generated regime (e.g. AI wrote CAUTION but live VIX has since moved to RISK-ON), the badge tooltip now reads `"... · Narrative written under CAUTION (conditions changed since generation)"`. This explains why the narrative text tone may not match the live badge — the narrative was authored under different market conditions. The badge itself always shows the live score (correct behavior, unchanged).

Body: rate differential ÷ HV30, what the ratio means in terms of carry per unit of vol risk. Example: "AUD/CHF +4.75% diff ÷ 8.0% HV30 = 0.59 — earns 0.59% of carry per 1% of annualised vol." No visual change to the header — tooltip attaches to the existing `sb-head` element with `cursor:help`. Guard `_carryTipAttached` prevents re-attachment on the 30-min refresh cycle. The 21:00–22:30 UTC window is fully covered by this live injection for all connected users. Single run at 22:30 UTC is now the authoritative and only scheduled run.

## v7.54.6 (2026-05-02) — fetch_ohlc: clamp H/L after prev_close open correction

### Engine — scripts/fetch_ohlc.py (also site/scripts/fetch_ohlc.py)
- **Root cause — gap sessions produce H < O or L > O:** The FX daily bar pipeline applies a `prev_close open correction` to the full series after 1H aggregation (Bloomberg/TradingView convention: open = prior session's close). This override is correct for body color consistency but it was NOT followed by an H/L clamp. On large-gap sessions — e.g. USD/JPY on 2026-05-01 where the 21:00 UTC session opened at ~157.x but prev_close was 160.184 — the imposed open (160.184) becomes larger than the 1H session high (157.326), producing `H=157.326 < O=160.184`: an impossible candle that Lightweight Charts renders as a zero-body or inverted candle. Audit confirmed: **all 27 FX pairs affected** with multiple invalid candles in the last 10 bars each.
- **Fix applied (two locations):**
  1. `fetch_fx_ohlc_from_1h()` — full series correction loop (line ~401): after `deduped[i]["open"] = deduped[i-1]["close"]`, now adds `high = max(high, new_o)` and `low = min(low, new_o)`.
  2. `fetch_fx_ohlc_from_1h()` — legacy 1D bars correction loop (line ~378): same clamp applied.
- **Semantics — gap candle:** When FX gaps down at the 21:00 UTC session boundary (open > 1H session high), `H = open` means the candle opens at the top and closes lower — a bearish marubozu without an upper wick. When FX gaps up (open < 1H session low), `L = open` gives a bullish marubozu without a lower wick. This is the correct industry representation and matches TradingView/Bloomberg candle rendering for gapped FX sessions.
- **Double-run (21:35 + 22:30 UTC) — not the cause:** The two workflow runs are idempotent (both produce valid data; second overwrites first). The double-run did not cause the H/L violation. The bug existed in both runs identically.

---

## v7.54.5 (2026-05-02) — TIER-1 label leak fix + regime mismatch tooltip

### Engine — scripts/generate_narrative_signals.py
- **Guard 5 (new) — TIER-1/2 internal label strip in `_apply_narrative_guards`:** Gemini occasionally echoes the context-block processing directive `TIER-1` into narrative output (e.g. "the primary TIER-1 focus"). This is a context bleed — the label is a pipeline directive, not client-facing language. Added Guard 5 with three ordered substitution passes: (a) `"primary TIER-1 focus"` → `"primary event risk"`, (b) `"the/a TIER-1 [noun]"` → `"the key [noun]"`, (c) any remaining bare `TIER-1`/`TIER-2`/`TIER1`/`TIER2` → `"high-impact"`. Verified: exact failing text `"primary TIER-1 focus"` → `"primary event risk"`. Log: `INFO _apply_narrative_guards: TIER-1/2 label stripped from narrative output`.
- **NARRATIVE_FORBIDDEN — added TIER-1/2 pattern:** `r'TIER[-\s]?[12]'` added to the forbidden-language list used for retry detection. If Gemini returns TIER-1 in output, the guard fires before `_apply_narrative_guards` runs and triggers a retry with an explicit prohibition message. This provides two layers: prompt-level retry + deterministic post-processing strip.
- **NARRATIVE_SYSTEM Rule 2 — added internal label prohibition:** Added `FORBIDDEN INTERNAL LABELS` clause to Rule 2 explicitly naming `TIER-1`, `TIER-2`, `TIER1`, `TIER2` as forbidden in narrative output, with a wrong/correct example pair. Reduces Gemini's tendency to echo the label in the first place.
- **Retry message — added TIER-1 label note:** When the forbidden-language retry fires, the retry prefix now explicitly states that TIER-1/2 labels must not appear in output and gives the correct substitute phrasing.

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js
- **Regime mismatch tooltip:** Added `_narrativeAiRegime` module-level variable to store the regime label from the AI JSON separately from the live-computed regime. When `renderRiskData()` renders the narrative badge and the live score differs from the AI-generated regime (e.g. AI wrote CAUTION but live VIX has since moved to RISK-ON), the badge tooltip now reads `"... · Narrative written under CAUTION (conditions changed since generation)"`. This explains to the user why the narrative text tone may not match the live badge — the narrative was authored under different market conditions. The badge itself always shows the live score (correct behavior, unchanged).

## v7.54.4 (2026-05-02) — Squeeze guard: broaden squeeze/replace patterns + carry bp diagnosis

### Engine — scripts/generate_narrative_signals.py
- **Squeeze guard fix — `_sq_squeeze_re` too narrow:** Pattern only matched `"squeeze risk"` (with space + "risk"). Gemini wrote `"massive short-squeeze if..."` — hyphenated, no trailing "risk" word — causing the guard to never fire. Broadened to `r'short[- ]squeeze|squeeze\s+risk|squeeze\s+potential'` which catches all three Gemini forms. Verified: exact run text `"leaving the pair vulnerable to a massive short-squeeze if..."` now triggers correctly.
- **Squeeze guard fix — `_sq_replace_re` too narrow:** Replace pattern required trailing `"risk"` word (`short.squeeze\s+risk`). Since `_sq_squeeze_re` now matches `"short-squeeze"` without "risk", the replace pattern must also match without it. Updated to `r'(?:a\s+)?(?:massive\s+|significant\s+)?short[- ]squeeze(?:\s+risk)?'` — `(?:\s+risk)?` makes "risk" optional. Replacement: `"short-squeeze (USD/JPY downside if shorts cover)"`.
- **Carry bp 175bp — root cause confirmed (not a bug):** The `+175bp Fed-ECB` value in narrative is correct per the data files: `rates/USD.json` shows Fed=3.75% and `rates/EUR.json` shows ECB=2.00% → spread = +175bp. The guard correctly does not override it because `abs(175 - 175) = 0 < 20`. Gemini is reading the context block accurately. No fix needed — the guard is working as designed. If the carry value is stale, the fix is to update `rates/USD.json`, not the guard.
- **OIS `mkt: 0% hike priced` for RBA — deployment lag:** The Fix 2 bias/prob guard (v7.54.3) was not yet deployed when this run executed. Once v7.54.3 is deployed, `bias="hike" AND hikeProb=0` will correctly fall through to `"mkt: 100% hold priced"`.

---

## v7.54.3 (2026-05-02) — 3 guard fixes: carry bp pair-anchor, OIS bias/prob mismatch, squeeze crowd pattern

### Engine — scripts/generate_narrative_signals.py
- **Fix 1 — Carry bp guard: pair-anchor pass (narrative guard):** The v7.54.2 carry bp guard only matched hardcoded bp values when Gemini included explicit CB pair names (`Fed-ECB`, `BoE-Fed`, etc.) in the surrounding text. The failing run showed `"+175bp carry disadvantage"` near `"EUR/USD"` with no CB names — the guard silently skipped it. Added a second pass (Pass B) that detects `"[+]Nbp carry [dis]advantage"` within ±150 chars of a pair name (`EUR/USD`, `GBP/USD`, etc.) and replaces with the exact bp from `rates/*.json`. The `dis` prefix is preserved so `"175bp carry disadvantage"` becomes `"+233bp carry disadvantage"` (not `"+233bp carry advantage"`). Expected log: `INFO _apply_narrative_guards: carry bp 175bp → +233bp via pair anchor (EUR/USD)`.
- **Fix 2 — OIS injection: bias/probability mismatch guard:** `meetings.json` can have `bias="hike"` with `hikeProb=0` when market pricing has repriced away from a hike but the bias tag wasn't updated (e.g. RBA after a data miss). Previous code: `if bias == "hike": show "{hikeProb}% hike priced"` → produced `"mkt: 0% hike priced"` (misleading — implies hike is priced but at 0%). Fix: added probability check to all three bias branches. New logic: `if bias=="hike" AND hikeProb > 0` → hike label; `elif bias=="cut" AND cutProb > 0` → cut label; else → hold label. Verified: `bias=hike, hikeProb=0` → `"mkt: 100% hold priced"`.
- **Fix 3 — Squeeze direction guard: crowd pattern + dir pattern + replacement rewrite:** Three sub-fixes. (a) `_sq_crowd_re` expanded from `"crowded short"` only to also match `"net short of -NNN,NNN"` and `"massive short"` — Gemini writes `"COT net short of -88,543"` without the word "crowded", causing the guard to silently skip JPY signals. (b) `_sq_dir_re` was self-matching `"short-squeeze risk"` (the trigger phrase) as a direction hit, preventing the guard from ever firing on Gemini output. Rewrote to only match explicit direction statements: `"USD/JPY downside"`, `"downside if shorts cover"`, `"shorts cover"`. (c) `_sq_replace_re` updated to match `"a massive short-squeeze risk"` / `"significant short-squeeze risk"` / `"short-squeeze risk"` (full phrase), with replacement `"short-squeeze risk (USD/JPY downside if shorts cover)"`. Also fixed: compiled pattern + `flags=` kwarg raises `TypeError` at runtime — removed `flags=_rg.IGNORECASE` from `.sub()` call (flags already set at compile time). Expected log: `INFO _apply_signal_guards squeeze: disambiguated in 'USD/JPY — G8 Bilateral Theme +'`.

---

## v7.54.2 (2026-05-02) — Bug fixes: OIS NoneType crash + narrative carry bp guard

### Engine — scripts/generate_narrative_signals.py
- **Critical crash fix — OIS probability `NoneType` subtraction (line 1447):** `_apply_signal_guards` Guard 5 (OIS injection) read `hikeProb` and `cutProb` from `meetings.json` using `.get("hikeProb", 0)` / `.get("cutProb", 0)`. When the meetings.json for a currency has an explicit `null` JSON value (e.g. `"hikeProb": null`), Python's `.get()` returns `None` — not the `0` default — because the key is present. The subsequent `100 - _hike_prob - _cut_prob` raised `TypeError: unsupported operand type(s) for -: 'int' and 'NoneType'`, crashing the entire run at the signals write step. Fix: changed to `_meet.get("hikeProb") or 0` / `_meet.get("cutProb") or 0` — `or 0` coerces both `None` and falsy values to `0`. Confirmed by run log: `"mkt: 100% hold priced"` for ECB and `"mkt: 40% hike priced"` for BoJ were injected correctly before the crash, so the logic itself is correct — only the None guard was missing.
- **Narrative carry bp guard (Guard 4 in `_apply_narrative_guards`):** Added deterministic post-processing to correct stale bp values in Gemini narrative output. When Gemini writes a hardcoded carry bp value (e.g. `+175bp`) near a CB pair mention (`Fed-ECB`, `Fed-BoE`, etc.) that differs from the actual computed value by ≥20bp, the guard replaces it with the exact bp from `rates/*.json` — the same source as the `CROSS_CARRY` context block. Trigger: run v7.54.0 showed `"a +175bp Fed-ECB carry advantage"` in the narrative despite the context block containing the correct `+233bp` value. Root cause: Gemini reads the context block for signals (which has per-signal rate guards) but narrative generation appears to prioritise training-memory values when the carry block is present but not referenced in a concrete example. The guard fires post-generation and corrects the value before write, logging `INFO _apply_narrative_guards: carry bp +175bp → +233bp (USD/EUR)`.

---

## v7.54.1 (2026-05-02) — Bug fix: _apply_narrative_guards modal regex + guard wiring audit

### Engine — scripts/generate_narrative_signals.py
- **Modal regex bug fix in `_apply_narrative_guards`:** `_VB` pattern used a non-capturing group `(?:...)` but replacement strings used `` backreference — this raised `re.error: invalid group reference` at runtime, silently breaking Guard 2 (modal verb sanitizer) for all Gemini-primary narrative output. Fix: changed `_VB` to a capturing group `(...)` and introduced a `_adv_vb_repl` lambda for the ADV+VB case (where the verb is `m.lastindex`, not ``). Verified: `"may weigh on EUR/USD"` → `"is likely to weigh on EUR/USD"`, `"may further weigh"` → `"is likely to weigh"`, `"could also drive the move"` → `"is likely to drive the move"` all pass. Note: `_apply_signal_guards` (Guard 2) used a capturing group from the start and was unaffected.
- **Guard wiring audit:** Confirmed `_apply_narrative_guards` is wired at the Gemini-primary narrative write point (`narrative = _apply_narrative_guards(narrative)` before `narrative["generated_at"] = ...`). Confirmed `_apply_signal_guards` is wired at the Gemini-primary signals write point (`signals = _apply_signal_guards(signals)` before `_write_signals()`). Groq fallback paths already had guards built-in via `generate_narrative()` / `generate_signals()` — no changes needed there.
- **Stale-date guard verified:** `_apply_signal_guards` Guard 1 correctly catches Gemini-generated dates in both `DD Month` and `Month DD` formats. Tested against actual v7.54.0 run output: `"next ECB meeting on 30 April"` → `"next ECB meeting"`, `"next BoE meeting on 30 April"` → `"next BoE meeting"`, `"next BoC meeting on 29 April"` → `"next BoC meeting"`.

---

## v7.54.0 (2026-05-02) — Architecture inversion: Gemini primary editor, Groq fallback

### Engine — scripts/generate_narrative_signals.py
- **Gemini promoted to primary draft author (narrative + signals):** Previous architecture: Groq → draft → Python guards → Gemini → audit. New architecture: Gemini → draft → Python guards → Groq → fallback only. The inversion is motivated by 3 factors: (1) 4 of 6 institutional-quality gaps identified in the v7.53.8 audit were fixed at data/guard layer — the remaining 2 (squeeze direction logic, geopolitical risk narrative coherence) require contextual reasoning that Gemini handles better than Groq; (2) Gemini's superior instruction-following reduces COT-OIS divergence retries and JSON malformation, cutting expected calls/run from ~4 to ~2; (3) 3 Gemini keys from separate Google accounts = 750 RPD free-tier capacity — sufficient for 24 runs/day with 15× headroom. Fallback chain: Gemini → (exhausted) → Groq → (exhausted) → preserve existing file on disk.
- **`generate_narrative_via_gemini()` promoted to primary path:** Docstring updated from fallback description to primary draft author. `_source` tag set to `"gemini"`.
- **`generate_signals_via_gemini()` promoted to primary path:** Docstring updated from `"emergency fallback when Groq exhausted"` to `"primary draft author (v7.54.0)"`. Log messages updated: `"Gemini signals fallback"` → `"Gemini signals"`. `_source` tag updated from `"gemini-fallback"` to `"gemini"`. This ensures signal provenance in logs and output is accurate.
- **Main module docstring updated:** Header line updated from `"via Groq (llama-3.3-70b-versatile)"` to `"via Gemini (primary editor) with Groq as coherence validator / fallback"`.
- **Startup log updated:** `"Groq keys available"` block now labels the Groq pool as fallback. Gemini key count displayed prominently with `"(primary editor)"` label.

---

## v7.53.8 (2026-05-02) — 5 institutional-quality guards: carry bp, adverb-bridged modals, OIS probability injection, squeeze direction

### Engine — scripts/generate_narrative_signals.py
- **Fix 1+2 — USD major pair carry pre-computed (eliminates hallucinated 175bp):** `CROSS_CARRY` block extended to include all USD major pairs (EUR/USD, GBP/USD, AUD/USD, NZD/USD, USD/CAD, USD/CHF, USD/JPY) listed first, so the LLM always receives the exact bp spread from rates/*.json before seeing any example. Root cause: `CROSS_CARRY` previously only covered JPY and CHF crosses — Groq had no pre-computed carry for EUR/USD and copied the hardcoded `175bp` from the prompt example. With Fed 4.33% vs ECB 2.00%, the actual spread is +233bp; the LLM now reads that number directly from the context block. The carry block note explicitly instructs: `"Never use training-memory rates to compute carry spreads — always use values above."` The prompt example was also changed to remove the hardcoded `175bp` value and reference the data block instead.
- **Fix 3 — Adverb-bridged modal patterns (`may further impact`, `may also influence`):** `_MODAL_SUBS` (pre-Groq) and Guard G (post-Gemini) only matched `may [verb]` with the verb immediately adjacent. The adverb `further`, `also`, `still`, `now`, `soon`, `yet`, `already`, `again`, `ultimately`, `additionally`, `potentially` between `may` and the verb caused the pattern to miss. Added adverb-bridging regex group to all three modal guard locations (pre-Groq narrative, pre-Groq signals, post-Gemini Guard G). Pattern: `may\s+(?:further|also|...)\s+[verb]` → `is likely to [verb]`. Verified: `"may further impact USD direction"` → `"is likely to impact USD direction"`.
- **Fix 4 — OIS probability injection guard:** New post-processing pass that detects signal text containing `"next [CB] meeting"` without an adjacent market-implied probability (e.g., `mkt: 88%`), then injects the OIS probability from `meetings.json`. Result: `"next BoE meeting"` → `"next BoE meeting (mkt: 88% hold priced)"`, `"next BoJ meeting"` → `"next BoJ meeting (mkt: 30% hike priced)"`. Without this, the meeting reference was editorial rather than institutional — Bloomberg always includes the OIS-implied probability alongside a CB meeting reference.
- **Fix 5 — Squeeze direction disambiguation for USD/JPY:** `"crowded short -88.5k sets up squeeze risk"` was directionally ambiguous — a crowded JPY short implies a short-squeeze would rally JPY = USD/JPY downside, but the original signal framed it as a USD/JPY upside thesis. Guard added: when a signal contains both `"crowded short"` and `"squeeze risk"` without an explicit direction note, replaces `"sets up squeeze risk"` with `"sets up short-squeeze risk (USD/JPY downside if shorts cover)"`. Mirrors Bloomberg's standard for disambiguating COT crowding signals.

---

## v7.53.7 (2026-05-02) — Gemini 3 Flash upgrade + key split fix + Gemini draft fallback

### Engine — scripts/generate_narrative_signals.py
- **Model upgrade — Gemini 2.5 Flash → Gemini 3 Flash Preview:** `GEMINI_MODEL` updated from `gemini-2.5-flash` to `gemini-3-flash-preview`. Gemini 3 Flash has superior reasoning and instruction-following vs 2.5 Flash (near-Pro quality, same free-tier quota, lower latency than Pro). Added `GEMINI_MODEL_LITE = "gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview"` as a faster/cheaper option for future use. `call_gemini()` updated to support both model families: Gemini 3.x uses `thinkingLevel="minimal"` (thought signatures required even at minimal); Gemini 2.5.x uses `thinkingBudget=512`. Without this fix, Gemini 3 calls would fail with a bad-request error on the old `thinkingBudget` parameter.
- **Key split bug fix (5-key configuration):** Previous split with 5 keys: `narrative=[0,1,4]` (3 keys) · `signals=[2,3]` (2 keys). Key 4 was allocated to narrative overflow despite signals being the more expensive path (COT retries, repair cycles, safe-haven rewrites). New split: `narrative=[0,1]` (2 keys) · `signals=[2,3,4]` (3 keys). Signals now has 50% more daily budget before exhaustion. Narrative with 2 keys is sufficient — it makes 1-2 calls per run vs signals' 3-5. Root cause of the "signals not activating third key" report: the third key was going to narrative, not signals.
- **Gemini draft fallback for narrative and signals:** When all Groq keys raise `DAILY_LIMIT` or return `[]`, the script now tries Gemini as a draft author instead of immediately falling back to stale data. Two new functions: `generate_narrative_via_gemini()` and `generate_signals_via_gemini()` — each uses the same system prompt as the Groq path so output is structurally identical, then runs through the same Python guards. On normal runs (Groq succeeds) this path never executes and consumes zero Gemini quota. On bad-key days it fires 1-2×/day — well within the 250 RPD free-tier limit. The fallback chain is: Groq → (fail) → Gemini draft → Python guards → write. If Gemini also fails, preserves existing signals on disk as before.

---

 — 6 Python guard completions: weaken modal, CB-closed artefact, drives-the-move, catalyst dedup, vague-impact tail, evidence chip coherence

### Engine — scripts/generate_narrative_signals.py
- **Fix 1 — `_NARR_MODAL_FX_VERBS` extended with FX movement verbs:** `weaken`, `strengthen`, `appreciate`, `depreciate`, `recover`, `retreat`, `advance`, `outperform`, `underperform`, `firm`, `soften`, `ease`, `tighten` added. Root cause: "USD/CAD may weaken" was not captured because `weaken` was missing from the narrative modal whitelist. Guard now converts "may weaken" → "is likely to weaken", "could soften" → "is likely to soften", etc.
- **Fix 2 — `[CB] closed at [rate]%` artefact removed (ADV_SUBS + Guard I):** In closed-market mode Groq interprets `market_closed=True` and writes "BoE closed at 3.75%" — applying a market-session verb to a CB policy rate. Added deterministic regex to ADV_SUBS: `(BoE|BoC|BoJ|RBA|RBNZ|SNB|ECB|Fed|FOMC)\s+closed\s+at` → ` at`. Same pattern mirrored in post-Gemini Guard I. Verified on GBP and CAD signals from last run.
- **Fix 3 — `drives the move` / `drives the pair` filler stripped (ADV_SUBS + Guard I):** "The BoE's 3.75% rate drives the move." was appearing in 3 signals per run (GBP, CAD, EUR). Added to ADV_SUBS: `\s+drives\s+the\s+(move|pair)\.?` → `.`. Pattern appended identically to Guard I for post-Gemini text. The sentence-ending period is preserved.
- **Fix 4 — Catalyst phrase deduplication expanded (ADV_SUBS + Guard I):** Previous dedup only covered `(as the key catalyst) (as the key catalyst)`. Groq was producing `is a key catalyst as the key catalyst` — a half-replaced hybrid when ADV_SUBS matched only the second half. New pattern: `is\s+(?:a|the)\s+key\s+catalyst\s+as\s+the\s+key\s+catalyst` → `is the catalyst`. Also added `(is the catalyst) as the key catalyst` fallback. Both patterns mirrored in Guard I.
- **Fix 5 — `is likely to impact [direction]` vague tail → `determines direction` (ADV_SUBS + Guard I):** Guard G converts "may impact the pair's direction" → "is likely to impact the pair's direction" but the result is still vague filler. Added to ADV_SUBS: `is\s+likely\s+to\s+impact\s+(?:the\s+)?(?:pair's\s+)?(?:direction|outcome|trend|bias)` → `determines direction`. Mirrored in Guard I.
- **Fix 6 — Evidence chip coherence guard:** When the hike-bias coherence fix rewrites signal text to say "hold bias (0% priced for a hike)", Groq's original evidence chip still reads "RBA hike bias: 40% priced" — a direct contradiction. New post-processing pass: for each CB in the signal, if signal text contains `[CB] hold bias` or `(0% priced for a hike)`, any evidence chip matching `[CB] hike bias: X% priced` is rewritten to `[CB] hold bias: 0% hike priced`. Runs immediately before the signal is appended to result.

---



### Engine — scripts/generate_narrative_signals.py
- **Fix 1 — Gemini system prompt: restrict to 4 data classes only:** The prior prompt gave Gemini a `CLASS 5 — SUBSTANDARD INSTITUTIONAL LANGUAGE` task (filler, first-person, vague language) in addition to the 4 factual data classes. This caused Gemini to rewrite signal text even when there was no data error — re-introducing "may", "potential", past dates, and filler phrases that Python had already cleaned. **Root cause:** two systems (Python guards + Gemini Class 5) were doing the same job non-deterministically, creating a loop. **Fix:** removed Class 5 entirely from the Gemini prompt. Gemini now corrects ONLY: (1) COT direction inverted, (2) yield advantage wrong, (3) CB rate/probability confused, (4) directional incoherence. Explicitly instructs Gemini: "NEVER introduce may/could/might/potential/would — keep existing language." Python guards handle all language cleaning deterministically.
- **Fix 2 — Example-driven error prevention in Groq signal prompts:** Added a `COMMON ERRORS — MEMORIZE THESE` section to both `SIGNALS_SYSTEM` (live) and `SIGNALS_SYSTEM_CLOSED` (weekend/closed-market) with 5–6 concrete ERROR/CORRECT pairs for the patterns that recur most in production: stale CB dates, hedge modals, "The mechanism driving the move is", "potential [noun]", "Hike bias (0% priced)" contradiction, and asymmetric/catalyst filler. Positive examples in the prompt are more effective at preventing errors than Python regex guards applied after the fact.
- **Fix 3 — Stale-date regex now catches "and next meeting on [date]":** Guards D (pre-Gemini) and Guard D-post (post-Gemini) used a pattern requiring a CB name immediately before "meeting" — e.g. `BoE meeting on 30 Apr`. The pattern missed "and next meeting on 30 Apr" (Groq's common phrasing when the CB name is earlier in the sentence). Updated pattern: `(?:CB)?\s*meeting` with CB group optional — now catches both forms. The fallback CB label is "CB" when no name is in the match. Verified: BoE/30 Apr, Fed/29 Apr, BoJ/28 Apr all caught; RBA/5 May and RBNZ/27 May preserved.
- **Fix 4 — Modal whitelists extended with 10 missing verbs:** `keep`, `introduce`, `stabilize`, `prevent`, `accelerate`, `delay`, `reinforce`, `amplify`, `reduce`, `increase`, `narrow`, `widen`, `close` added to `_NARR_MODAL_FX_VERBS` (narrative), `_MODAL_SUBS` (pre-Gemini signals), and `_PGG_VERBS` (Guard G post-Gemini). "may keep the pair stable", "could introduce volatility" now converted to "is likely to keep"/"is likely to introduce".
- **Fix 5 — ADV_SUBS extended with 7 new filler patterns:** (a) `"The mechanism driving the move is [the/a]"` → stripped (appeared in EUR/USD, GBP/USD, USD/CAD signals in the last 2 runs); (b) `"potential [decrease|increase|impact|drop|rise|compression|expansion|correction]"` → strips "potential" (broadens the existing potential list); (c) `"affecting [X]'s value"` → stripped from sentence tail (verbose/redundant); (d) `"will be a/the key catalyst"` → `"is the catalyst"`; (e) `"an asymmetric risk"` → `"unwind risk"`; (f) `"considering the [CB]'s [stance]"` → stripped; (g) `"affecting [pair]'s direction/movement"` → stripped. All 7 also added to Guard I (post-Gemini).
- **Fix 6 — RBA/hike-bias contradiction fixed at data layer:** `build_ois_bias_block()` and `_build_gemini_fact_block()` now apply a coherence rule before writing the OIS block: if `bias=hike` and `hikeProb=0`, override `bias_raw` to `"hold"`. This prevents "RBA Hike bias (0% priced)" — a self-contradictory label — from reaching either Groq or Gemini. Same fix applies to `bias=cut` + `cutProb=0`. The meetings.json data is not modified — only the representation in the prompt block.
- **Fix 7 — Guard I (post-Gemini filler) extended to full ADV_SUBS parity:** Guard I previously only covered `potential [shift/carry extension/catalyst]` and `pointing to a`. Expanded to cover all 7 new ADV_SUBS patterns above, making pre- and post-Gemini filler stripping fully symmetric.

---

## v7.53.4 (2026-05-02) — Stale-date regex, post-Gemini modal pass, modal verb whitelist + FTSE guard

### Engine — scripts/generate_narrative_signals.py
- **Fix 1 — Stale-date regex misses `meeting is on [date]`:** Guard D (stale past-date) used the pattern `meeting(?:\s+on)?` which only matched "meeting on 28 Apr" and "meeting 28 Apr" — not "meeting is on 28 Apr" (Gemini's preferred phrasing). All three stale dates in the run (BoJ 28 Apr, BoE 30 Apr, Fed 29 Apr) passed through undetected. Fixed pattern: `meeting(?:\s+is)?\s+(?:on\s+)?` — now handles "meeting on", "meeting is on", "meeting 28 Apr" in both the pre-Gemini signal guard and the post-Gemini Guard D.
- **Fix 2 — Missing verbs in modal whitelists:** `impact`, `resolve`, `determine`, `affect`, `shape`, `dominate`, `dictate`, `guide` were absent from both the narrative and signal modal verb whitelists. "geopolitical risks may dominate", "meeting may impact", "meeting may resolve" — all passed through unchecked. Added to both `_NARR_MODAL_FX_VERBS` and `_MODAL_SUBS` in the signal path.
- **Fix 3 — Post-Gemini has no modal pass (Guard G):** The pre-Gemini modal sanitizer ran on Groq output, but Gemini rewrote signals and could re-introduce "may impact", "may resolve" etc. The post-Gemini loop had guards A–F but no modal pass. Added Guard G: applies the full expanded modal verb substitution (`may/could/might [verb]` → `is likely to [verb]`, `may be` → `is likely to be`) to every signal in the post-Gemini loop.
- **Fix 4 — FTSE/named-entity correlation claims (Guard H):** Gemini introduced "correlation break with FTSE 100 is a secondary driver" in GBP/USD — the hallucination guard cannot catch this because it has no X.XX% number to compare. Added Guard H: detects "correlation/corr with/to [FTSE/DAX/Nikkei/S&P 500/CAC 40/ASX 200]" in Gemini output that wasn't in the original Groq signal, and strips the entire clause. Applied in the post-Gemini loop.
- **Fix 5 — Extended `_ADV_SUBS` filler patterns:** Added `potential shift`, `potential catalyst`, `potential divergence`, `potential carry extension` to the "potential [X]" stripper (was only covering unwind/squeeze/reversal/rally/decline/move). Added `pointing to a [potential] X` → strip entire editorial wrapper (appeared in NZD/USD signal in this run). Guard I in the post-Gemini loop also applies the same expansions to Gemini output independently.

---

## v7.53.3 (2026-05-02) — 7-fix precision pass: grammar, stale dates, carry counterpart, filler

### Engine — scripts/generate_narrative_signals.py
- **Fix 1 — Narrative `may be [verb]` gap:** The narrative modal sanitizer covered `may [verb]` but missed `may be [verb/adj]` — "AUD/USD may be supported" passed through unchanged. Added generic `may be` → `is likely to be` fallback rule as the last pattern in the narrative modal guard.
- **Fix 2 — Subject-verb agreement (narrative + signals):** Modal substitution was producing grammatically incorrect output: "tensions is likely to weigh" (plural subject + singular predicate). Added a post-modal pass that detects plural FX nouns (tensions, pressures, flows, movements, developments, risks, etc.) within the same clause and corrects `is likely to` → `are likely to`. Applied in both the closed-market narrative path and the signal-level modal sanitizer.
- **Fix 3 — Closed-market safe-haven guard was unreachable:** The live-path safe-haven guard (replacing "safe-haven USD bid" with "geopolitical risk premium" in MIXED/RISK-ON regimes) sits after the closed-market `return` — making it completely unreachable for weekend/after-hours narratives. Added an equivalent guard directly inside the closed-market path, before the `return`. The last run's "safe-haven USD bid" in the narrative would now be caught here.
- **Fix 4 — Stale past meeting dates in signals:** Groq sometimes writes "next BoJ meeting on 28 Apr" using a date already in the past (the previous meeting date from meetings.json). No Python guard existed for this. Added a deterministic stale-date guard in the signal post-processing loop: detects `[optional next] [CB] meeting on [D Mon]` patterns where the parsed date is before UTC today, and replaces with `next [CB] meeting` (date stripped). Applied at Groq output time; mirrored in the post-Gemini re-sanitization pass so Gemini re-introductions are also caught. The regex consumes the leading "next" to avoid "next next BoJ meeting" double-prefix.
- **Fix 5 — Wrong carry counterpart for USD counter-pairs:** Gemini occasionally substitutes BoJ as the carry counterpart in non-JPY USD pairs — e.g. "GBP/USD carry advantage over the BoJ (BoE 3.75% vs BoJ 0.75%)" when the correct counterpart is Fed. Added a post-Gemini guard: for signals with EUR/USD, GBP/USD, AUD/USD, or NZD/USD in the title, strips parenthesized `[non-BoJ CB] X.XX% vs BoJ Y.YY%` carry clauses and corrects "carry advantage over the BoJ" → "carry advantage over the Fed".
- **Fix 6 — Filler phrases added to advisory stripper:** Added 5 patterns to `_ADV_SUBS`: `especially considering` → `given`; `creating an asymmetric squeeze/unwind risk` → `creating squeeze/unwind risk` (strip "asymmetric" — every squeeze is by definition asymmetric); `is likely to be the catalyst for this [X]` → `is the catalyst for [X]` (shorter, more direct); `potential unwind/squeeze/reversal/rally` → strips "potential" (hedging language inappropriate in institutional signals); `will be important for resolving` → `determines`.
- **Fix 7 — Subject-verb agreement in post-Gemini pass:** Added Guard F to the post-Gemini re-sanitization loop that applies the same plural-subject fix (`movements/tensions is likely to` → `are likely to`) on Gemini's output, since Gemini can re-introduce the pattern after Python's pre-Gemini pass has already corrected it.

---

## v7.53.2 (2026-05-01) — Regime MIXED→RISK-ON promotion + narrative modal guard

### Engine — scripts/generate_narrative_signals.py
- **Root cause (MIXED vs RISK-ON mismatch):** `validate_regime()` clamped upward (MIXED→CAUTION/RISK-OFF) but never promoted downward (MIXED→RISK-ON). When VIX=16.99, SPX=+0.29%, Gold=-0.09%, MOVE=70.4 — score=0, `correct_regime=RISK-ON` — but the function returned "MIXED" unchanged because no rule covered that direction. The dashboard Risk Monitor showed RISK-ON (from a separate calculation), creating a visible contradiction. **Fix:** added `if regime in ("MIXED","NEUTRAL") and correct_regime == "RISK-ON": return correct_regime`. The log now shows `INFO Closed-market regime corrected: MIXED → RISK-ON`.
- **Root cause ("may" in narrative):** The closed-market narrative path applied `_NARR_VERB_SUBS` (catches "drives/pushes/sends" active verbs) and `_fix_potentially` (catches "potentially Xing"), but had no guard against uncertainty modals ("may/could/might"). "USD/JPY may extend gains" and similar phrases were passing through unchecked. **Fix:** added `Narrative modal sanitizer` immediately after `_NARR_VERB_SUBS` in the closed-market path. Replaces `may/could/might [FX-verb]` → `is likely to [verb]` using the same verb whitelist as the signals modal guard. Excludes "May" as a month name via negative lookbehind on digit prefix.

---

## v7.53.1 (2026-05-01) — Post-Gemini re-sanitization + narrative safe-haven regex fix

### Engine — scripts/generate_narrative_signals.py
- **Root cause (hike-prob contradiction surviving Gemini):** Python guards run inside `generate_signals()` on Groq output, then Gemini re-writes signals afterwards — re-introducing errors the guards had already fixed. In the last run AUD/USD showed "hike bias (0% priced)" in text vs "40% priced" in evidence because Groq wrote 40%, Python corrected it, Gemini reverted to 0%.
- **Fix 1 — Post-Gemini re-sanitization pass:** After the hallucination guard and before `return merged`, a second sanitization pass re-applies the three guards most likely to be undone by Gemini: (A) hike/cut probability guard (CB name + percentage anchored regex, same logic as the Groq-side guard), (B) COT squeeze-risk terminology guard (replaces "squeeze risk for [CCY]" with "crowded-short unwind risk" when that CCY has net < −10k, because "squeeze risk for JPY" in a USD/JPY upside signal is a directional contradiction), (C) WTI price-drop guard (if "WTI crude oil price drop" appears but WTI 1D% is not negative, replaces "price drop" with "price move").
- **Fix 2 — Narrative safe-haven guard regex:** The guard fired on `safe-haven bid` but missed `safe-haven USD bid` (word between hyphenated term and "bid"). Pattern updated to `safe[\s\-]haven(?:\s+\w+){0,2}\s+bid` to cover 0–2 intervening words. Guard also extended from `regime == "MIXED"` to `regime in ("MIXED", "RISK-ON", "RISK_ON")` — safe-haven framing is inappropriate in both.
- **Fix 3 — Gemini CLASS 4 prompt clarification:** Added squeeze-risk terminology explanation to the DIRECTIONAL INCOHERENCE class: "crowded-short unwind risk" = short-squeeze risk = pair moves against the short = USD/JPY DOWN. "Squeeze risk for JPY reinforces USD/JPY upside" is a contradiction. Correct framing for a USD/JPY upside thesis: "crowded JPY short creates potential carry extension."

---

## v7.53.0 (2026-05-01) — Gemini audit: data quarantine + hallucination guard

### Engine — scripts/generate_narrative_signals.py
- **Root cause (hallucinated values):** `audit_signals_with_gemini()` passed the full 11k-char context to Gemini. Gemini would "find" numbers anywhere in the block (G8 correlations, cross-asset data, headlines) and introduce them into signal text, or fall back to training memory when confused (e.g. "JGB 2.42%", "GBP/FTSE +0.327"). The previous "DO NOT ADD" instruction was insufficient.
- **Fix 1 — `_build_gemini_fact_block()`:** New function that builds a compact (~40-line), structured fact-check block containing only the values Gemini needs: CB rates + next meeting date + hike/cut probabilities, bond yields, COT net positions + direction flags, and FX spot levels. Gemini receives this instead of the full context. Context shrinks from 11k to ~1.5k chars, dramatically reducing the search space for hallucination.
- **Fix 2 — Strengthened `_GEMINI_AUDIT_SYSTEM` prompt:** Replaced generic "NEVER introduce data" rule with a STRICT DATA QUARANTINE section listing 6 explicit prohibitions. Added CLASS 4 — DIRECTIONAL INCOHERENCE: detects signals that state two opposite directions (e.g. "yield gap limits JPY upside … sustained JPY bid") and instructs Gemini to keep the dominant driver and delete the contradiction without adding content. Added explicit past-date handling: if a CB meeting date in the block is before CURRENT UTC DATE, write "next [CB] meeting" without a date.
- **Fix 3 — Post-audit hallucination guard:** After Gemini returns corrected signals, a Python validator checks every X.XX% value introduced by Gemini against an authoritative set built from `rates/*.json`, `extended-data/*.json`, and `meetings-data/meetings.json`. Any value not in the set and not present in the original signal is flagged; that signal is reverted to the Groq original with a WARNING log. Prevents hallucinated yields/correlations from reaching production regardless of prompt compliance.

---

## v7.52.2 (2026-05-02) — Suppress redundant regime-summary signals

### Engine — scripts/generate_narrative_signals.py
- **Root cause:** `SIGNALS_SYSTEM_CLOSED` described `"info" — regime context, COT positioning background` — the LLM interpreted this as an invitation to generate a standalone regime/VIX/G8-scorecard summary card, duplicating what the Narrative panel already covers. Additionally, the regime value in that signal was inconsistent with the risk monitor (MIXED vs RISK-ON), creating contradictory output.
- **Prompt fix (`SIGNALS_SYSTEM_CLOSED`):** Replaced the permissive `info` description with an explicit NEVER rule: regime, VIX, G8 scorecard rankings, and retail sentiment are context layers — they belong fused inside a pair-specific signal, not as a standalone card. Added a WRONG/CORRECT example pair.
- **Python filter (second line of defense):** Added `Regime-summary signal suppression` block at the top of the signal filter loop. Detection: `priority == "info" AND empty title AND any regime keyword present` (15 keywords covering "current regime", "vix at", "g8 scorecard", "retail traders", etc.). Logs `INFO Regime-summary filter: dropped redundant context signal`. Falls into `dropped_reasons` so the retry logic can request a replacement pair signal if needed.
- **Effect on the run that triggered this fix:** The `[INFO] "" — "The current regime is mixed..."` signal would have been suppressed at filter time, keeping the 6-signal cap filled with 6 actual pair signals.

---

## v7.52.1 (2026-05-02) — Hotfix: hike-prob guard cross-CB contamination + duplicate phrases + empty title

### Engine — scripts/generate_narrative_signals.py
- **hike-prob guard (bug fix):** Regex pattern `(\d{1,3})%\s+(?:priced|probability)` was too broad — it matched any percentage in the signal text and attributed it to whichever CB was iterating at that moment. Root cause: the second branch of the alternation had no CB name anchor. Effect: `BoC 40% → 0%`, `BoJ 0% → 40%`, `Fed 40% → 0%` all firing on the AUD/USD signal. Fix: all three regex branches now require the CB name to appear explicitly near the percentage (before or after). Tested: `BoC`/`BoJ` no longer match an RBA signal; `RBA` correctly matches `RBA hike bias (40% priced)`.
- **Advisory stripper — duplicate phrase guard (new patterns):** Added three patterns to collapse adjacent duplicate phrases produced when `for this trade → as the key catalyst` replacement runs on a sentence that already contains `as the key catalyst`. Catches `"as the key catalyst as the key catalyst"` → `"as the key catalyst"`.
- **Empty title guard (new):** Signals with `title: ""` are now caught before appending to results. Fallback derives the title from the first FX pair found in the signal text (e.g. `"EUR/USD — Market Update"`), or `"FX Market Update"` if no pair is found. Logs a WARNING. Prevents blank signal cards in the frontend.

### .github/workflows/generate-ai-narrative.yml
- Added `GEMINI_API_KEY`, `GEMINI_API_KEY_2`, `GEMINI_API_KEY_5` to the `env:` block of the run step — required for `load_gemini_keys()` to pick up secrets. Updated the secrets documentation comment at the top of the file.
