## v7.16.19 (2026-04-11) — Net Position bars use single identity color (LF blue)

### Frontend — assets/cot-modal-chart.js — the same blue used for Leveraged Funds in the Participants chart) instead of red/green by value sign. Positive bars render at 85% opacity, negative at 35% — consistent with Bloomberg/Refinitiv COT positioning charts where color identifies the series, not the value sign. The y-axis already conveys positive/negative unambiguously.
- **Cache bust:** `index.html` script tag bumped to `v=7.16.19`.

---

## v7.16.15 (2026-04-11) — Fix: CSS !important overrides Chart.js inline canvas styles

### Frontend — assets/cot-modal-chart.js
- **Root cause confirmed:** Chart.js sets `style="height:167px;width:335px"` directly on the `<canvas>` element after construction, locking it at the initial (wrong) size regardless of the container height. The JS height calculation was correct (367px on the container) but Chart.js inline styles won. Fix: CSS rule `#c-net, #c-split { width:100% !important; height:100% !important }` overrides the Chart.js inline style, forcing the canvas to fill the `.cot-chart-area` container at its correct JS-computed height.

### Frontend — index.html
- Bumped `cot-modal-chart.js` query version to `v=7.16.15` to bust browser cache.

---

## v7.16.14 (2026-04-11) — Fix: replace canvas element on rebuild to clear stale dimensions

### Frontend — assets/cot-modal-chart.js
- **Root cause:** After `chart.destroy()`, Chart.js leaves stale `width`/`height` attributes on the `<canvas>` element. When a new Chart instance is created on the same canvas, it inherits those stale pixel dimensions instead of measuring the container. Fix: after destroying the old chart, replace the `<canvas>` with a fresh `document.createElement('canvas')` with the same `id`. The new canvas has no attributes, so Chart.js measures the container cleanly and renders at the correct height.
- **Also removed:** The `chart.resize()` shortcut added in v7.16.13 — no longer needed since the canvas is always fresh on rebuild.

---

## v7.16.13 (2026-04-11) — Fix: chart.resize() after height set — replaces rebuild

### Frontend — assets/cot-modal-chart.js
- **Root cause confirmed via DevTools:** Chart.js built the canvas at 167px (default 2:1 aspect ratio) when the container had no height yet. After that, `built[tabId]=true` prevented rebuilding, so the chart stayed at 167px regardless of the container growing to 367px. Fix: after setting `chartArea.style.height`, call `Chart.getChart(canvas).resize(w, h)` directly if a chart instance already exists — this forces an immediate re-render at the correct dimensions without destroying and recreating the chart.

---

## v7.16.12 (2026-04-11) — Fix: dynamic DOM height measurement for Net/Long-Short charts on mobile

### Frontend — assets/cot-modal-chart.js
- **Chart fill on mobile:** Replaced hardcoded `used` height calculation (which assumed desktop padding values) with a fully dynamic DOM measurement. `cotTab()` now sums the actual `paddingTop + paddingBottom + borderTopWidth + borderBottomWidth` of `.cot-cw` via `getComputedStyle`, plus the measured height of all sibling elements (e.g. `.cot-ct` label) and the body's own padding. This means the calculation is correct at all screen sizes — desktop, mobile, and any custom breakpoint — without hardcoded pixel offsets.
- **CSS cleanup:** Removed `flex:1;min-height:0` from `#p-net.on .cot-cw > .cot-chart-area` and `#p-split.on` equivalent. These CSS rules conflicted with the JS-set `style.height` in px; now the chart-area has only `position:relative` and is sized exclusively by JS.

---

## v7.16.11 (2026-04-11) — Fix: chart fill via rAF+getBoundingClientRect; Participants DOM legend

### Frontend — assets/cot-modal-chart.js
- **Fix: chart height measurement uses getBoundingClientRect after double rAF** — replaced setTimeout(80ms) with requestAnimationFrame x2, which fires after the browser has painted the panel visible. Uses getBoundingClientRect() (returns actual rendered px, not 0) to compute exact available height and sets it on chartArea before Chart.js builds.
- **Fix: buildChart always rebuilds for net/split tabs** — removed the built[tabId] short-circuit for chart tabs so the height is always re-measured and the chart always builds at the correct size.
- **Fix: Participants legend moved from canvas to DOM** — replaced the custom afterDraw canvas-drawing legend (which overlapped chart data) with an HTML flex row above the chart area. Each item is a colored line swatch + label text. Chart top padding reduced to 4px.
- **Fix: Participants chart height increased to 300px** for better data visibility.

---

## v7.16.10 (2026-04-11) — Hotfix: SyntaxError from duplicate const body declaration

### Frontend — assets/cot-modal-chart.js
- **Hotfix: modal not opening** — a duplicate `const body` declaration in `cotTab()` (introduced when merging two edits in v7.16.8 and v7.16.9) caused a SyntaxError that crashed the entire JS file, reverting the page to the TradingView fallback. Removed the duplicate block; function now has a single `const body` and `const isChartTab`.

---

## v7.16.9 (2026-04-11) — Fix: chart height via JS measurement; Participants lines solid

### Frontend — assets/cot-modal-chart.js
- **Fix: Net Position and Long/Short charts now fill available height via JS measurement** — abandoned the flex-fill CSS approach entirely (Chart.js cannot measure a flex:1 container reliably across browsers). Instead, cotTab() now calculates the exact available pixel height by measuring bodyEl.clientHeight minus all padding, header, and label offsets, then sets that value directly as chartArea.style.height before calling buildChart(). This is guaranteed to work on all browsers.
- **Fix: Participants lines all solid** — removed borderDash from Asset Managers and Dealers datasets. All three participant lines now render as solid lines matching the LF style, differentiated only by color (blue/orange/red). Inline legend drawing also simplified.

---

## v7.16.8 (2026-04-11) — Fix: chart fill root cause — lazy build, Participants legend overlap

### Frontend — assets/cot-modal-chart.js
- **Fix: root cause of chart not filling height** — the pre-build `setTimeout(() => buildChart('net'), 60)` was constructing the Net chart while its panel was hidden (`display:none`) and before `cot-body--chart` class was set. Chart.js measured a height of 0 at build time and that size was locked. Removed the pre-build entirely; all charts now build lazily on the first tab click, when the panel is visible and the body class is already applied.
- **Fix: tab build delay increased to 80ms** to ensure the DOM paint cycle completes (panel visible + overflow-y changed) before Chart.js measures the canvas.
- **Fix: Participants inline legend overlapping chart data** — top padding increased from 38px to 44px, providing clearance for a two-line legend wrap (16px line-height + 12px margin above data area).

---

## v7.16.7 (2026-04-11) — Fix: chart fill on mobile using JS class toggle instead of :has()

### Frontend — assets/cot-modal-chart.js
- **Fix: Net Position and Long/Short charts now fill full height on all mobile browsers** — replaced the CSS `:has()` selector (unreliable on older WebKit/mobile browsers) with a JS class toggle. `cotTab()` now adds/removes `cot-body--chart` on `#cot-m-body` when switching to/from chart tabs. The CSS rule `#cot-m-body.cot-body--chart { overflow-y:hidden }` is applied both in base styles and in the mobile media query, ensuring flex-fill propagates height to the canvas on all browsers.
- **Fix: same overflow-y fix applied to desktop** — the root cause was not mobile-specific; added the class-based rule outside the media query so desktop chart tabs also fill correctly.

---

## v7.16.6 (2026-04-11) — Fix: COT modal mobile polish — chart fill, table overflow, legend wrap

### Frontend — assets/cot-modal-chart.js
- **Fix: Net Position and Long/Short charts fill full height on mobile** — added `:has()` selector to switch body to `overflow-y:hidden` when a single-chart panel is active, allowing the flex-fill layout to propagate height to the canvas correctly.
- **Fix: Participants inline legend now wraps to second line on narrow screens** — the custom afterDraw legend plugin now tracks available horizontal space and wraps items to a new line if they would overflow chartArea.right, preventing "Dealers" from being clipped on mobile.
- **Fix: Participants chart padding increased to 38px top** to accommodate potential two-line legend wrap without overlapping data.
- **Fix: Overview Participants table cells no longer wrap text** — added `white-space:nowrap` to all mobile table cells; table scrolls horizontally via touch scroll.
- **Fix: Subtitle line truncated on mobile** to prevent second-line wrap in header.

## v7.16.5 (2026-04-11) — Fix: COT modal mobile layout — bottom sheet, compact header, responsive tables

### Frontend — assets/cot-modal-chart.js
- **Fix: modal on mobile now uses bottom sheet pattern** — on viewports ≤600px the modal slides up from the bottom at 92vh, full width, with rounded top corners and no bottom border. This matches Bloomberg/TradingView mobile conventions and gives charts maximum vertical space.
- **Fix: title no longer wraps on mobile** — header padding reduced and title constrained to single line with text-overflow:ellipsis.
- **Fix: metrics strip compact on mobile** — 3-column layout with reduced cell padding and smaller font sizes (8–11px) so the strip takes minimal vertical space.
- **Fix: all 5 tabs visible on mobile** — tab font reduced to 10px and padding to 9px so the full tab bar fits without scrolling.

- **Fix: History table scrolls horizontally on mobile** — min-width:560px with reduced font/padding so all 9 columns remain readable.

## v7.16.4 (2026-04-11) — Fix: Participants native legend truly hidden, no right dead space

### Frontend — assets/cot-modal-chart.js
- **Fix: Chart.js native legend was not being suppressed in Participants** — root cause was that `{ ...cfg, plugins: [inlineLegend] }` spread overwrites the entire `cfg.plugins` options object with the plugins array, making Chart.js ignore `legend: { display: false }` and render the native legend on the right. Fixed by passing a proper `options.plugins.legend: { display: false }` at the options level and `plugins: [inlineLegend]` at the top-level Chart config, matching the correct Chart.js API separation.

---

## v7.16.3 (2026-04-11) — Fix: COT modal chart sizing — Net/Split fill body, Participants uses fixed chart height

### Frontend — assets/cot-modal-chart.js
- **Fix: Net Position and Long/Short charts now fill the entire body area** — switched canvas wrappers to use `cot-chart-area` class with `flex:1;min-height:0;position:relative` so Chart.js can measure and fill all available vertical space. Previously the wrapper `<div>` had no height, so Chart.js defaulted to a small fallback height.
- **Fix: Participants chart no longer overlaps description text** — replaced the `height:300px` inline style with a `cot-chart-area` class at `height:260px`, and made the panel `overflow-y:auto` so the description block scrolls below the chart cleanly without overlap.
- **Fix: CSS rules for chart panels reorganised** — removed the shared rule that incorrectly included `#p-participants.on .cot-cw:first-child` in the flex-fill group (Participants needs a fixed chart height, not a fill layout).

---



### Frontend — assets/cot-modal-chart.js
- **Fix: modal no longer changes size between tabs** — replaced `max-height:90vh` (which caused the modal to grow/shrink with content) with `height:min(680px,90vh)`. The modal now has a fixed footprint; all panels fill the same space.
- **Fix: body flex layout hardened** — body is `flex:1;min-height:0` so it always fills exactly the remaining height between tabs and modal bottom. All panels are `flex:1` so they expand to fill, never pushing the modal taller.
- **Fix: Participants right-side dead space eliminated** — Chart.js native legend was causing ~150px of horizontal space loss even when positioned `top`. Replaced with a custom `afterDraw` plugin that draws the legend inline inside the chart canvas (top-left, above the plot area). The chart data area now uses 100% of the canvas width.
- **Fix: tab scrollbar hidden** — tab bar overflow scrollbar hidden via `scrollbar-width:none` (cosmetic, tabs still scrollable on small screens).

The modal body is now a flex column; single-chart panels (`#p-net`, `#p-split`) are `display:flex;flex:1` so the chart fills all available vertical space regardless of viewport height or data range. Multi-content panels (Overview, Participants, History) retain their block/scroll behaviour.

## v7.15.9 (2026-04-11) — Fix: COT modal UI bugs — scrollbars, chart colors, Y-axis overflow, tooltip

### Frontend — assets/cot-modal-chart.js
- **Fix: scrollbars now match site theme** — replaced default OS white scrollbar with thin dark scrollbar (rgba(255,255,255,.12) thumb, transparent track) on both the modal body and the History horizontal scroll. Uses `scrollbar-width:thin` + `scrollbar-color` (Firefox) and `::webkit-scrollbar` (Chrome/Safari).

## v7.15.7 (2026-04-11) — Fix: backfill diagnostic output to identify CSV market name format

### Engine — scripts/cot_backfill_52w.py (site repo root)
- **Fix: date parser extended** — added YYYY-MM-DD (ISO), YYYYMMDD, and YYMMDD formats to parse_date_col(). The previous version only tried MM/DD/YYYY which failed on col[2] = Report_Date_as_YYYY-MM-DD.
- **Fix: currency match now falls back to col[1]** — if col[2] date parse fails, tries col[1] (As_of_Date_in_Form_YYMMDD) as fallback.
- **Diagnostic added** — parse_annual_csv() now prints the first 8 unique Market_and_Exchange_Names values with their CCY match result, plus the raw/parsed date from the first data row. This output will reveal the exact string format on the next run so the patterns can be corrected if still failing.
- **Failure counters added** — date_fail and int_fail counters now printed in verbose mode to distinguish which check is dropping rows.

---

## v7.15.6 (2026-04-11) — Fix: backfill script parser rewritten for CFTC annual CSV format

### Engine — scripts/cot_backfill_52w.py (site repo root)
- **Fix: parser completely rewritten from HTML block parser to CSV parser** — the previous version used `extract_tff_block()` which searched for a `Positions` keyword line, the format used by the CFTC weekly HTML page (`financial_lof.htm`). The annual ZIP files (`com_fin_txt_YYYY.zip`) are comma-separated text files with a completely different column layout. The script now uses Pythonβ€˜s `csv` module and reads columns by index: `Lev_Money_Positions_Long_All` (col 14) and `Lev_Money_Positions_Short_All` (col 15). This is why 53 occurrences were found (currency name matched) but 0 records extracted (the `Positions` keyword never appears in CSV format).
- **Root cause confirmed:** annual ZIP β‰  weekly HTML. Two entirely different data formats from CFTC.
- **Parser now handles:** header row detection, `Report_Date_as_MM_DD_YYYY` column for weekEnding, currency matching by substring in `Market_and_Exchange_Names`, and all three participant groups (Leveraged Funds, Asset Manager, Dealer) from their respective column indices.

---

## v7.15.5 (2026-04-10) — Frontend: WoW Δ and Net%OI now derived from history when absent from JSON root

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js
- **Fix: WoW Δ and Net%OI showing `—` despite 2 weeks of history** — the workflow that ran on 2026-04-11 used the pre-v7.15.1 code and produced JSON files without `wowNetChange` or `levNetPctOI` in the root. The frontend was reading only those root fields. Both the COT panel renderer and the `prefetchCOT()` / `COT_DATA_CACHE` now compute these values from `history` as a fallback when the root fields are absent: `wowNetChange = currentNet − history[-2].levNet`; `levNetPctOI = round(net / (long + short) × 100, 1)`. From the next workflow run onwards the root fields will be present and the fallback becomes inert.
- **Fix: WoW Δ formatting** — `wowStr` was incorrectly prepending `+` twice for values ≥ 1000 (`'+' + Math.round(wow / 1000) + 'k'`). Removed redundant sign prefix; sign is now applied once via the leading `(wow > 0 ? '+' : '')`.

Data updated to week ending 2026-04-07. WoW Δ and Net%OI shown as `—` (correct — only 2 weeks of history, these populate from week 3 onwards). TV link column removed (not present in live panel). Removed old v7.13.0 comment tag.
- **Fix: panel description updated** — text now lists all 8 currencies (AUD, CAD, CHF, EUR, GBP, JPY, NZD, USD) and all 8 columns including WoW Δ and Net%OI.

### Guides — guide-dashboard.html
- **Fix: Bloomberg and JP Morgan references removed** — three occurrences: (1) "Bloomberg/Reuters ISO hierarchy" → "standard ISO currency hierarchy" in cross pairs description; (2) "Bloomberg's Carry Monitor and JP Morgan GBI" → removed, carry-to-vol ratio described as industry-standard without attribution; (3) "Consistent with Bloomberg Terminal convention" → removed, 90-day reset described neutrally.

- **Fix: Net column had no tooltip** — `.cot-net` span was rendered without a `title` attribute. Added tooltip: "LF net contracts (longs minus shorts). Positive = net long speculative positioning; negative = net short. Primary directional signal from CFTC Disaggregated TFF report."

## v7.15.2 (2026-04-10) — CSS: OI arrow spacing fix in COT panel

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.css

## v7.15.1 (2026-04-10) — Workflow: wowNetChange and levOI now written to JSON root

### Engine — .github/workflows/update-cot-cftc-all.yml
- **Fix: `wowNetChange`, `levOI`, `levNetPctOI` now saved to JSON root** — the v7.15.0 entry added these fields to each history snapshot but omitted them from the root `data` dict that the frontend reads. Added all three to `save()`. `levNetPctOI` is computed as `round(lev_net / lev_oi * 100, 1)`. `null` on first run (single week of history, no prior week for WoW).
- **Fix: history comment updated** — comment still referenced 26 weeks; corrected to 52 weeks. History schema updated to document `levOI` and `wowNetChange` fields added in v7.15.0.
- **Fix: print statement extended** — `SAVED` line now shows `WoW=±N,NNN` (or `WoW=n/a(first week)`) alongside existing fields for easier run log inspection.
- **Note on `levNetPctOI` methodology** — uses LF OI (long+short) as denominator instead of total market OI. This is a proxy; full total-OI parsing requires additional HTML extraction. Adequately cross-currency comparable at this stage given LF share of FX market OI is consistent across pairs.

---



### Engine — .github/workflows/update-cot-cftc-all.yml
- **New: `extract_total_oi()` function** — parses total market Open Interest from the TFF long report header ("Open Interest is NNN,NNN"). Stored as `totalOpenInterest` in each currency JSON. Required for cross-currency net normalisation.
- **New: `wowNetChange`** — week-over-week change in LF net contracts, computed as `current_net − prior_week_net` using the chronologically last entry of `existing_history` before deduplication. Primary momentum signal in institutional COT analysis. `null` on first run (no prior week). Stored in both the root JSON and each history snapshot.
- **New: `levNetPctOI`** — LF net as a percentage of total market Open Interest (`net / totalOpenInterest × 100`). Bloomberg equivalent: "Net % OI". Normalises positioning across currencies with different contract sizes (EUR = 125k, JPY = 12.5M contracts). Stored in root JSON.
- **Fix: history retention extended from 26 to 52 weeks** — enables z-score and percentile calculations once sufficient history accumulates.
- **Fix: history snapshots now include `totalOI` and `wowNetChange`** — frontend can recalculate these metrics over time for trend analysis.

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js
- **Fix: `history[1]` bug (two locations)** — `fetchCOTData()` and `prefetchCOT()` both used `history[1]` to find the prior week, but history is sorted chronologically oldest→newest, making `history[1]` the second-oldest entry (up to 52 weeks ago). Corrected to `history[history.length - 2]` (second-to-last = prior week). Affects OI arrow direction in the COT panel and OI delta in the pair detail popup.
- **New: WoW Δ column** — week-over-week change in LF net contracts shown in the COT panel. Coloured green (positive) / red (negative). Shows `—` until two weeks of history are available. Primary Bloomberg COT signal.
- **New: Net%OI column** — LF net as % of total market OI shown in the COT panel. Coloured green/red. Shows `—` until `totalOpenInterest` is captured by the updated workflow.
- **New: `COT_DATA_CACHE` extended** — now stores `wowNetChange`, `totalOI`, `levNetPctOI` per currency for use in the pair detail popup.
- **New: pair detail popup COT section extended** — adds "LF WoW Δ" and "Net % OI" cells with tooltips explaining institutional interpretation. Both show `—` gracefully until data is available.

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.css
- **New: `.cot-wow`** — WoW Δ cell: monospace, right-aligned, coloured via global `.up`/`.down`/`.flat`.
- **New: `.cot-pcoi`** — Net%OI cell: monospace, right-aligned.
- **Updated: `.cot-row` grid** — expanded from 6 to 8 columns (`36px 1fr 42px 64px 52px 14px 46px 68px`) to accommodate WoW Δ and Net%OI.

### Frontend — index.html
- **Updated: COT panel column headers** — added "WoW Δ" and "Net%OI" headers with descriptive `title` attributes matching the new grid layout.

---

## v7.14.6 (2026-04-10) — Feature: COT positioning rows sorted by Long% descending

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js
- **Sort COT rows by Long% descending:** `fetchCOTData()` now sorts `results` by Long% (highest to lowest) before rendering the `#cot-rows` container. Most bullishly-positioned currencies appear at the top; most bearishly-positioned at the bottom. This matches the display convention used by Bloomberg and FXSSI Pro for positioning tables — allowing traders to instantly identify the most extreme long and short crowding without scanning the full list.
- Sort is computed from the raw `longPositions / (longPositions + shortPositions)` ratio, consistent with the `longPct` value already rendered per row. No changes to data, calculations, labels, or colors.

---

## v7.14.5 (2026-04-10) — Fix: suppress push-failure emails from intraday quotes workflow

### Site — .github/workflows/update-intraday-quotes.yml

- **Fix: changed `exit 1` to `exit 0` in the "all push attempts failed" branch** — when
  the 4-attempt rebase retry loop exhausts (e.g. due to concurrent runs queued by
  cron-job.org), the step now exits cleanly instead of with an error code. A push failure
  is not a data loss event: the next cron-job.org dispatch (≤5 min) will fetch and push
  fresh data. Exiting with 1 triggered GitHub's failure notification email on every
  collision; exiting with 0 is silent. No impact on data freshness or terminal behavior.

---



### Site — .github/workflows/update-intraday-quotes.yml

- **Fix: removed `schedule` trigger** — the GitHub Actions schedule (every 15 min) was running
  concurrently with cron-job.org dispatches (every 5 min). At 15-minute marks both triggered
  simultaneously, creating two parallel runs each trying to `git push` the same `quotes.json`.
  The 4-attempt rebase retry loop exhausted and exited with code 1, triggering GitHub's
  failure notification email on every collision (~4-6 times per hour during market hours).
  Resolution: `schedule` block removed entirely. cron-job.org is the sole clock.
  If cron-job.org goes down, the maximum data gap equals its configured interval (5 min) —
  acceptable for a ~5-minute delayed terminal. Manual trigger via `workflow_dispatch` preserved.

---

## v7.14.3 (2026-04-10) — Revert sticky Price Chart (v7.14.1)

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.css

- **Revert: removed `#section-fxpairs` sticky positioning** — reverted to default flow positioning per user preference.

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js

- **Revert: `loadTVChart()` scroll logic** — restored original `scrollIntoView({ behavior:'smooth', block:'start' })` unconditional on every symbol load.

---



### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js

- **New: click-to-chart on Retail FX Positioning rows** — each pair row in the Myfxbook/sentiment panel now opens its TradingView chart on click, consistent with COT rows, FX table rows, Cross-Asset cells, and Carry Ranking rows. Symbol conversion: `p.sym` (`EUR/USD`) → `FX_IDC:EURUSD` (strip `/`, add prefix). `cursor:pointer` and `title` tooltip added to the row wrapper. Combined with the sticky chart (v7.14.1), the chart updates in place at the top of the viewport without any scroll.

## v7.14.1 (2026-04-10) — Sticky Price Chart: chart stays visible while scrolling to COT and lower panels

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.css

- **New: `#section-fxpairs` sticky positioning** — the Price Chart panel now uses `position: sticky; top: 0; z-index: 10` within `#main`. As the user scrolls past the FX Pairs table, Currency Strength Heatmap, Cross-Asset, Rates, and into the COT section, the chart panel anchors to the top of the content area and remains visible. Clicking any COT row updates the chart symbol in-place without losing sight of the chart — matching the Bloomberg Terminal paradigm of a persistent chart alongside scrollable data panels. `z-index: 10` keeps the sticky panel above scrolling content while remaining below `#topbar` (z-index: 200) and popovers (z-index: 1000+).

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js

- **Update: `loadTVChart()` scroll logic** — with the chart now sticky, unconditional `scrollIntoView` on every symbol load was incorrect (it would fight the sticky behaviour when triggered from below-chart panels like COT). Updated to only trigger scroll when `rect.top > 60px`, i.e. when the user is above the chart and hasn't scrolled into it yet. When the panel is already stuck at the top (rect.top ≈ 0), no scroll is triggered.

---



### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js

- **Remove: Dead code — `fetchEtfIV()` function and `ETF_IV_MANIFEST` constant**
  - `fetchEtfIV()` was never wired to HTML (no `id="etf-iv-rows"` container) and never called from `boot()` or any `setInterval`. Removing eliminates ~85 lines of unreachable code and prevents future confusion with the active `fetchOptionSkew()` (Positioning Bias panel).

- **Upgrade: `fetchCarryRanking()` — Bloomberg Carry Monitor / JP Morgan GBI conventions**
  - **Primary sort changed from gross rate differential to carry-to-vol ratio** (rate diff / HV30). This is the industry-standard metric: units of carry earned per unit of realised vol risk. Falls back to gross differential when HV30 is unavailable.
  - **Rate-cycle regime badges added** (↑ hiking / ↓ cutting / → hold) for both legs of each pair, derived from `computeCBTrend()` — same logic as the CB Rates panel, no double computation.
  - **Panel subtitle updates dynamically**: shows `G8 · carry-to-vol ratio` when HV30 data is available, `G8 · CB rate differential` when falling back.
  - **Enriched tooltip**: long rate / short rate / gross diff (bp) / HV30 / carry-to-vol — full institutional detail on hover.
  - **Color thresholds updated for carry-to-vol**: green (>0.30), neutral (>0.12), dim (<0.12) — previously used gross diff thresholds.
  - HV30 source: `intraday-data/quotes.json → hv30` (same source as FX table and pair detail popover).

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.css

- **Fix: `.carry-rank-row` grid template** updated from 4 to 5 columns (`12px 52px 28px 1fr 40px`) to accommodate the new regime badge column.
- **New classes**: `.cr-regimes`, `.cr-regime`, `.cr-hike` (var --up), `.cr-cut` (var --down), `.cr-hold` (var --text3), `.cr-regime-sep`.

### Frontend — index.html

- **Fix: Carry Trade Ranking subtitle** — static initial text updated from `G8 · CB rate differential` to `G8 · carry-to-vol ratio` (matches the JS dynamic state when HV30 is available, which is the expected default).
- **Fix: Positioning Bias initial label** — static HTML label corrected from `ETF IV · COT` to `COT-derived` (correct fallback state before `fetchOptionSkew()` resolves). JS overwrites to `ETF options IV · CBOE` on successful fetch, as before.

### Frontend — guide-dashboard.html

- **Update: Carry Trade — Top Pairs section** — description rewritten to explain carry-to-vol ratio as the primary sort metric (Bloomberg Carry Monitor / JP Morgan GBI convention), regime badges (↑/↓/→), tooltip detail, and fallback behavior.
- **New: Carry Trade mock illustration** — 5-row panel mock added matching the v7.14.0 layout: 5-column grid (rank / pair / regime badges / bar / carry-to-vol value), with accurate color coding and regime badge legend.

### Frontend — about.html

- **Update: Data sources table** — added dedicated `Carry Trade Ranking (carry-to-vol)` row with sources `Official central bank sources · yfinance HV30` at `~30 MIN` refresh. Previously the carry ranking had no dedicated row.
- **Fix: Positioning Bias row label** — corrected from `ETF IV · COT` to `ETF options IV · COT` for consistency with the JS dynamic label.

- **Fix: Translate 4 remaining Spanish-language comments missed in v7.13.47**
  - Line 395: `// Fallback: ECB Frankfurter (solo si RT cache no esta disponible aun)`
  - Line 431: `// HV30 - volatilidad historica 30 dias calculada por ...`

### Guide — guide-dashboard.html

- **Fix: CB Rates mock table — corrected trend arrows for all 8 banks**
  - All banks now show `--` (neutral) in the mock illustration, consistent with `computeCBTrend()` pause-detection logic: as of April 2026, no G8 central bank has moved rates within the 90-day PAUSE_DAYS window.
  - Previous mock incorrectly showed Fed=Cutting, BoE=Cutting, RBA=Hiking, RBNZ=Cutting.

Guide illustrations must match production panel behaviour at time of publication.
The Bloomberg-equivalent 90-day pause-detection rule and OIS-only sourcing for CB bias
are now explicitly described in the guide.


## v7.13.47 (2026-04-10) — Audit corrections: override rule, tilde bias, editorial compliance

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js

- **Fix: Restore canonical narrative badge override rule** (regression from undocumented change)
  - Reverted `shouldOverride` to the exact form specified in GUIDELINES.md:
    `isCurrentStale || !_aiRegimeFresh || (liveRank > currentRank && liveRank >= 2)`
  - Removed the undocumented `|| (aiLiveDivergence >= 2)` branch that was causing the live assessment to override a valid AI badge even when live rank was only MIXED (rank 1). The `liveRank >= 2` guard ensures the live signal only takes precedence when it reaches CAUTION or RISK-OFF level.
  - Removed stale `aiLiveDivergence` variable (no longer used).
  - Updated `narrRegEl.title` to always read `"Live assessment · VIX …"` (no per-branch label needed).

- **Fix: Add `~` prefix to CB Rate Expectations fallback bias labels**
  - When `meetings.bias` is absent (no curated OIS/futures field), the UI now renders `~ ↓ Cut`, `~ ↑ Hike`, or `~ → Hold` instead of the unlabeled form.
  - The tilde signals that the value is estimated from historical rate trajectory via `computeCBTrend()`, not sourced from market consensus — consistent with GUIDELINES.md and the `meetings.json` schema table.

- **Fix: Remove investment advice language from IV−HV tooltip**
  - `data-tip-ex` text changed from *"Traders may sell vol strategies in this environment"* (prescriptive) to *"IV−HV > +3% historically indicates options are pricing in a premium above recent realised moves — hedging costs are elevated relative to actual market movement"* (descriptive).
  - Complies with the non-negotiable rule: signal subtexts must be descriptive, not prescriptive.

- **Fix: Translate all Spanish-language code comments to English**
  - Lines 394, 1046, 1070–1072, 1080, 2047, 4322–4325, 4329 — all translated to English.
  - Policy: "All pages are in English only" applies to all repo content including inline comments.

### Frontend — index.html

- **Fix: Sync CSS cache buster with JS version**
  - `dashboard.css?v=7.13.37` → `dashboard.css?v=7.13.46` to match the deployed JS version.
  - The CSS file itself did not change between v7.13.37 and v7.13.46; the cache buster is updated for consistency and to avoid ambiguity in deployment logs.

### Frontend — about.html

- **Fix: Remove unverified `twitter:site` meta tag**
  - Removed `<meta name="twitter:site" content="@GlobalInvesting">`.
  - GUIDELINES.md: "No invented social media handles or accounts. Only link to accounts that actually exist." The handle could not be verified as belonging to this project.

### SEO — sitemap.xml

- **Fix: Update `lastmod` for pages changed in this release cycle**
  - `index.html` and `about.html` updated to `2026-04-10` (date of this release).
  - Remaining pages retain `2026-04-01` as their last structural change date.

### GUIDELINES.md

- No rule changes in this release. All fixes implement existing rules that were either
  regressed or never applied. The override rule comment in the code now cites GUIDELINES.md
  explicitly for future reference.

### Industry standard note

None of these changes affect data calculations or Bloomberg/Refinitiv-equivalent behaviour.
All fixes are editorial, compliance, or code-hygiene corrections.


## v7.13.46 (2026-04-10) — Fix CB Rates trend arrow: apply recency + pause detection

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js
- **Rewrote `computeCBTrend()`** to use the same two-layer logic as the workflow bias detection, fixing trend arrows that diverged from the `meetings.json` bias field:
  - **Layer 1 — Recent momentum (`PAUSE_DAYS = 90`):** directional signal (up/down) is only applied when `obs[0]` is less than 90 days old. Previously, the ECB's last cut (Jun 2025, ~10 months old) kept showing ↓ because the old code only checked the 180-day data-gap guard without verifying how recent the *move itself* was.
  - **Layer 2 — Pause detection:** computes `effectiveFlat = max(consecutiveFlatMonths, dataAgeMonths − 1)`. If ≥ 3 → `'flat'`. This catches both banks with genuinely flat rates (Fed, BoE, BoC, SNB, RBNZ) and banks whose `rates/*.json` is stale because the rate hasn't changed (ECB at 2.00% since Jun 2025).
  - **BoJ special case:** BoJ is correctly shown as `—` (flat) in the *rates* panel — the rate has been at 0.75% for 8 months. This is intentional: the trend arrow shows **historical rate direction** (what has happened), while the bias in the *Expectations* panel shows **forward direction** (what markets expect next). These are different signals and should differ when a CB pauses between hikes.
- **Cache buster:** `dashboard.js?v=7.13.43` → `?v=7.13.46`.
- **Validated 8/8** correct against current `rates/*.json` data before deployment.

### Industry standard note
Bloomberg Terminal and Refinitiv Eikon display the CB rate trend arrow based on the **direction of the most recent policy decision**, resetting to neutral (—) after 3+ months without a move. The new `computeCBTrend()` matches this behaviour.

### GUIDELINES.md
- **Added "Industry standard compliance — non-negotiable" section** (after Product identity): mandates that every change to the terminal must meet Bloomberg / Refinitiv Eikon conventions. Covers CB trend arrows, rate bias labels, color coding, forward rate methodology, signal separation (retrospective vs prospective), and the mandatory pre-change question ("How does Bloomberg handle this?"). Lists authoritative reference sources (Bloomberg, Eikon, CME FedWatch, ASX Rate Tracker, OIS pricing).
- **Added industry standard check to both deployment checklists**: first item in "Checklist — before adding or modifying any data panel" and "Checklist — before any frontend deployment". Confirms the check is required even when the change seems purely cosmetic.
- **Version footer bumped** to v7.13.46.

---

## v7.13.45 (2026-04-09) — Fix CB Rate Expectations bias: correct 5 stale values, automate detection

### Data — meetings-data/meetings.json
- **Fixed 5 incorrect bias fields** that had not been updated to reflect post-Middle-East-war central bank reality:
  - `USD` (Fed): `cut` → `hold` — Fed on hold since Dec 2024; CME FedWatch 94-98% probability of no change at Apr 29 FOMC.
  - `EUR` (ECB): `cut` → `hold` — ECB held at last meeting; cutting cycle considered complete; inflation converging to 2% target.
  - `GBP` (BoE): `cut` → `hold` — BoE held unanimously in March 2026; Middle East energy shock raised near-term CPI above 3%; 90% of surveyed economists expect hold at Apr 30.
  - `AUD` (RBA): `cut` → `hike` — RBA raised in both February and March 2026 (cash rate now 4.1%); Westpac forecasts peak at 4.85%; UBS labels RBA a "hawkish outlier".
  - `JPY` (BoJ): `hold` → `hike` — BoJ in active hiking cycle; multiple board members support further increases; near-2% inflation and negative real rates underpin the case.
- **Unchanged (already correct):** `CAD` hold, `CHF` hold, `NZD` hold.

### Engine — .github/workflows/workflow_meetings.yml
- **Replaced static bias preservation with automatic two-layer trajectory detection.** The previous logic copied `bias` from the prior file unchanged every Monday, meaning stale values were never corrected automatically. New system:
  - **Layer 1 — Recent momentum:** checks whether the rate moved within the last `PAUSE_MONTHS` (3) months. If it rose → `hike`; fell → `cut`. Critically, this layer is skipped if the latest data point is older than 3 months — this prevents old ECB cuts (Jun 2025) from being misread as current momentum.
  - **Layer 2 — Pause detection:** computes `effective_flat = max(consecutive_flat_months, data_age_months − 1)`. If ≥ 3 → `hold`. This correctly catches both paused-hiking banks (BoC, RBNZ at 2.25% flat) and banks whose rate JSON is stale because the rate hasn't moved (ECB at 2.00% since Jun 2025 → 10 months → `hold`).
  - **Per-bank hawkish override:** `HAWKISH_OVERRIDE = {'JPY': 'hike'}` corrects BoJ, which is in an active hiking cycle but shows a flat plateau in the monthly rate series (0.75% for 8 months). The override fires when Layer 2 would otherwise return `hold`.
  - Falls back to prior file bias on any exception — no data loss on transient failures.
  - Logs `data_age`, `flat_months`, and `eff_flat` diagnostics in Actions output for auditability.
  - Validated 8/8 correct against current real rate data before deployment.

---

## v7.13.44 (2026-04-09) — Fix EUR rate: remove fabricated estimate, strengthen fetch_eur_ecb

### Engine — scripts/fetch_rates.py
- **Removed incorrect EUR MANUAL_OVERRIDE (1.75%):** The 1.75% value added in v7.13.42 was a fabricated estimate with no verified source. The last confirmed ECB DFR in EUR.json (2.00%, Jun 2025) is more accurate and honest than a made-up number labeled `manual-override`. Rule added to GUIDELINES: never add EUR to MANUAL_OVERRIDES.
- **Rewrote `fetch_eur_ecb()` with 4 sources:** The function previously had only the ECB SDMX API (1 source, 4 format variants). When the SDMX endpoint fails in CI (connection issues or API changes), EUR falls through all the way to stale cache. New source chain: (1) ECB SDMX API — unchanged, (2) ECB website HTML key-rates page — scrapes `ecb.europa.eu/mopo/implement/sf/html/index.en.html` for "Deposit facility X.XX%", (3) BIS CBPOL `stats.bis.org` for eurozone (XM), (4) global-rates.com. This makes EUR as resilient as AUD, CAD, and GBP.
- **MANUAL_OVERRIDES comment updated:** Documents that EUR must never be added here and why.

### Site — rates/EUR.json
- **Reverted to last confirmed value (2.00%, 2025-06-01):** Removed the 6 fabricated `manual-estimate` observations (1.75% and 2.00% entries from Jul 2025 onward). The ECB DFR as of June 2025 was confirmed at 2.00% by the ECB decision data already in the file. Subsequent entries were unverified estimates.

### GUIDELINES.md
- **Added EUR rate — authoritative sources and staleness rule section:** Documents DFR vs MRO distinction, 4-source fetch priority, and the non-negotiable rule against fabricated MANUAL_OVERRIDES for EUR.
- **Version bumped** to v7.13.44.

---

## v7.13.43 (2026-04-09) — Fix CB Rate Expectations panel rendering

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js
- **Bug fix — `trendDir` scope error:** `trendDir` was only declared inside the `else` branch of the bias block, making it `undefined` for currencies with a `meetingsBias` value. The USD forward rate projection (`current ± 0.25`) silently failed because `step` referenced the undefined variable. Fixed by hoisting `computeCBTrend(obs)` to before the bias conditional — it is always needed for the FWD column regardless of whether `meetingsBias` is set.
- **Bug fix — CIP spot rate source:** The CIP forward rate for non-USD currencies read exclusively from `STATE.rates` (Frankfurter), which is empty on first render because `fetchFrankfurter()` runs non-blocking in background. Fixed by reading `STOOQ_RT_CACHE[pair].close` first (already awaited at boot) with `STATE.rates` as secondary fallback. FWD RATE now populates on first load without waiting for Frankfurter.
- **Removed `~` tilde prefix from fallback bias labels:** The tilde caused visual noise and user confusion. When `meetings.bias` is absent the fallback uses `computeCBTrend()` with standard `↓ Cut / → Hold / ↑ Hike` arrows (no prefix). The `title` tooltip on each span still indicates the source.

### Frontend — index.html
- **Cache buster:** `dashboard.js?v=7.13.36` → `?v=7.13.43` to force browser cache invalidation and ensure the patched JS is served immediately.

---

## v7.13.42 (2026-04-09) — CB Rates & Expectations panels aligned to industry standards

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js
- **CB Rate Expectations — Bias column:** Replaced backward-looking `computeCBTrend()` derivation with a `meetings.bias` field read from `meetings-data/meetings.json`. When `bias` is present (`'cut'` | `'hold'` | `'hike'`), the panel shows the market-consensus forward direction (OIS/futures). When absent, falls back to `computeCBTrend()` with a `~` prefix to signal estimation rather than confirmed market pricing.
- **CB Rates table — rate color:** Rate value is now colored by trend direction (`up` = green hiking cycle, `down` = red cutting cycle, `flat` = neutral). Previously hardcoded `class="up"` (always green) regardless of policy direction — a 0.75% BoJ rate in a hiking cycle now correctly shows green, while a 1.75% ECB rate in a cutting cycle shows red.

### Engine — meetings-data/meetings.json (site repo)
- **Added `bias` field** to all 8 G8 currencies reflecting April 2026 OIS/futures market consensus: USD cut, EUR cut, GBP cut, JPY hold, AUD cut, CAD hold, CHF hold, NZD hold.
- **EUR rate corrected:** EUR.json was 10 months stale (stuck at 2.00% from Jun 2025). Added estimated ECB DFR observations through Mar 2026 based on the documented easing cycle. Flagged as `manual-estimate` source pending ECB SDMX API reconnection.

### Engine — .github/workflows/workflow_meetings.yml
- **Bias preservation:** The meetings workflow now reads existing `bias` fields before rebuilding `meetings.json` and carries them forward. Previously, each Monday run would silently erase any manually-set `bias` values.

### Engine — scripts/fetch_rates.py
- **EUR MANUAL_OVERRIDE added:** EUR DFR set to 1.75% (2026-03-01) to unblock the ECB SDMX API staleness issue. The override comment documents the official ECB source URL and instructs when to remove it.

### GUIDELINES.md
- **Added CB Rate Expectations — bias field** section: documents `meetings.bias` schema, fallback behavior, `~` prefix convention, and operator update responsibilities.
- **Added CB Rates table — rate color rule** section: documents the trend-based coloring logic replacing the hardcoded green.
- **Version bumped** to v7.13.42.

---

## v7.13.41 (2026-04-09) — Stress scoring thresholds aligned to industry standards

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js
- **MOVE index threshold:** `> 120` → `> 100`. BofA/ICE define MOVE >100 as "elevated" bond market stress. The previous threshold of 120 only activated during late-stage crisis (SVB, Oct 2023 peaks), missing the early stress signal window. The MOVE display logic already used 100 as the "Elevated" label boundary — the scoring now matches.
- **Gold intraday threshold:** `> 1.0%` → `> 2.0%`. A 1% gold move occurs on routine inflation prints and USD weakness days with no systemic stress. Institutional risk models (BlackRock RAI, MSCI) use rolling ratio metrics rather than intraday; the closest equivalent for an intraday signal is a 2%+ safe-haven surge.
- **SPX intraday threshold:** `< -0.5%` → `< -1.5%`. A -0.5% SPX dip is a normal intraday fluctuation. -1.5% represents a statistically meaningful risk-off session (roughly 1σ for a typical low-vol day).
- **Regime tooltip updated** to reflect new thresholds and boundary rationale.

### GUIDELINES.md
- **Added stress scoring canonical thresholds table** under the Regime badge architecture section. Documents all boundary values, point weights, and rationale for each factor. Future changes to scoring must update this table and the test suite.
- **Version bumped** to v7.13.41.

---

## v7.13.40 (2026-04-09) — Fix FX panel click intercept without removing TV chart margin

### Frontend — index.html & assets/dashboard.css
- **Root cause of FX export buttons and panel title being unclickable:** `#tv-chart-wrap` had `margin-bottom:-32px` which was needed to hide the TradingView copyright bar that the widget renders inside the iframe at 290px+32px total height. This negative margin caused the chart container's invisible overflow area to intercept pointer events on the `panel-head` of the FX Pairs section below it.
- **Fix:** Restored `margin-bottom:-32px` (necessary to hide TV copyright bar) and added `pointer-events:none` to `#tv-chart-wrap` in dashboard.css. The iframe inside gets `pointer-events:auto` explicitly so chart pan/zoom/interaction continues to work. The 32px invisible overflow area no longer intercepts clicks on the FX panel below it.

---

## v7.13.39 (2026-04-09) — Fix export download: replace blob: URL with data: URL for Edge compatibility

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js
- **Root cause identified:** Edge Enhanced Tracking Prevention silently blocks programmatic `blob:` URL downloads triggered by `a.click()` — no error is thrown, no console output, the click is simply discarded. This caused CSV/JSON exports to appear completely non-functional despite `exportPanel()` running correctly and generating the file content.
- **Fix:** Replaced `URL.createObjectURL(blob)` + `a.click()` with a `data:` URI (`data:text/csv;charset=utf-8,...`). Edge does not restrict `data:` URL downloads under Tracking Prevention, and the approach is compatible with all modern browsers.

---

## v7.13.38 (2026-04-09) — Fix export buttons: replace onclick with addEventListener, fix panel-sub overflow

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js
- **Replaced `onclick=""` attributes with `addEventListener` for all export buttons:** Edge Enhanced Tracking Prevention can suppress inline event handlers on elements adjacent to ad/tracking scripts. The FX and COT CSV/JSON export buttons now wire their click handlers entirely in JS via `wireExportButtons()` IIFE, with `e.stopPropagation()` to prevent interference from document-level capture listeners.

### Frontend — index.html
- **Removed `onclick=""` from FX and COT export buttons:** Buttons now use stable IDs (`export-fx-csv`, `export-fx-json`, `export-cot-csv`, `export-cot-json`) wired from dashboard.js.
- **Updated CSS cache buster:** `dashboard.css?v=7.12.42` → `?v=7.13.37` to match current codebase version.

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.css
- **Fixed `panel-sub` overflow:** Added `min-width:0; overflow:hidden; text-overflow:ellipsis; white-space:nowrap` to `.panel-sub`. In flex rows, a long subtitle without `min-width:0` can expand past the `export-btns` container and intercept pointer events on the buttons behind it.

---

## v7.13.37 (2026-04-09) — Fix loadIntradayQuotes race condition and sentiment interval

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js
- **Fixed race condition in `loadIntradayQuotes()`:** Multiple async callers (fetchSentiment, fetchRiskData, fetchCrossAssetData, fetchQuoteBarRT, etc.) were firing simultaneously on page load and at each refresh cycle. Because they all passed the cache guard before any single call had completed and set `_intradayCacheTime`, each fired an independent fetch — producing hundreds of duplicate `[Intraday] ✓ Loaded` log entries and saturating the browser's network/event loop, which prevented click handlers (including CSV/JSON export buttons) from responding. Fix: added `_intradayInFlight` promise deduplication — if a fetch is already in progress, subsequent callers await the same promise instead of starting a new one.
- **Fixed `fetchSentiment` poll interval:** Was running every 30 seconds despite the underlying data source (Myfxbook via GitHub Action) only updating every 30 minutes. Interval changed to 10 minutes, eliminating 18 of every 20 redundant calls per half-hour.

---

## v7.13.36 (2026-04-09) — index.html audit: 8 issues corrected + TV widget load fix

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js
- **Fixed TradingView widget load race condition:** `initTVWidgets()` usaba `addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', ...)` que nunca se disparaba porque `dashboard.js` se carga con `defer` (el DOM ya está parseado cuando el script corre). Reemplazado por guard `document.readyState === 'loading'` — si el DOM ya está listo, `attachObservers()` se llama de inmediato; si no, espera el evento. Esto restaura la carga correcta del chart de TradingView, el calendario de eventos y el mapa económico.

### Frontend — index.html
- **Removed `offers` block from JSON-LD WebApplication schema:** The `{"price":"0","priceCurrency":"USD"}` entry violated GUIDELINES ("never mention free/zero cost"). Block removed entirely from structured data.
- **Corrected cache buster:** Updated `dashboard.js?v=7.12.42` → `?v=7.13.36` to match current codebase version and force cache invalidation.
- **Fixed Session Vol label:** `#svol-sub` subtitle was `HV30-derived · session ratios BIS 2022`; corrected to `5yr historical avg · fixed reference` per GUIDELINES (Data integrity section).
- **Moved inline `<script>` to dashboard.js:** ~105-line TradingView lazy-loader / IntersectionObserver block removed from `<body>` of index.html. Logic migrated to `initTVWidgets()` IIFE in dashboard.js. GUIDELINES architecture rule enforced: no inline JS in index.html.
- **Corrected "Real-time"/"live" claims in metadata:** `meta[description]`, `og:description`, `twitter:description`, and JSON-LD `description` all referenced "real-time" or "live" FX rates. Status bar shows DELAY ~5 MIN. All instances replaced with `~5-minute delayed` qualifier.
- **Fixed JSON-LD featureList:** `"Volatility Risk Monitor — VIX, MOVE Index, EUR/USD IV"` corrected to `EUR/USD HV 30d` — the panel displays realized historical volatility, not implied volatility.
- **Removed unverified Twitter handles:** `twitter:creator` and `twitter:site` tags pointing to `@GlobalInvesting` removed per GUIDELINES ("no invented social media handles").

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js
- **Added `initTVWidgets()` IIFE:** Migrated TradingView lazy-loader from the removed inline script. Includes `loadTVEvents()` (Events Calendar widget injection into `#tvcal-scale`), `loadTVEconMap()` (Economic Map custom element injection replacing `#tv-econmap-placeholder`), and an IntersectionObserver (`rootMargin: 150px`) watching `#tv-chart-wrap`, `#tvcal-inner`, and `#section-econmap`. Includes `IntersectionObserver` feature-detection fallback for legacy browsers.

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js
- **Agregado `widget.id = 'tv-chart-widget'` en `loadTVChart()`:** La función global en dashboard.js no asignaba el `id` al nuevo div del widget al reconstruir el DOM, a diferencia de la versión original del inline script. Esto rompía `watchForIframe()` (el skeleton nunca se ocultaba) y causaba conflicto entre las dos instancias de `loadTVChart` compitiendo al cargar la página. Fix: el div reconstruido ahora recibe `id="tv-chart-widget"` consistentemente.

### Frontend — all pages (11 files)
- **Synchronized FX Terminal version label:** `FX Terminal v4.1` updated to `FX Terminal v7.13` across all HTML pages: index.html, about.html, contact.html, privacy.html, terms.html, guide-dashboard.html, guide-cot.html, guide-cross-asset-risk.html, guide-fx-liquidity.html, guide-market-sentiment.html, guide-rates-yield-curve.html.

## v7.13.35 (2026-04-08) — Guide sync: audit and corrections across all satellite pages

### Guides — guide-dashboard.html
- **Font loading fix:** Changed Google Fonts link from non-blocking `media="print" onload` pattern to standard blocking `rel="stylesheet"`. GUIDELINES require blocking font load to prevent font CLS; this guide was the only one still using the non-blocking pattern.
- **TOC class fix:** Changed `<nav class="guide-toc">` to `<nav class="toc"><div class="toc-inner">` with matching close tag. The CSS defines `.toc` and `.toc-inner` — `guide-toc` was undefined, so the sidebar received no styles (no sticky positioning, no width, no border).
- **Layout mock updated:** Added "Historical VaR 95% · 1-day · 252d window (full width)" row to the main content area diagram, reflecting the new full-width VaR/CVaR table that sits below the Cross-Asset + Risk Monitor columns.

### Guides — guide-cross-asset-risk.html
- **`gdpr.js` defer added:** Script tag was missing `defer` attribute, causing synchronous execution blocking.
- **New section added — Historical VaR / CVaR (id="var-cvar"):** Full guide section documenting the new table: what it shows, layout (scrollable tbody, 4-row initial view), column definitions (VaR 95%, CVaR 95%, ES/VaR ratio, n), color thresholds, ⚠ regime-shift flag, and data source. Includes a mock table illustration matching the production layout.
- **TOC entry added:** "Historical VaR / CVaR" entry added to the guide TOC, linking to `#var-cvar`.

### All satellite pages — gdpr.js defer
- Added `defer` attribute to `<script src="assets/gdpr.js">` in: `guide-rates-yield-curve.html`, `guide-market-sentiment.html`, `guide-fx-liquidity.html`, `guide-cot.html`, `contact.html`, `terms.html`, `privacy.html`. Synchronous script in `<head>` is a deployment blocker per GUIDELINES.

## v7.13.32 (2026-04-08) — Layout: VaR/CVaR table full-width below Cross-Asset + Risk Monitor

### Frontend — index.html
- **Final layout restructure:** Wrapped the Cross-Asset + Risk Monitor grid and the VaR/CVaR table in a single outer container with `border-bottom`. The inner grid (`1fr 1fr`) holds the two panels side by side as before. The VaR/CVaR table sits below both columns at full width, separated by a `border-top`. This eliminates the empty-space problem regardless of which column is taller — neither panel needs `align-self:start` workarounds, and the VaR table uses the full available width (11 instruments, 5 columns) which suits a data-dense table better than a half-width column. Removed `align-self:start` from both panel divs since stretch (default) is correct when both panels are the same height.

## v7.13.31 (2026-04-08) — Fix: Risk Monitor panel clipped on desktop; Cross-Asset half-width on mobile

### Frontend — index.html
- **Desktop layout fix:** Removed `min-height:320px` from the Cross-Asset + Risk wrapper grid. The minimum height was capping the grid row, preventing `#section-risk` from expanding past 320px even with `align-self:start`. Both panels now grow freely to their content height.
- **`#section-risk`:** `align-self:start` retained (added in same session) so the panel doesn't stretch to match its sibling's height.

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.css
- **Mobile layout fix:** Added rule overriding `align-self` and `border-right` on `#section-crossasset` and `#section-risk` within the mobile breakpoint. When the wrapper converts to `flex column`, `align-self:start` (set inline) was causing the cross-asset panel to shrink to content width instead of filling the screen. The override forces `align-self:stretch` and `width:100%` on both panels on mobile, and removes the decorative right border that is no longer needed when stacked.

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.css

This was the root cause — the `height:350px` rule never applied on mobile, causing the flex container to collapse to 0px and the widget to render with zero height.

### Frontend — index.html
- **Mobile height fallback in `loadMQL5Calendar()`:** Widget now uses `350px` as the explicit height when `window.innerWidth <= 900`, matching the CSS rule, instead of reading `inner.offsetHeight` (which returns 0 before the CSS fix applies at render time).

## v7.13.28 (2026-04-06) — MQL5 Calendar: overlay footer attribution

### Frontend — index.html
- **Footer overlay:** Added an `position:absolute` overlay div at the bottom of `#mql5cal-inner` (height 26px, `background:var(--bg)`) that covers the tradays-injected "Economic Calendar · MQL5" iframe footer. Replaced with a discrete `9px` terminal-style label "Economic Calendar · tradays.com" using `--text3` color and `--font-ui` — consistent with other panel attributions (e.g. Myfxbook sub-label). `pointer-events:none` ensures the calendar scroll is not blocked.

---



### Frontend — index.html
- **Root cause identified:** The tradays iframe is cross-origin; its dimensions cannot be observed from the parent. All ResizeObserver / polling approaches from v7.13.26 were ineffective.
- **New approach — fixed grid height:** The SESSIONS + SPREADS grid is now `height:780px` (up from 580px), sized to fit Market Sessions + Session Vol + Upcoming Events header + ~490px of calendar widget. Both columns fill `height:100%` of this fixed grid, so Retail FX Positioning automatically aligns in height with the calendar — no JS needed.
- **Widget height passed explicitly:** `loadMQL5Calendar()` reads `inner.offsetHeight` at load time (after flex layout has settled) and passes that value as `height` in the widget JSON config, matching the iframe to the available space exactly.
- **`#mql5cal-inner`:** Changed from `height:500px;min-height:350px` to `flex:1;min-height:0` — fills remaining column space naturally inside the 780px grid.
- **`#section-tvcalendar`:** Restored `flex:1;min-height:0` so it expands to fill the column below the static session tables.
- **Removed all v7.13.26 complexity:** No ResizeObserver, no setInterval polling, no postMessage listener — the fixed grid height makes dynamic sync unnecessary.

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.css
- **Mobile override:** Updated selector to `div[style*="height:780px"]` to match new grid height attribute.

---



### Frontend — index.html
- **Grid height fixed → min-height:** The SESSIONS + SPREADS grid was `height:580px` (fixed), which clipped the MQL5 calendar widget at initial load. Changed to `min-height:580px` so the grid can grow when the calendar widget expands.
- **Left column overflow:** Removed `height:100%` from `#section-crossasset` so it no longer constrains itself to the fixed grid height. The column now grows with its content.
- **Right column alignment:** Removed `height:100%` from the Reference Spreads / Retail FX Positioning column. With the grid using `align-items:stretch` (default), the right column automatically matches the left column's height — keeping Retail FX Positioning vertically aligned with the calendar.
- **`#section-tvcalendar`:** Removed `flex:1;min-height:0` which were constraining the calendar to leftover flex space inside the 580px column. Now uses natural height.
- **`#mql5cal-inner` initial height:** Set to `500px` (matching the expanded widget state users see after selecting currencies) instead of relying solely on `min-height:350px`. This eliminates the small-on-load / large-after-edit size jump.
- **`#economicCalendarWidget`:** Changed from `height:calc(100% + 26px)` to `height:100%` so it fills the concrete height of `#mql5cal-inner` correctly.
- **ResizeObserver + postMessage sync:** `loadMQL5Calendar()` now attaches a `ResizeObserver` to `#mql5cal-inner` that detects when the tradays iframe grows and updates the container height accordingly. Also listens for `postMessage` height events from the iframe.
- **Inline critical CSS:** Updated `#mql5cal-inner` entry to `height:500px;min-height:350px`.

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.css
- **Mobile override selector:** Updated `div[style*="height:580px"]` → `div[style*="min-height:580px"]` to match the new attribute value and continue collapsing the grid on mobile.

---

## v7.13.25 (2026-04-06) — MQL5 Calendar: revert scroll-lock (broke mouse wheel scroll)

### Frontend — index.html
- **Reverted:** The `blur/focus + scroll` lock introduced in v7.13.25 was removed — it blocked normal mouse wheel scrolling on `#main` whenever the iframe had focus. `loadMQL5Calendar()` is back to simply injecting the widget script. The popup scroll-jump remains a known limitation of the cross-origin iframe.

---

## v7.13.24 (2026-04-06) — MQL5 Calendar: fix footer visibility and popup scroll jump

### Frontend — index.html
- **Footer masked:** Added `#mql5cal-footer-mask` — an `position:absolute; bottom:0; height:26px; z-index:5; pointer-events:none` div inside `#mql5cal-inner` styled with `var(--bg2)` and a top border matching the panel border. Sits above the iframe and covers the "Economic Calendar · MQL5" footer bar without creating dead space. `pointer-events:none` keeps the scrollbar area of the widget fully interactive.
- **Popup scroll-jump fixed:** When the user opens the currency/importance filter popup, the MQL5 iframe internally calls `scrollIntoView`, which scrolls the `#main` ancestor. A `ResizeObserver` on `#mql5cal-inner` detects when the iframe grows beyond the container height (popup open) and immediately freezes `#main` scroll (`overflow:hidden`) while saving the current `scrollTop`. On popup close (iframe shrinks back), scroll is restored and `overflow:hidden` removed.

---

## v7.13.23 (2026-04-06) — MQL5 Calendar: fix settings popup shifting content up

### Frontend — index.html
- **Bug fix — settings popup shifts calendar up:** When the user opened the currency/importance filter popup inside the MQL5 widget, the content scrolled up because `overflow:hidden` on `#mql5cal-inner` (and the implicit `min-height:0` overflow clipping on the flex parent `#section-tvcalendar`) was forcing the widget's iframe to scroll internally to reveal the modal. Fixed by setting `overflow:visible` on both `#mql5cal-inner` and adding `overflow:visible` to `#section-tvcalendar`. The popup now overlaps adjacent content rather than pushing the widget upward.

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js
- **Bug fix — fired badge persists after removing last alert:** `alertsRender()` returned early when `arr.length === 0` before reaching the badge update block, leaving the `⬤1` badge visible indefinitely. Fixed by computing `firedCount` and updating `#alerts-fired-badge` **before** the early return — badge now hides correctly as soon as the last fired alert is deleted.
- **All signal notification functions from v7.13.20 included** (this JS replaces the ZIP base).

## v7.13.20 (2026-04-06) — Alerts & Market Signals: configurable browser notifications for new AI signals

### Frontend — index.html
- **New UI:** Added a "Notify" toggle button in the `Alerts & Market Signals` panel header. Sits right-aligned in the `panel-head`, next to the subtitle. Shows a bell icon + label. States: off (default, muted border), on (green border + teal background), blocked (red, cursor disabled).

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js
- **`SIG_NOTIF_KEY` / `SIG_NOTIF_SEEN_KEY`:** Two new `localStorage` keys — `gi_sig_notif` stores the user's preference (`'on'`/`'off'`, default off); `gi_sig_seen` stores the fingerprint of the last-rendered signal set for change detection.
- **`sigNotifEnabled()`:** Returns true when `gi_sig_notif === 'on'`.
- **`updateSignalNotifBtn()`:** Syncs button visual state (CSS classes, `aria-pressed`, `aria-label`, tooltip, label text) with current pref and `Notification.permission`. Handles three visual states: off / on / blocked.
- **`toggleSignalNotifications()`:** Async — requests `Notification.requestPermission()` on first enable. Bails silently if permission denied; updates localStorage and refreshes button on success.
- **`sigFingerprint(signals)`:** Produces a stable string from `time|title|priority` for each signal — used to detect when the signal set genuinely changed vs. a re-render of the same data.
- **`maybeNotifyNewSignals(signals)`:** Called by `buildRichNarrative()` after rendering signals. Guards: notif disabled → skip; permission not granted → skip; fingerprint unchanged → skip; first load (no prior fingerprint) → records baseline without notifying. On genuine change: fires one `Notification` tagged `gi-signals-update` (replaces prior notification instead of stacking). Body summarises count by priority (e.g. "5 signals — 1 critical, 2 warning").
- **`initAlerts()`:** Calls `updateSignalNotifBtn()` on boot to restore button state from localStorage.

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.css
- **`.sig-notif-toggle`:** New CSS class for the notification toggle button. Compact pill style (9px font, uppercase, rounded border). Three modifier classes: `.sig-notif-on` (teal border + faint background), `.sig-notif-blocked` (red, `cursor:not-allowed`).

Added `<tr><td>DXY</td><td>SPX</td>…</tr>` and `<tr><td>Gold</td><td>DXY</td>…</tr>` to match the real render.

## v7.13.18 (2026-04-06) — Correlations injected into Groq context for narrative and signals

### Engine — scripts/generate_narrative_signals.py
- **New data source in `build_context()`:** Section 8 reads `correlations[]` from `intraday-data/quotes.json` (computed by `fetch_intraday_quotes.py` v2.8 with 252d norm and z_score). Only pairs with |z_score| > 1.0 are included — notable deviations from historical norm. When all correlations are within 1σ, a one-line summary confirms no regime breaks.
- **Contextual annotations:** Each break includes a human-readable note explaining what the deviation means (e.g. "positive DXY/SPX = USD funding stress", "positive Gold/DXY = safe-haven model broken or inflation bid", "positive USD/JPY vs VIX = JPY no longer safe haven").
- **`SIGNALS_SYSTEM` updated:** New rule instructs the LLM to generate at least one "warning" or "critical" signal for pairs with z > 1.5σ, using corr/norm/z values as evidence. Priority escalates to "critical" above 2.5σ.
- **`NARRATIVE_SYSTEM` updated:** New optional rule allows the LLM to weave a single clause referencing the most significant correlation break (z > 1.5σ) into the narrative when it adds directional context.
- **Docstring updated** to list correlations as a data source.

---



### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js
- **Bug:** el posicionamiento del pair detail popover usaba `ph = 300` como altura estimada fija. El panel real tiene ~500–700px de contenido, por lo que el cálculo de desbordamiento `y + ph > vh - 8` fallaba — el panel aparecía cortado debajo de la pantalla.
- **Fix:** el panel se renderiza primero off-screen (`visibility:hidden`) para que el browser calcule su altura real, luego `requestAnimationFrame` lee `getBoundingClientRect()` y recalcula `x/y` con las dimensiones reales antes de hacerlo visible. Funciona correctamente para todos los tamaños de panel independientemente del contenido.

---

## v7.13.16 (2026-04-06) — fix: 1D % change FX aún incorrecto — usar regularMarketChangePercent de Yahoo

### Engine — scripts/fetch_intraday_quotes.py
- **Causa raíz definitiva del bug +0.00%:** `fast_info["last_price"]` y `closes.iloc[-1]` pueden coincidir en FX porque yfinance incluye la barra de reapertura del domingo 22:00 UTC (con el precio del viernes) en el historial del lunes. Tanto `last_price` como `closes.iloc[-1]` devuelven ese mismo precio → chg = 0.
- **Fix v2.7 — cascade de 3 estrategias:** (A) `ticker.info["regularMarketChangePercent"]` + `regularMarketPrice` + `regularMarketPreviousClose` — idéntico al porcentaje que muestra Yahoo Finance en su sitio; (B) `fast_info.previous_close` (campo oficial de Yahoo, no el bar de la historia) vs `fast_info.last_price`; (C) `closes.iloc[-1]` vs `closes.iloc[-2]` como último recurso.
- **Fuente primaria ahora es `ticker.info`**, el mismo endpoint que usa finance.yahoo.com para mostrar el % de cambio — elimina toda discrepancia con lo que el usuario ve en Yahoo Finance o TradingView (que también usa Yahoo como fallback).

---

## v7.13.15 (2026-04-06) — fix: 1D % change still 0.00% — replace intraday bars with fast_info

### Engine — scripts/fetch_intraday_quotes.py
- **Bug fix (definitive):** Even with UTC-date filtering (v2.5), intraday intervals (`period="1d"/"2d"`) for FX pairs include a Sunday 22:00 UTC reopening bar carrying Friday's price. That bar passes the "today" date filter on Monday, making `close ≈ prev_close` → `+0.00%`. Replaced the entire intraday-bar approach with `ticker.fast_info["last_price"]` — a lightweight real-time field yfinance always populates without bar alignment, available for all symbols including FX. `prev_close` remains `closes.iloc[-1]` from the daily `period="5d"` bars (last completed session). `day_high`/`day_low` come from `fast_info["day_high"]`/`["day_low"]`. Fallback to `iloc[-1]` vs `iloc[-2]` (prev vs prior session) if `fast_info` throws.
- **Script version bumped:** v2.5 → v2.6.

---



### Engine — scripts/fetch_intraday_quotes.py
- **Bug fix (root cause):** `_date.today().isoformat()` returned the **server's local date**, not UTC. On GitHub Actions runners in non-UTC timezones, this caused `hist_today` to filter to an incorrect date (e.g. "2026-04-05" instead of "2026-04-06"), leaving it empty. Fixed by replacing with `datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y-%m-%d")` — always UTC.
- **Bug fix (secondary):** The single `period="2d", interval="1h"` fetch is unreliable for FX pairs in CI — hourly bars for the current UTC day may not yet exist. Replaced with a cascade: try `period="1d", interval="5m"` first (more granular, better FX intraday coverage), then `period="2d", interval="1h"` as fallback. Both paths use the new `_extract_today()` helper for consistent UTC-date filtering.
- **Script version bumped:** v2.4 → v2.5.

---



### Engine — scripts/fetch_intraday_quotes.py
- **Bug fix:** `fetch_yfinance_all()` used `period="5d", interval="1d"` and took `closes.iloc[-1]` as current price and `closes.iloc[-2]` as prev_close. On Mondays (and any post-holiday open), `iloc[-1]` is the **prior Friday close** and `iloc[-2]` is Thursday — producing a Thu→Fri change instead of today vs Friday. Fixed by: (1) using `iloc[-1]` from the daily bars as `prev_close` (last completed session), and (2) fetching `period="1d", interval="1m"` for the true current intraday price and today's real high/low. Falls back to the old daily-only logic when intraday data is unavailable (weekend, holiday).

---

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js
- **Bug fix:** `openPairPopover()` used a hardcoded `ph = 300` estimate to calculate whether the popover would overflow the bottom of the viewport. The actual rendered height of the panel is significantly taller (Price + Volatility + COT Positioning + Retail Sentiment sections). Fixed by positioning the popover at `top: 8px` temporarily, then re-positioning after `updatePairDetail()` resolves using `pop.offsetHeight` — the real rendered height.

## v7.13.7 (2026-04-06) — Gold spot source XAUUSD=X; FX chart symbols → ICEUS

### Engine — scripts/fetch_intraday_quotes.py (site repo)
- **Gold yfinance ticker:** `GC=F` (Gold futures, CME) → `XAUUSD=X` (Gold spot). Eliminates the futures basis premium (~$5–15/oz) that caused a small but visible price discrepancy between the quote bar and the TradingView spot chart.

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js
- **FX chart symbols → ICEUS:** All three symbol-builder functions (`fxSymbol`, `carryTV`, and the Crosses builder) now generate `ICEUS:EURUSD` etc. instead of `FX_IDC:EURUSD`. `pairMetaFromSym` strip regex updated to also accept `ICEUS:` prefix for pair metadata lookups.

### Frontend — index.html
- **All `data-sym` FX attributes updated:** 43 occurrences of `FX_IDC:` in `data-sym` replaced with `ICEUS:`. Default fallback symbol also updated to `ICEUS:EURUSD`.

---

## v7.13.6 (2026-04-06) — COT TV symbols: _FO_NCP_L confirmed; Gold chart → FOREXCOM:GOLD

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js
- **COT TV symbols corrected to `_FO_NCP_L`:** Format confirmed by user as working in TV embedded widget. `COT:{code}_FO_NCP_L` = NonCommercial Positions Long, Futures+Options Combined. All 7 currencies updated.
- **Gold chart symbol:** `TVC:GOLD` → `FOREXCOM:GOLD` in cross-asset config. Price tracks spot XAU/USD, closely matching the yfinance `GC=F` futures source used in quotes.json (minor futures premium difference expected but negligible for display purposes).

### Frontend — index.html
- **Gold `data-sym` updated:** All three gold click targets (quote bar, TV tab, cross-asset cell) changed from `FX_IDC:XAUUSD` to `FOREXCOM:GOLD` to match the updated chart symbol.

## v7.13.5 (2026-04-06) — COT TV symbols: confirmed suffix _FO_LMP_L (Leveraged Funds Positions Long)

### Frontend — assets/dashboard.js
- **TV symbol suffix confirmed and corrected:** Set to `COT:{code}_FO_LMP_L` — verified via TradingView Symbol Search showing `232741_FO_LMP_L = AUSTRALIAN DOLLAR - CME - Futures and Options - Leveraged Funds Positions Long`. Prefix remains `COT:` (Legacy report in TV hosts the LMP series). `_FO_` = Futures+Options Combined, `_LMP_` = Leveraged Funds Positions, `_L` = Long.
- **Previous attempts:** `_F_L` (non-existent), `COT3:..._FO_TLF_L` (non-existent) — both caused "symbol doesn't exist" in TV.

### Guides — guide-cot.html
- **TradingView Chart section:** Updated example symbol to `COT:099741_FO_LMP_L` with correct suffix breakdown.
